title: Bevy 0.19: shadow rendering ~6x more expensive at 4096/3-cascade (60->10fps); +96-step vol fog stacks to 7fps
description: Live GUI smoke on branch bevy-0.19 (Bastok Mines, char Bevyprobe, local LSB): steady-state ~7fps after all asset churn settled (churn img+0 mesh+0 zone+0 skin+0). perf_hud breakdown: CPU baseline 8.4ms (fine) and every named CPU render span sub-ms (early prepass ~0.4ms, main_opaque_pass_3d ~0.2ms, clustering ~0.1ms), yet rprep ~80ms + rgraph ~60ms ~= rtotal ~140ms. rprep includes swapchain acquire, rgraph is encode+submit+present; the ~0ms remainder rules out bevy_framepace sleep capping frames. => render thread is GPU-present-bound, not our CPU code. Consistent with upstream Apple-Silicon 0.19 regression bevy#24448 (flagged in migration plan risk register). Rendering itself is CORRECT (zone textured, skinned actor posed) — this is perf only. Definitive A/B vs 0.18 needs main to build (currently red: FfxiLightingUniform time_params, fixed only on branch). Evidence log: /tmp/bevyprobe-gui.log. Relates to pre-existing kuluu-xf8 (periodic ~70ms GPU spikes) which this makes constant.
notes:
ROOT-CAUSE LOCALIZED via feature-toggle matrix on 0.19 branch (Bastok Mines, Bevyprobe). Earlier 60->7fps A/B was CONFOUNDED: vol-fog commit 32d6710 is branch-only (absent on main), so I'd compared 0.18-no-fog vs 0.19-with-fog. De-confounded matrix (all same zone/char/camera, exclusive GPU):
  0.19 minimal (fog off, shadow 512, bloom 0):        60fps, rtotal ~5-8ms
  0.19 shadow 4096 only (fog off, bloom 0):           ~10fps, rtotal ~95ms
  0.19 fog off (shadow 4096 + bloom 0.12):            ~10fps, rtotal ~95ms
  0.19 full (fog 96-step ON + shadow 4096 + bloom):   ~7fps, rtotal ~140ms
  0.18 full config (shadow 4096, fog code ABSENT):    60fps, rtotal ~10ms
CONCLUSIONS: (1) NOT a flat upstream 0.19 GPU wall — 0.19 minimal = 60fps identical to 0.18. bevy#24448 likely NOT the cause. (2) SHADOWS are the dominant regression: shadow_map_size 4096 / 3 cascades / 200u ran 60fps on 0.18 but ~10fps on 0.19 => Bevy 0.19 made 4096 shadow rendering ~6x more expensive (~50ms/frame). Shrinking to 512 restores 60fps. Prime suspects: 0.19 shadow-cascade/PCF changes, or our migration's shadow rewiring (shadows_enabled->shadow_maps_enabled, fetch_directional_shadow frag_coord_xy signature). Relates to kuluu-xf8 (shadow cascades already suspected). (3) The 96-step volumetric_fog (branch-only feature 32d6710, actively being tuned in weather.rs) adds ~45ms/frame on top (10->7fps) — separate, feature-cost, not a 0.19 bug; consider lowering fog_step_count. Logs: /tmp/bevyprobe-{minimal,shadow,nofog,gui}.log + /tmp/bevyprobe-018.log.

title: c2s datagram seq-window violation silently deafened sessions (Horizon MH dead interactions, fresh-char c2s ignored)
description: Two independent counters (sub_seq/bundle_seq) violated the LSB dispatch window (vendor/server/src/map/map_networking.cpp:419-428): a subpacket is dispatched only when client_packet_id < sync <= datagram header, so after the second multi-subpacket bundle every c2s was silently skipped while the session looked healthy. 7dac9be's SendResRdy keepalive piggyback triggered it on every zone-in — on HorizonXI all MH interactions (Moogle talk, /logout, /mhexit, door exit) went dead. Fix: header = last subpacket sync (session.rs::datagram_header_id), single counter, guard test modeling the server window. Companion fix: zone-in cutscenes ride 0x00A LOGIN (EventPara@0x60, presence = PosHead.server_status==4) and must be answered with 0x05B End or the char sticks InEvent (0x05E/0x0E7 rejected) and the CS re-fires every relog — decode::ZoneInEvent + begin_server_event routing. Verified live on local LSB: fresh char completes intro CS 503 + MH expo CS 30000, enters MH via zmr0, Moogle spawns + Mog Menu opens (0x02E), zmrq exit works, logout in MH instant (0x00B state=1), relog clean.
close_reason: Fixed in 1e015c4; live-verified on local LSB (fresh char full MH loop)

title: WASD movement latches floating at stair lips / navmesh seams (Jeuno) — MAX_GROUND_STEP_DOWN clamp regression
description: The kuluu-nvqx Bastok-canal fix added a per-tick MAX_GROUND_STEP_DOWN=4.0 clamp in view_native/input.rs re-ground (line 933). It rejects any single-tick descent >4 yalms and falls back to the stale predicted Z. Because basis_pos is the client prediction (input.rs:706) and the client's own Move sets the server Z too (no >5y divergence to force a resync), a rejected drop latches: the player floats above the surface and cannot descend/cross. Symptom: random difficulty crossing stairs and zone-mesh edges in Jeuno (recording 2026-07-21 10.02). Fix: ground on navmesh CONNECTIVITY (move_along_surface end-poly get_poly_height) instead of a blunt per-tick delta + floor-jumping nearest_height_at; this keeps the canal unreachable (disconnected) while allowing steep connected descents.
notes: IMPLEMENTED unified MZB grounding per user directive: input.rs MoveEnvParams now takes MzbCollisionGeometry (not NavmeshState); movement grounds height via collision.ground_nearest(bevy xz, ref=-basis.z); navmesh fully removed from movement (kept for /pathto + minimap culling). AGENTS.md updated (/update-config). Wall-collision follow-up = kuluu-q5sn (server-authoritative interim). Compiles clippy-clean; release binary built 11:20. Offline: navmesh CONFIRMED flat at user coords (4.58,36.63)/(2.07,-5.78)/(−37.45,−53.01); MZB has a floor at the user's position (~0.0 matching reported z=-0.09) so grounding source is present, but a straight-line offline walk couldn't cleanly trace the specific rising staircase (unknown stair direction). Needs LIVE test at those coords to confirm climbing works.

title: main red: glam 0.33.2 bump duplicates glam vs bevy_math 0.19 (glam 0.32.1) — 18 Mat4 unification errors in ffxi-viewer-core
description: Commit 7c0c96f 'build(deps): bump glam from 0.32.1 to 0.33.2' bumps the workspace's direct glam dep (used by ffxi-actor, ffxi-nav) to 0.33.2, but Bevy 0.19's bevy_math pins glam ^0.32 (0.32.1), so the lock now carries BOTH versions and ffxi_actor::Mat4 no longer unifies with bevy::bevy_math::Mat4: cargo check -p ffxi-viewer-core --lib fails with 18 E0277/E0308 errors (e.g. skinned_ffxi_material.rs:102 set_from(&[Mat4])). Same duplicate-crate dedup gotcha as the gpu-allocator/windows 0.54-vs-0.58 issue that broke v0.2.0. Fix: revert 7c0c96f (glam must track bevy_math's pin — 0.32.x while on Bevy 0.19); consider ignoring glam in dependabot config or deriving the dep via bevy_math to prevent recurrence.

title: Panic: bevy_light check_dir_light_mesh_visibility index OOB when shadow cascade count increases at runtime
description: Crash: 'index out of bounds: the len is 2 but the index is 2' at bevy_light-0.19.0/src/lib.rs:477:57 in check_dir_light_mesh_visibility (Compute Task Pool). Root cause: the system's Local<Parallel<Vec<Vec<Entity>>>> thread-local queues are resized to the view's cascade count only in for_each_init (only on threads that receive a batch), but the aggregation loop iterates ALL thread-local queues including stale ones sized for a previous smaller cascade count. Increasing ShadowCascadeCount (graphics menu cycle 2->3, or preset change) triggers it racily. Fix: vendor bevy_light 0.19.0 (precedent: vendor/bevy_pbr) and guard aggregation with .get_mut(view_dest_index); also make apply_cascade_config_system write only on change to stop per-frame CascadeShadowConfig change-detection churn.
notes: Fix implemented: vendored bevy_light 0.19.0 at vendor/bevy_light with a .get_mut(view_dest_index) guard in the check_dir_light_mesh_visibility aggregation loop (grep KULUU PATCH), wired via [patch.crates-io]. cargo check -p bevy_light passes; full workspace check blocked by pre-existing unresolved merge conflicts (UU: dat_mmb.rs, dat_mzb.rs, zone-render-headless.rs from 'Merge Phase A' water work) — unrelated to this fix. Correction to description: apply_cascade_config_system is already gated run_if(resource_changed::<GraphicsSettings>), so no per-frame churn; no settings.rs change needed. Upstream bevy main still has the unguarded index — PR candidate. Drop vendor copy when upstream lands a fix.
close_reason: Verified via examples/zz-cascade-panic-repro.rs: stock bevy_light panics <2s, patched passes 4/4 runs

title: MH zone-in stranded player on the interior model's roof (stale previous-zone collision during async DAT swap)
description: Entering a Mog House from the city (or any zone transition) kept the OLD zone's MzbCollisionGeometry active until the new DAT's async load landed. snap_entities_to_mzb_floor_system grounded the player against city geometry at the MH-origin column, inflating transform Y; once the MH collision replaced it, ground_nearest resolved that stuck Y to the MH roof plane (DAT 391: floor y=0.01, roof y=5.0) — and nearest-floor is a fixed point, so the player stayed on the roof. Fix: auto_load_zone_geometry_system clears MzbCollisionGeometry the moment the effective zone DAT id changes (tagged via new source_file_id); the zero-tri snap branch pins self to the wire Y until the new collision lands. Regression test mh_collision_grounding pins DAT 391 spawn-column geometry (floor near 0 picked from ref 0; stale high ref sticks to roof band). Verified in the GUI on local LSB: login-into-MH and city→zmr0 re-entry both land on the interior floor next to the Moogle.
close_reason: Fixed; GUI-verified on local LSB both entry paths

title: Event menu option value: client sends raw UI row index as 0x5B EndPara, not script-computed option (Signet never applies)
description:
Symptom: talking to a Conquest gate guard/overseer, navigating the menu to 'Receive your nation's Signet', completes the event but no SIGNET status icon appears.

Evidence (server received & processed EVENT_END, but applied nothing):
- Packet log shows 0x052 (GP_SERV_COMMAND_EVENTUCOFF) + 0x0df (HP update) after the menu select — exactly the tail of vendor/server/src/map/packets/c2s/0x05b_eventend.cpp:71-72. So OnEventFinish ran.
- No 0x063 status-icon packet follows -> addStatusEffect(SIGNET) never called.
- LSB grants signet only when option==1: vendor/server/scripts/globals/conquest.lua:1352-1356 (overseerOnEventFinish). Server derives option from 0x5B EndPara (0x05b_eventend.cpp:38).

Root cause: the client sends EndPara = the raw 0-based UI cursor row index.
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:2439 confirm_dialog_choice(cursor.cursor) -> AgentCommand::EndEventChoice{choice}
- ffxi-client/src/session.rs:1307 / 1318 build_subpacket_event_end(.., choice) puts choice into EndPara (build_subpacket_event_end at session.rs:3039, buf[8..12]).
But the conquest option codes are script-COMPUTED, non-contiguous (signet=1, supply=65541..65565, exp rings=32933..32935) and depend on getArg1 (conquest.lua:651) sent in the 0x034 event packet. A row index can never equal these.

Two faithful gaps:
1. The 0x034 EVENTNUM params (8x i32 nums = startEvent args incl. getArg1) are decoded (session.rs decode_event_0x034, DialogState.nums) but NOT passed into the event VM. EventVm::start (ffxi-event/src/vm.rs) zero-inits work arrays, so the script computes its menu/option from zeros.
2. OP_SENDTAG (0x43) — the opcode that conveys the script-computed option to the server via FUNC_SendPendingTag — is stubbed as a no-op in ffxi-event/src/vm.rs:181-185. The session instead synthesizes a single EVENT_END with EndPara=raw index.

Faithful fix (design): inject 0x032/0x034 event params into the VM's work/reference region (per research/XiEvents getworkofs routing); implement OP_SENDTAG to emit the EVENT_END(0x5B) with the VM-computed EndPara/Mode; stop overriding EndPara with the UI row index. NOTE FUNC_SendPendingTag's EndPara computation is not fully reversed in research/XiEvents — needs empirical trace of a real conquest event (install present; zone 241 Harara overseer event 32759, see ffxi-event runner.rs test). Must not regress simple dialogs (talk/yes-no) which currently work because raw index coincides with the expected option.
notes:
FIXED & verified against authoritative spec + real game DAT.

Root cause refined: the 0x05B EndPara is NOT the menu row index. Per research/XiPackets/world/client/0x005B (reversed retail client): Mode 0 non-cancel -> EndPara = Work_Zone[1]; cancel -> 0x40000000; Mode 1 -> Work_Zone[1]. The event script reads the selection from Work_Zone[0] and COMPUTES the option code into Work_Zone[1]. The client was sending the raw cursor row index instead.

Empirical proof (Harara, zone 241 Windurst Woods, event 32759; install present): first menu (msg 9050) = 'What do you want? / Would you cast Signet on me? / I'd like to set my home point here. / Nothing.' Driving the VM:
  pick 0 (Signet)   -> Work_Zone[0]=0 Work_Zone[1]=1   (== conquest.lua overseerOnEventFinish 'if option == 1')
  pick 1 (homepoint)-> Work_Zone[0]=1 Work_Zone[1]=4
  pick 2 (Nothing)  -> Work_Zone[0]=2 Work_Zone[1]=0
The VM already computes Work_Zone[1] correctly with NO param injection and NO SENDTAG impl needed (those items from the original plan turned out unnecessary for this flow).

Fix:
- ffxi-event/src/vm.rs: add EventVm::work_zone(i) accessor.
- ffxi-event/src/runner.rs: DialogStep::Ended now carries end_para = Work_Zone[1] (0x40000000 on cancel).
- ffxi-client/src/event_dialog.rs: Advance::Ended { end_para } propagates it.
- ffxi-client/src/session.rs: both VM-end branches send build_subpacket_event_end(.., end_para) instead of the raw choice.
- Regression test ffxi-event runner::tests::harara_signet_pick_returns_option_1 pins EndPara==1 (self-skips without install). Passes.

Legacy (non-VM) dialog path still sends the raw choice as a best-effort fallback; conquest uses the VM path. Awaiting user live-server confirmation.
close_reason: Event menu now returns Work_Zone[1] as 0x05B EndPara (Signet pick -> option 1), per XiPackets client 0x005B; verified by real-DAT regression test. Implemented in vm.rs/runner.rs/event_dialog.rs/session.rs.

title: /exit (and window close) intermittently hard-locks on macOS, requiring force-quit
description:
On macOS, issuing /exit sometimes leaves the process wedged (beachball) so the user must Force Quit; shell shows '[1] terminated' only after the kill.

Repro log:
  reqlogout send (0x0E7) kind=LogoutOn
  disconnect-watcher: returning AppPhase to Launcher kind=Forced   (~1 frame / 15ms later)
  [1] terminated   (user force-quit)

Hypothesis (grounded):
/exit -> SlashOutcome::QuitWithLogout(LogoutOn) at slash_commands.rs:599-603. In apply_slash_outcome (text_input.rs:944-956) it fires THREE things in one frame: cmd_tx.send(ReqLogout), cmd_tx.send(Disconnect), exit.write_default() (AppExit).

The Disconnect crosses to the tokio session, which breaks its loop and emits Disconnected{reason:'agent requested disconnect'} (session.rs:1319-1321). That reason does NOT start with 'server logout state=', so classify_disconnect_reason returns Forced (mod.rs:336-341), and return_to_launcher_on_disconnect (runs in_state(InGame), mod.rs:448-473) sets NextState(AppPhase::Launcher) — racing the already-requested AppExit.

When both land in the same teardown window, Bevy runs a full InGame->Launcher transition (OnExit(InGame) despawn_ingame_entities + drain_* at mod.rs:198-206, then OnEnter(Launcher) launcher_backdrop/launcher_ui spawn NEW GPU/Metal resources) concurrently with winit's exit on the macOS main thread. Issuing Metal work against a surface mid-shutdown is a classic intermittent main-thread deadlock -> 'sometimes'.

Ruled-down secondaries: no spawn_blocking in client/dat/audio/nav (tokio runtime drop should be fast); session task & folder_task JoinHandles are discarded at mod.rs:544-545 so cleanup relies entirely on Disconnect delivery; bevy_audio(rodio/cpal/CoreAudio) teardown possible but Bevy-managed; Bevy AsyncComputeTaskPool DAT read on a slept FFXI_DAT_PATH could stall task-pool shutdown.

Immediate step (this bead): add an exit watchdog + teardown checkpoints to positively confirm WHICH path stalls (winit/wgpu vs tokio runtime drop vs lingering thread). Watchdog arms on AppExit, and if the process has not finished teardown within N seconds, logs the last checkpoint and SIGABRTs to capture an all-thread crash report (~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports). Likely fix (follow-up): /exit and /quit should request AppExit WITHOUT routing through the Disconnect->Launcher path (or gate the disconnect-watcher when an exit is pending).
acceptance_criteria: Watchdog converts the hard-lock into a logged abort with a captured backtrace identifying the stuck teardown stage; root cause confirmed; minimal fix removes the AppExit-vs-Launcher race.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED via watchdog + crash report (~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/ffxi-client-2026-06-22-215120.ips): main thread wedged in winit internal_exit -> World::clear_resources -> <RenderAppChannels as Drop>::drop -> event_listener wait -> pthread_cond_wait. It is a Bevy pipelined-rendering shutdown deadlock (render-thread handoff never completes), NOT the tokio runtime drop and NOT (causally) the disconnect->Launcher transition (that watcher fires on EVERY /exit since /exit always sends Disconnect, so it was correlation, not cause). last_stage stayed 'AppExit observed' (app.run() never returned).

FIX APPLIED (user chose): disable bevy::render::pipelined_rendering::PipelinedRenderingPlugin in DefaultPlugins (ffxi-client/src/view_native/mod.rs). Removes the RenderAppChannels resource entirely so clear_resources has nothing to block on; preserves graceful shutdown. Tradeoff: render no longer overlaps simulation (small always-on frame-time cost; relevant to kuluu-p09). Builds clean; fmt+clippy clean. Watchdog (exit_watchdog.rs) kept as permanent backstop (should now never fire).

STATUS: fix landed, NOT yet runtime-verified. NEXT: run release build, /exit ~10x to confirm no hang and no watchdog abort.

title: Cast interrupt must cancel the looping cast animation pose
description: Interrupting a spell (movement/paralyze/silence) clears the cast bar + self_casting, but the self actor's looping cast pose (action_routine kind 8, ActionPlayback looping, remaining=CAST_TIMEOUT_FRAMES 60s) is only cleared by a later ActionStarted event. An interrupt is a battle-message, not an ActionStarted, so the caster keeps playing the cast loop for 60s. Fix render-side: tag the spell cast-loop ActionPlayback as cast_pose and cancel it on the self actor in tick_live_ffxi_actors when self_casting is None/interrupted.
close_reason: Render-side cast_pose tag + cancel on self_casting None/interrupted in tick_live_ffxi_actors

title: Home Point crystal body strobes to fully transparent (continuous track-less particle fade)
description: The Home Point crystal renders as a generic NPC actor whose visible geometry is built from embedded particle generators. The crystal BODY is a 'continuous' generator (GEN_FLAG_CONTINUOUS) that holds exactly one particle, re-emitted each time it expires. In particle_sim.rs rebuild_mesh, a generator with no alpha keyframe track defaults its per-frame alpha to 1.0-progress (particle_sim.rs:479), so each re-emit cycle fades the single body particle to nothing -> the whole crystal strobes to fully transparent. The aura shell carries a real alpha track and is unaffected. Fix: a continuous track-less generator holds its init opacity (init_color[3]) instead of fading; transient sprays keep the 1.0-progress fade.

title: Cloud canopy occludes zone geometry: CLOUD_MIN_RIM=4000 is 1500u inside sky dome
description: weat/<type> cld1/cld2 canopy (zone_clouds.rs) is AlphaMode::Blend and camera-following. Its rim is pushed only to CLOUD_MIN_RIM=4000, but the comment (and the depth-occlusion invariant it relies on) require it just inside SKYBOX_RADIUS=5500. Any terrain past 4000 (Jeuno, wide fields) is farther than the canopy, so the translucent canopy draws over it -> dome-shaped foggy band overlaying zone mesh. Fix: derive the min rim from SKYBOX_RADIUS so the const can't drift from its own comment.
close_reason: Derived CLOUD_MIN_RIM from SKYBOX_RADIUS (5400, was 4000); canopy now depth-occluded by all in-dome terrain instead of draping over it

title: Minimap/map pane edge-flash: translucent FRAME_BG shimmers over moving world through transparent map margins
description:
Repro: while moving, the minimap pane's edges flash/shimmer. Root cause: MinimapRoot BackgroundColor is theme::FRAME_BG = srgba(0.05,0.07,0.16,0.88) (translucent navy) at ffxi-viewer-core/src/minimap/mod.rs:319. The retail/top-down map texture has transparent margins; update_minimap_crop_rect (mod.rs:451) pans the crop under the centered player so those transparent regions reach the pane edges, where the 0.88-alpha navy composites over the LIVE moving 3D scene -> per-frame shimmer.

Fix: give the map an OPAQUE backing (named const e.g. MINIMAP_BACKING, alpha 1.0 dark navy) behind the MinimapImage so transparent map pixels read as a static fill, not the moving world. Better than fully removing the background (which would show the raw moving world through the map's transparent corners). Apply the SAME opaque backing to the new full-screen Map screen (hud/map_screen.rs, kuluu-n3gw) so both are consistent. Verify via /verify that the edge flash is gone while walking.

Reported by user via screen recording 2026-07-21; diagnosed from code (video not machine-readable).
close_reason: Implemented, committed (c0e115e/5e579eb/9c0af00), statically gated (compile+clippy+tests), LSB protocol review PASS, and live GUI-verified 2026-07-21 (single-pane two-page Commands menu; Map screen with populated Wide Scan + markers legend; opaque edge-flash backing).

title: Client-side horizontal wall collision from MZB (replace removed navmesh wall-slide)
description: kuluu-oe8y removed the LSB navmesh from client movement (it flattened stairs); player Y now grounds on MZB collision. The navmesh slide_along also provided horizontal wall-sliding, now gone — walls are server-authoritative in the interim. Add faithful client-side wall collision from MZB wall triangles (normal.y < FLOOR_NORMAL_MIN): a horizontal raycast/sweep against the MZB collision geometry so the player slides along walls instead of clipping through. MzbCollisionGeometry currently only does vertical column raycasts (ground_raycast/for_each_hit_in_column); needs a horizontal query.

title: Widescan protocol (0x0F4-0x0F6): consts, decode, session model, wire boundary
description:
Faithful client Widescan (Tracking) driven by the server list. Add packet consts to ffxi-proto/src/map.rs: c2s TRACKING_LIST=0x0F4 (SendFlg=1), TRACKING_START=0x0F5 (ActIndex), TRACKING_END=0x0F6; s2c TRACKING_LIST=0x0F4, TRACKING_POS=0x0F5, TRACKING_STATE=0x0F6 (cite vendor/server/.../0x0f4..0x0f6*). Decoders in ffxi-proto/src/decode.rs: WidescanEntry{act_index,level,kind(0char/1npc/2mob),rel_x,rel_z,name}, WidescanPos{act_index,x,y,z,lost}, WidescanState{list_start/list_end} (+roundtrip tests).

Session model in ffxi-client/src/state.rs: SessionState.widescan{entries,tracked,building}. Inbound arms session.rs:573+: 0x0F6 ListStart->clear+building; 0x0F4->push while building; 0x0F6 ListEnd->building=false; 0x0F5->set/clear tracked. Outbound AgentCommand::{WidescanRequest,WidescanTrack(act_index),WidescanEnd} via build_subpacket_tracking_* (session.rs), dispatched reactor.rs:482. Cross ffxi-viewer-wire: add widescan list + tracked to SceneSnapshot (wire_translate.rs::state_to_snapshot).

Server ref: zone_entities.cpp:1578 (floor-aware, range by job charutils.cpp:7507, ALL_JOBS_WIDESCAN map.lua:115). Run protocol-conformance-reviewer + lsb-invariant-prober on decoders. Independent of UI.
close_reason: Implemented, committed (c0e115e/5e579eb/9c0af00), statically gated (compile+clippy+tests), LSB protocol review PASS, and live GUI-verified 2026-07-21 (single-pane two-page Commands menu; Map screen with populated Wide Scan + markers legend; opaque edge-flash backing).

title: Two-pane generic menu refactor (foundation)
description:
Refactor the one-pane in-game menu into retail's two-pane layout, modeled on the existing MenuStack (no parallel state machine). Left pane = parent level, right pane = current/top level; at Root the right pane previews the highlighted category's child rows. Add active_pane flag (Pane{Left,Right}, default Right) on MenuStack + toggle_pane(); nav acts on the active pane's MenuLevel. '-' (Minus) toggles active_pane while InputMode::Menu (menu context, read KeyCode directly in handle_menu_key). Confirm on a left-pane row re-drills and moves focus right; Cancel from right moves focus left before pop.

Files: input_mode.rs (Pane, MenuStack.active_pane, toggle_pane; push/pop reset to Right); hud/menu.rs (split spawn_main_menu into MainMenuLeft/MainMenuRight pools MainMenuRow{pane,slot}; rework update_main_menu to fill both, highlight only active pane, inactive cursor dimmed no caret; keep+extend suppression arm menu.rs:868); text_input.rs::handle_menu_key (intercept Minus, route nav to active pane); menu_help_bar.rs (active-pane hint + '- : switch pane' affordance).

Tests: two-pane focus toggle + Root preview. Blocks the rest of the epic.
notes:
DESIGN CORRECTION (user feedback + HorizonXI screenshots 2026-07-21): the two-pane refactor as built (permanent side-by-side left=Root + right=live-preview) is WRONG and not retail. Retail's Commands menu is a SINGLE top-right pane with TWO PAGES. '-' flips between page 1 and page 2 (Left/Right arrows also flip); Esc closes; Enter drills. Page1 (our subset of retail): Status, Equipment, Magic, Items, Abilities, Party, Search, Map. Page2: Key Items, Macros, Config, Current Time, Communication, Graphics, Debug, Shut Down, Log Out.

Rework plan (minimal-churn): keep Pane/active_pane/toggle_pane (the MAP screen legitimately uses them for map-vs-widescan focus). Convert only the generic Commands menu: (1) reorder ROOT_ENTRIES to page1(8)+page2, add ROOT_PAGE_1_LEN + root_page_bounds(cursor) [page derived from cursor, no new state]; (2) spawn_main_menu single column; pane_view returns current TOP level (Root windowed to current page); resolve_viewport(Root)=(page_end,page_start); (3) drop the preview (root_child_kind stays for drill only); (4) handle_menu_key: '-' at Root jumps cursor between pages (keep '-'=toggle_pane for Map), Up/Down wrap within current page, Left/Right flip page; (5) menu_help_bar hint -> page flip; (6) rewrite the two_pane_focus / root_right_pane_previews tests as page-flip tests. Files: menu.rs, text_input.rs handle_menu_key + mouse_nav_dispatch, menu_help_bar.rs.
close_reason: Implemented + live GUI-verified 2026-07-21 (commit 9c0af00 single-pane two-page Commands menu; c0e115e Map screen).

Add retail FFXI's Map experience by reusing the existing minimap render layer (ffxi-viewer-core/src/minimap/) and refactoring the one-pane in-game menu into retail's two-pane layout. Spec: /Users/jon/.claude/plans/fancy-soaring-bubble.md.

Locked decisions: Widescan uses the faithful server 0x0F4 tracking list (names enriched from local entity table as display fallback since LSB sends empty sName); full generic two-pane menu refactor + Map screen on top; pane-toggle key is '-' (Minus), context-sensitive (opens menu from World, toggles active pane while a menu is open); in scope = Map screen + Widescan + markers legend/filters; deferred = Switch Map floor selection + zone-line exit markers.

Grounding evidence in spec; key seams: input_mode.rs:3/98/148 (MenuStack), hud/menu.rs:410/842/868, text_input.rs:3260/2372/2447, minimap/mod.rs:167/110, minimap/overlay.rs:45/247, ffxi-proto/src/{map,decode}.rs, state.rs:146/1831, session.rs:573+, reactor.rs:482.

title: Textured top-down minimap bake (lit + re-bake on light change)
description:
Replace the grey synthetic-collision-mesh top-down minimap bake with a render of the real textured zone geometry (MMB meshes via FfxiZoneMaterial), viewed top-down from the ceiling-clamped ortho bake camera.

Current: ffxi-viewer-core/src/minimap/topdown.rs bakes a synthetic unlit mesh rebuilt from MzbCollisionGeometry. Camera sits at ceiling_y = max_y - top_cull_yalms looking down; near plane clips the roof.

Target: point the same offscreen ortho bake camera at the real textured static zone meshes instead.
- Tag static zone meshes (dat_mmb.rs MMB geometry) onto MINIMAP_BAKE_LAYER (RenderLayers::from_layers(&[0, BAKE_LAYER])) so the bake camera sees walls/floors but NOT actors/sky/weather/particles/water.
- Keep the ceiling-clamp camera placement so the near plane still slices the roof.
- Lighting: LIT bake (FfxiZoneMaterial vertex lighting + point lights + time-of-day). Lights must be on the bake render layer. Re-bake when zone lighting/time-of-day changes materially so the map stays current (do not leave a night-baked map frozen).
- One-shot per (zone, lighting-state); dispose bake mesh path since synthetic mesh no longer used.

Faithfulness: Enhanced fallback only (retail uses hand-drawn static map art = Retail backend). Applies where no retail map art exists (Mog House interiors etc.).
close_reason: Implemented + runtime-verified: top-down minimap bake renders real textured zone geometry (dat_mmb static meshes tagged onto MINIMAP_BAKE_LAYER; synthetic collision mesh removed) lit by the ZoneGlobalLighting global uniform, with a quantized-lighting-bucket re-bake trigger. Verified live in San d'Oria (GUI, verilight/Verilamp): top-down mode shows textured green grass/grey stone/building outlines (not flat grey), and the night bake was dimmer than the earlier daytime bake after !addtime. Evidence: artifacts/verify/minimap-{before,after-timeshift,currentstate}.png

title: Re-orient camera to player heading when toggling first-person mode
description: When switching to/from first-person view, chase.yaw is left at its previous camera-relative value, so the view can face a random direction. Set chase.yaw = yaw_for_heading(self_pos.heading) at the moment camera_transition.begin() runs so both FP-entry (look where player faces) and FP-exit (camera behind player) start aligned to the character heading.
close_reason: Set chase.yaw = yaw_for_heading(self_pos.heading) at camera_transition.begin() in input.rs; both FP-entry and FP-exit now start aligned to player heading. Builds clean.

title: Chase camera clips through Mog House door (non-collision zone geometry ignored)
description: In the Mog House the chase camera passes through the closed exit-door panel and escapes the room into the void. Root cause (confirmed via camera_collision debug probe): the door panel is visible MZB geometry flagged doesnt-block-LOS, so it lands in the non-collision set and is excluded from the camera's zone BVH (which is built only from block-LOS/collision triangles). Per-cast probe in SdO MH (zone 0x00E6): rays toward the door return bvh_hit=false AND brute_hit=false while rays toward real walls/ceiling hit. Fix: include the non-collision (camera_only) MZB geometry in the camera-collision BVH (ZoneCollisionBvh) while keeping grounding/nav on collision-only geometry. Matches XIM PolarCamera (Collider.getObjectDistance casts against all visible geometry, skipping only hitWall material tris).
close_reason: Fixed in 23c56cf: MH chase camera now collides with furniture/door MMB placements. Verified live via camera_collision probe (door-direction raycast false->true, total_tris 5210->10859). Guard test mog_house_camera_always_collides_with_mmb passes.

title: ffxi-mcp/agent-socket: focus-less GUI driving (simulated movement input + debug_heights readback)
description: Live GUI verification of input-driven bugs (e.g. kuluu-nvqx fall-through) is blocked: the agent socket only carries session-level AgentCommands (Move/PathTo route through the reactor's own nav + movement, NOT the GUI input.rs re-ground where movement bugs live), and /debug heights is GUI-only (typed slash cmd). Live driving falls back to osascript keystrokes which lose focus and don't register key-hold. Add: (1) a socket/MCP command to inject simulated movement INPUT (forward/strafe for N ticks) that flows through dispatch_movement_system; (2) a socket/MCP command to trigger /debug heights and return the server/nav/mzb grounding numbers as an event. Bridge socket<->Bevy via shared SessionState fields. Then update /verify skill for focus-less driving.
notes:
DONE + verified live. Implemented: AgentCommand::DebugDrive{forward,strafe,duration_ms} + DebugHeights, intercepted in agent_codec (not forwarded to session) into a shared Arc<Mutex<DebugControl>> (ffxi-client/src/debug_control.rs); GUI holds it as DebugControlHandle resource. input.rs dispatch_movement_system overrides forward/strafe from an unexpired DebugDrive (walks the REAL input path: heading/wall-slide/re-ground). debug_heights.rs: trigger system fires DebugHeightsRequest on seq bump + logs server/nav/mzb heights under tracing target 'debug_heights' (readable focus-less). ffxi-mcp tools: walk(forward,strafe,duration_ms) + debug_heights. Verify skill drive-gui.md updated.

VERIFIED live (char 'hello', zone 235): socket debug_drive walked the char through input.rs; debug_heights logged grounding; reproduced kuluu-nvqx (char ended IN the canal water — screenshot artifacts/verify/kuluu-0pof-fell-in-water.png). fmt clean; clippy running.
close_reason: Built + verified live + committed (be8d8f9). debug_drive/debug_heights socket+MCP commands bridge to the GUI input path via Arc<Mutex<DebugControl>>; reproduced kuluu-nvqx focus-less. /verify drive-gui.md updated.

title: Buff ribbon focus shows buff name in top menu help bar (retail-faithful)

title: Item UI recast display + disabled-item state don't match retail
description: Two gaps vs retail item menu/detail (user screenshots): (1) FORMAT: our item detail shows 'Recast: 0:00/(1092:15)'; retail shows the enchantment/recast as '<uses/max HH:MM:SS/[max_recast, reuse]>' (e.g. '<1/1 22:55:57/[24:00:00, 0:30]>') at the bottom of the item tooltip. Match retail's recast/enchantment line format. (2) DISABLED STATE: retail grays out / marks items unusable due to cooldown or zone state in the item list; ours does not visually distinguish them. Add disabled/greyed rendering for items not currently usable. Files: ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/item_detail.rs, item_screen.rs, item_ui.rs.
notes:
IMPLEMENTED (commit 73d2d38). Vertical slice across ffxi-proto/ffxi-dat/ffxi-viewer-wire/ffxi-client/ffxi-viewer-core:
- ffxi-proto decode.rs: ItemAttr::charge_info() decodes 24-byte extdata (header 0x01 gate, charges@1, ready bit 0x40 @flags-hi[3], next-use vana ts @4..8) — offsets audited vs LSB 0x020_item_attr.cpp + timer_info.h by protocol-conformance-reviewer (all correct). 3 guard tests.
- ffxi-dat item_dat.rs: recast_base widened u16->u32 (dropped the .min(u16::MAX) clamp that saturated a 24h/86400s enchant reuse at 18h).
- wire InventoryItem + client ItemSlot: +charges_remaining:Option<u8>, +next_use_vana_ts:Option<u32> (#[serde(default)]); populated on ITEM_ATTR path, None on ITEM_LIST. wire PROTOCOL_VERSION 4->5.
- item_detail.rs: retail line <cur/max HH:MM:SS/[HH:MM:SS]> via format_charge_line + hhmmss; 5 tests incl the exact <1/1 22:55:57/[24:00:00]> case.
- item_meta.rs: compose_item_detail populates charges+live recast countdown; item_unusable() predicate (empty OR recast-running); find_slot() resolves the exact (container,index) instance. 5 tests.
- item_screen.rs: greys non-cursor unusable rows theme::MUTED, keyed per-instance.

REVIEW: adversarial workflow (conformance + correctness, 5 findings verified). Fixed: per-instance resolution (was item_no find-first -> wrong instance for dup charged items; now (container,index)), wire version bump, misleading decode comment. All unit tests green (proto 3, item_detail 5, item_meta 5), clippy clean.

DEFERRED: (1) retail's 2nd bracket use/cast time (0:30) -> kuluu-qxbf (DAT activation field not parsed). (2) clock-skew: countdown uses wall-clock now_vana_ts (no server GameTime offset plumbed) — acceptable per design, low sev. (3) job/level/zone/status unusable gating is server-side (luautils onItemCheck), deliberately NOT reproduced to avoid false-greying.

PENDING: live GUI confirmation with an enchanted/charged item in inventory (tooltip countdown + greying) — GUI menu nav not agent-drivable; needs human eyes.

Match retail paging on Left/Right. Likely in ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/ item list/grid navigation + input_mode key handling.

If retail's Items menu ALSO felt non-paging to the user, note the main Items menu already paged pre-fix — the gap was UsableItems/other lists; may need to confirm which menu the user tested.

title: Bastok Markets (zone 0xEB): character falls through floor into water between two positions
description: Walking between two specific positions in Bastok Markets (zone id 0xEB=235) causes the player to fall through the floor and appear in the water. Likely a ground-height / collision raycast gap that falls back to water_height or Y=0. Reported with screen recording.
notes:
LIVE REPRO via the new focus-less tooling (kuluu-0pof). Drove char forward from point A; it fell into the canal water (visually confirmed, screenshot). /debug heights readings:
- At A (walkway): server_z=1.80, mzb_floor_bevy=-2.0, nav_bevy=-1.8 (all agree, on walkway).
- At the drop (x=-200.6, y=-128 / y=-126): server_z fell 1.8 -> -3.3 -> -3.9; mzb_floor_bevy=-8.0 AND nav_bevy=-7.8 — BOTH drop to the canal/water floor (~6 yalms below the walkway).
So mzb (visual) and nav AGREE at the fall spot too — the floor genuinely drops off to water level here; the char walks off the walkway edge and is grounded onto the low canal floor. Still TBD: whether the drop is a smooth descent or a cliff/hole (trace debug_heights at closely-spaced points from y=-157 to y=-126 along x~-200.6), which decides the fix (edge/rail collision vs accept). Tooling now makes this a quick trace.

title: Retail-faithful delivery box: dedicated screen + numeric spinner + functional gil

Add wire::DeliveryBoxState projection (slots/stat, recipient+status, queued). New reusable hud/spinner.rs backing item quantity AND gil (gil-send verified functional on LSB: gil is inventory item 65535 slot 0, Set validator doesn't restrict ItemWorkNo). Reuse item_grid::spawn_item_cell, item_ui::focus_detail, item_screen::viewport_start. Grey EX/RARE/non-deliverable via item_flags::deliverable. Per-region cursor memory; default Slot(0) focus even when empty. Cutover from local_menu::open_delivery_box. Plan: ~/.claude/plans/nested-munching-sketch.md

Both crates built clean at each commit (last full green: both crates exit 0). Functional gil verified vs LSB Set validator + live DB. Not this feature's code. Run scripts/checks.sh + live verify once that tree is green.

description: The contextual action-ring 'Items' list (MenuKind::UsableItems) renders through the generic top-right text panel while the main-menu Items (MenuKind::Items) uses the rich item_screen panel (icons + detail + tabs + sort). Route UsableItems through item_screen too (icons + detail, no bag tabs, no sort box), keeping direct UseItem on click. Fold out duplicated item-label formatting, action item_no extraction, and cursor-prefix literals.
notes: Implemented in ffxi-viewer-core: UsableItems now routes through item_screen (icons+detail, no bag tabs, no sort box) via ItemScreenMode{Inventory,Usable}; header shows plain 'Items' in Usable mode; generic panel suppressed for UsableItems (menu.rs). DRY: DynamicMenuAction::item_no(), menu::item_qty_label(), style::cursor_prefix(). Unit tests added + all 25 hud::menu + 24 hud::item tests pass; viewer-core clippy clean. Runtime verify BLOCKED by pre-existing/unrelated client breakage (delivery_box field on SceneSnapshot not wired into ffxi-client wire_translate.rs/state.rs — inherited WIP, not this change).

title: Retail-faithful sky/lighting/atmosphere: fix zone-geom distance fog, sun disc visibility, warm ToD tone
description: Comparison vs retail (Sarutabaruta, dawn) shows our rendering diverges: (1) zone terrain/water ignores DistanceFog (zone_ffxi.wgsl never imports/samples bevy_pbr::fog) -> distant horizon falls to dark ClearColor 'black void' instead of retail's hazy fade; (2) no visible sun disc/bloom at times the sun is up; (3) cooler/greener overall tone + flat sky vs retail warm golden-hour gradient. Infra exists (skybox.rs gradient, sun_moon.rs disc+lens flare, weather.rs DAT fog/ambient, atmosphere.rs) but the play-path result is wrong. Fix the confirmed fog gap first, then diagnose sun-disc + tone at runtime via verify/retail-observe loop.
notes:
RESOLVED 2026-07-20 (both main gaps):
(1) DISTANCE FOG — zone_ffxi.wgsl + skinned_ffxi.wgsl now apply_distance_fog() under #ifdef DISTANCE_FOG (consume the DistanceFog weather.rs already sets). Deterministic proof artifacts/verify/fog-fix-BEFORE/AFTER-saruta-dawn.png: distant terrain fades to warm horizon vs black void.
(2) SUN GLOW / retail sky — ROOT CAUSE: default sky_style was Enhanced (all 4 presets), which DISABLES the retail painterly lens flare (lens_flare_system gates on !sky_embellishments); Enhanced's sun is a flat glowless disc (bloom 0.08 ~no-op). Vanilla style = retail-faithful painterly sun (bright core + soft halo + horizontal streak + ghosts). Fix: flipped all presets sky_style Enhanced->Vanilla (settings.rs for_preset Low/Med/High/Ultra), aligning with AGENTS.md vanilla-parity-default. Updated 5 settings tests; 35 pass. A/B artifacts/verify/sun-ENHANCED-default-flat-h7.png vs sun-VANILLA-retail-faithful-h7.png vs sun-DEFAULT-now-vanilla-retail-h7.png.
SKY GRADITENT: confirmed FINE (skybox.wgsl 8-color DAT gradient) — user's flat green sky was overcast weather + low sun, not a gradient bug; at clear dawn it renders navy zenith->warm amber horizon.
HARNESS: examples/zone-render-headless.rs gained --sky/--vanilla-sky/--enhanced-sky flags reusing the REAL SkyboxPlugin+MoonMaterialPlugin+LensFlarePlugin+spawn_sun_and_moon+sun_moon_system (no reimplementation) — deterministic daytime sky/sun tuning at fixed --hour.
CAVEAT: our LSB dev weather != retail HorizonXI weather (independent); matched structure not exact pixels. NOTE: working tree had many unrelated pre-existing uncommitted files (not mine).
close_reason: Closed

Focused window shows a border affordance. Chat log: Enter expands to full-screen, Esc contracts. Buff ribbon: arrow-select a buff, Enter sends 0x0F1 buffcancel (gated to cancelable buffs via scraped NO_CANCEL flag). Enhancement: hover tooltips for buff names.

title: F (SelectActiveWindow) cycles chat pane focus in default/world state
description: Retail's compact1 'F' / 'Select active window' key cycles the focused on-screen window. In our client the analog is the chat panel's tab set (Chat/Battle/System). Currently SelectActiveWindow is only handled inside the Items menu; in InputMode::World it is a no-op. Wire F to cycle ActiveChatTab in the default state, matching retail's window-change key.
close_reason: Implemented: F cycles focused chat pane (Chat->Battle->System) in InputMode::World via ChatKind::cycle_next; verified live (Battle->System->Chat observed). Retail's compact1 F toggles the single Log Window's focus; our tabbed chat panel maps it to a pane cycle.

title: Launcher/char-select UI click leaks into world targeting, opens no-target action menu on zone-in
description:
click_to_target_system (picking.rs) ran unconditionally in every AppPhase, gated only on InputMode::World (the startup default). During Launcher/character-select, clicking a UI button resolves to hit_id=None with current_target=None -> ClickResolution::OpenContextMenu, setting InputMode::TargetAction with an empty (no-target) context. That state persisted into InGame; once the HUD spawned on zone-in the leaked Magic/Abilities/Items target-action menu became visible.

Fix: added WorldPickingEnabled(bool) resource in ffxi-viewer-core::picking (default true for the launcher-less wasm viewer). click_to_target_system early-returns + drains the click queue when false. Native client (view_native/mod.rs) overrides default to false and toggles true on OnEnter(AppPhase::InGame) / false on OnExit(AppPhase::InGame), stopping the leak at its source.
close_reason: Fixed: WorldPickingEnabled gate blocks world-click targeting outside AppPhase::InGame

title: Top menu bar retail parity: wrap/overflow, Help placement, net-panel z/anchor, status ribbon overlap, framed title segment
description: Screenshots show the menu help bar diverging from retail and misbehaving responsively: (1) help hint wraps to two lines and spills out of the 26px bar at narrow widths; (2) 'Help' label uses a hard-coded 150px right margin so it floats mid-bar instead of hugging the network panel; (3) bar (WINDOW_Z=20) draws over the network panel (z=10) so the bar border slices through it, and the panel floats at top:4/right:4 instead of sharing the bar's top edge; (4) status ribbon at top:8 sits under the bar so chip timers overlap the title; (5) retail renders the menu title in its own framed segment at far left. Fix in menu_help_bar.rs / network_status.rs / status_ribbon.rs.

title: Solo A/D in chase mode should turn in place, not translate (retail parity)
description: Retail (HorizonXI video 2026-07-20): holding A/D with no W/S pivots the character on the same tile with zero translation; camera trails behind via auto-recenter. Fix: pivot_steer feeds resolved.steer into advance_heading_turn (same path as first-person arrow-turn) at HEADING_TURN_RATE when forward==0; steer_in_chase now requires forward!=0 so steer only becomes a camera-relative run direction combined with W/S. moving=false while pivoting, so no run animation.
close_reason: Fixed in input.rs: pivot_steer + steer_in_chase requires forward; verified cargo check + suite (agent_session failure pre-existing)

title: Debug rendering leaks into normal play: navmesh overlay visible at spawn, [dbg] lines spam the chat log by default
description: Retail parity tour 2026-07-19 (artifacts/verify/parity-tour/00-initial.png shows a navmesh overlay blob in a Mog House; every capture shows [dbg] skybox/music lines in chat). Retail shows neither. Gate the navmesh overlay and [dbg]-tagged chat sink behind explicit dev toggles, off by default on the play path, still reachable via slash command //devhud etc.
close_reason: Runtime-verified: spawn-clean.png (no overlay, no [dbg]), devhud-on.png (reveal works), no-navmesh-hotkey.png (N dead). Committed.

title: Self player never plays directional locomotion anims (mvl/mvr/mvb): backpedal moonwalks with the forward run clip
description: ffxi-viewer-core/src/ffxi_actor_render.rs:2055-2059 hardcodes forward_vel/strafe_vel to (0.0,0.0) unless engaged && !is_self, so the self character always plays run?/wlk? while moving — S-key backpedal (BACKPEDAL_SCALE 0.5, input.rs:721) slides backward under a forward-run animation (observed live 2026-07-19, artifacts/verify/mvcam-06-s-hold-b.png). Selection logic already supports the directional clips (ffxi-actor/src/actor_state.rs:185-198 movement_direction thresholds at 161-183); it's only the self input wiring that zeroes them. Retail shows directional gait when moving backward/sideways (locked-on strafe/backpedal). Fix: feed real forward/strafe velocity for self; retest backpedal + locked-on strafe visuals.
notes: Do NOT fix naively by feeding real forward/strafe velocities unconditionally: retail plays directional clips (mvl/mvr/mvb) ONLY when locked on — unlocked movement is always velocity-parallel-to-facing (tank controls), so unlocked run/walk is the only correct clip. Under Kuluu's current camera-relative steer (kuluu-ltns), W+A has a heading-lerp lag window (HEADING_LERP_RATE 5.0/s, input.rs:697-698) where velocity and facing disagree; unconditional velocity feed would flicker mvl/mvr there — a NEW divergence retail cannot express. Correct shape: resolve kuluu-ltns first (movement model), then gate directional anims on lock-on; unlocked stays run?/wlk?. Also the blend-lag foot-slide itself (diagonal translation under forward-run clip) is an artifact only the tank-control model eliminates.
close_reason: SelfMoveIntent now carries forward/strafe character-frame components; ffxi_actor_render feeds them for self (was hardcoded 0,0). Directional clips (mvl/mvr/mvb) can only trigger while locked on because the tank model makes unlocked velocity always facing-parallel (S about-faces instead of backpedaling) — matching retail's lock-on-only directional gait. Moonwalk gone by construction; visual confirmation of mvl/mvr/mvb clips during locked strafe still worth a human-eyes pass.

title: Keyboard movement model diverges from retail: camera-relative W + steer-blend A/D vs retail character-relative tank controls; S backpedals instead of turn-and-run
description:
Retail observation (HorizonXI, char Oldman, Mog House + plaza, artifacts/retail/20260719-14*.png session): keyboard movement is CHARACTER-relative. 'A'/left-arrow rotate the character in place (tank turn, camera trails behind); W+A carves a curved arc; S turns the character 180 and runs toward the camera (no unlocked backpedal); after orbiting the camera with numpad4, W continues along the character's OWN facing (camera swings back), not camera-forward.

Kuluu today (ffxi-client/src/view_native/input.rs): W snaps heading to camera yaw (input.rs:659-662, heading_for_yaw(chase.yaw)) — camera-relative; A/D do a camera-relative steer-blend (turn_in_chase, input.rs:668-708, HEADING_LERP_RATE 5.0/s); S backpedals facing away at BACKPEDAL_SCALE 0.5 (input.rs:50,721). Fast auto-recenter (camera_polish_system input.rs:979-1027, rate 3.0) masks the difference in casual play — live drive 2026-07-19 (artifacts/verify/mvcam-08-*.png) observationally matched retail only because the camera had already recentered before W was pressed.

Decide: vanilla-faithful tank-control mode (at least as the default or an option) vs keeping the modern scheme as an enhanced-flagged alternative. Retail evidence: first driver log in session artifacts; remake evidence artifacts/verify/mvcam-*.png.
close_reason: Implemented retail tank controls in dispatch_movement_system (input.rs): unlocked A/D rotate the character in place at TURN_KEY_RATE_RAD_PER_SEC 5.0 (fast, per user feedback — Q/E keep the slow HEADING_TURN_RATE 0.86; exact retail A/D rate still unmeasured, retest in a future retail session); W moves along the character's own heading (camera-relative snap removed); unlocked S = about-face (+128) + full-speed forward run, no unlocked backpedal; locked-on A/D strafe and S backpedals with heading pinned to target; steer-blend (HEADING_LERP/CHASE_TRACK) removed; autorun now steerable (turn keys no longer cancel it). FP keeps view=facing (rigid yaw coupling). Live-verified with probes+screenshots (artifacts/verify/tankmove-*), unit tests resolve_tank_inputs in input.rs.

title: Intermittent slab_allocator use-after-free corrupts renderer: all zone geometry renders pure black after first zone-in until relaunch
description: Found during kuluu-042y verification (2026-07-19): bevy_render::slab_allocator logged 'Use-after-free: attempted to copy element data for an unallocated key' repeatedly right after first outdoor zone-in; from then on ALL zone geometry (walls/floor/ceiling/lamps) rendered pure black in every dynamic-lights mode while skinned character models stayed lit. Clean relaunch of the identical character rendered normally. Intermittent Bevy renderer corruption independent of the lights feature but fully masks it when it occurs. Suspect mesh-allocator interaction with zone mesh despawn/respawn on zone-in; possibly related to bevy 0.18 slab allocator + our zone rebuild path.
notes: LIVE VERIFY 2026-07-21 (GUI, Verilamp): fixed build, login -> zone 231 (San d'Oria) -> !zone 234 (Bastok Mines), ~90s runtime. 7 auto-run particle generators spawned and ran (nak0/nak1/sil0/sil1/bnd0/wa00/wa01 — each meshes.add(empty_mesh()) + per-frame rebuild, the exact zero-vertex path). Result: 0 'unallocated key'/Use-after-free lines, 0 slab_allocator lines all session (grep on artifacts/verify/particle-empty-mesh-run.log). Evidence recorded .verify/latest.json. REMAINING: the all-black zone-geometry symptom was NOT visually re-checked this run (macOS console was locked -> screencapture/System Events blind, gotcha drive-gui.md:110). Reopen a GUI session on an unlocked console to confirm zone geometry stays lit across repeated zone-ins; if black-out ever recurs with 0 slab errors, it is a separate bug from this (now-fixed) empty-mesh spam.

title: Launcher backdrop: animated retail-style flythrough camera (currently static)
description: Retail char-select/title shows a slow continuous camera flythrough of a zone (observed on HorizonXI 2026-07-19: high vantage -> ravine -> waterfall canyon over ~37s, no cut, UI fixed overlay; artifacts/retail/20260719-1419*.png). Our launcher backdrop camera is bolted at a fixed transform (launcher_backdrop.rs:115). Add a terrain-following dolly using ZoneCollisionBvh raycasts (down-probe altitude, forward wall probe, ~45s segment fade-cuts to new vantages), and run build_zone_collision_bvh_system during AppPhase::Launcher.
close_reason: Flythrough implemented in launcher_backdrop.rs (terrain-following dolly via ZoneCollisionBvh raycasts, 45s segment fade-cuts, deterministic vantages); BVH build debounced (collision_bvh.rs) to stop per-chunk rebuild freezes; verified via GUI capture burst (artifacts/verify/flythrough3-*.png)

title: Launcher login UI responsive fixes: account chips, wrapping rows, panel width clamp
description: Login screen broke at narrow window widths: fixed 560px panel overflowed the viewport, saved-accounts row ran off the right edge, and the action-button row squished button text into bad line breaks. Fixed DRY in launcher_ui/common.rs: panel_layout gets max_width 100% + screen_root edge padding (clamps every launcher panel), row() wraps, breadcrumb wraps/clamps, new chip_group() visually attaches account+delete buttons, and the graphics wheel-scroll system generalized to a shared ScrollRegion used by the new wrapping/height-capped saved-accounts region. '+ New' became a compact '+' chip. Added FFXI_WINDOW_SIZE env knob for scripted responsive verification. Verified via GUI screenshots at 1400x900/700x900/420x850.
close_reason: Implemented and GUI-verified at three window sizes; gates pass

title: Dynamic-lighting settings incompatible with retail: heuristic emitters double-light Generator zones; knobs scale the faithful path
description: Two point-light systems run at once: zone_point_lights.rs (faithful DAT Generator lights) and zone_lights.rs (heuristic overbright-vertex emitters tuned by LightThreshold/Intensity/Range). The knobs also scale the faithful path (light_reach_scale on ZONE_LIGHT_REACH_SCALE, zone_point_lights.rs:133,379), so retail-faithful lighting can be silently distorted, and Generator zones get double-lit. Fix: DynamicLights becomes Off|Vanilla|Enhanced (serde aliases Few/Many->Vanilla). Vanilla (default): Generator lights only, DAT-driven consts, no user scaling. Enhanced: adds heuristic emitters; Advanced threshold/intensity/range knobs apply to emitters only. Flicker knob stays global (default on = retail). Presets pin Vanilla. Update GRAPHICS_ENTRIES mirror + /lights command.
notes: Implemented + reviewed (2026-07-19): DynamicLights Off/Vanilla/Enhanced with serde aliases; faithful path de-scaled (reach = DAT range x ZONE_LIGHT_REACH_SCALE); emitters Enhanced-only; presets pin Vanilla; GRAPHICS_ENTRIES mirror + /lights updated. 73 targeted tests pass; code review clean (no findings >= threshold; sub-threshold note: WGSL alpha-discard 69/255 literal is a cross-module contract with SKINNED_ALPHA_DISCARD, unguarded because WGSL cannot import Rust consts). Runtime evidence so far: /lights prints mode labels (artifacts/verify/lights-status.png); Vanilla lamp glow vs Off flat (lights-vanilla.png / lights-off.png). Enhanced capture re-running.
close_reason: Runtime-verified 2026-07-19: crystal-aura-{1,2,3}.png (animated blue additive aura on translucent faceted crystal, HomePoint#2, matches retail r020.png); wall-shadow-fixed-{1,2}.png (wall base now casts coherent ground shadow vs wall-no-shadow-22.png reference); lights-status/vanilla/off/enhanced.png (/lights mode labels + per-mode lamp glow). Full checks.sh fmt+clippy+test green; commits 716230c, ad74fde, 3b673ab.

title: Home Point crystal (and glowing lamp fixtures) render as dark slabs: skinned-actor path drops mesh render state; model-embedded particle generators never auto-run
description: Retail (2026-07-19 capture): Home Point crystal is a glowing translucent blue crystal with an animated electric aura; our client renders a dark flat slab (Upper Jeuno HomePoint#2). Same symptom family: Upper Jeuno hanging crystal lamp fixtures show no glow. Diagnosis (file:line): (1) The crystal is a skinned NPC actor (npc_list.sql:292 look modelid 51 -> DAT 1351 via look_resolver.rs:14-25). ffxi-dat DOES parse per-mesh RenderProperties (display_type_flag, t_factor - skel_mesh.rs:10-24,433-455) but build_actor_children discards them (ffxi_actor_render.rs:924-953) and FfxiSkinnedMaterial hardcodes AlphaMode::Mask(0.271) (skinned_ffxi_material.rs:285-286); skinned_ffxi.wgsl has no additive/translucent/emissive branch. XIM builds a per-mesh RenderState with additive blend + depth-write-off for blended meshes (SkeletonMeshSection.kt:35,210-217,364-384; GLDrawer.kt:198-201) - our zone/MMB path honors this, the skinned path does not. (2) The electric aura: particle_sim.rs supports Additive/Blend but is fed only by ActionStarted (scheduler_runtime.rs:340-384); XIM runs startAutoRunParticles() at model-ready for embedded autoRun generators (Actor.kt:127,724-734; default blend Src_One_Add, ParticleInitializers.kt:1008-1022). Fix surface: thread RenderProperties into build_actor_children + FfxiSkinnedMaterial (non-hardcoded alpha_mode, t_factor tint, emissive) and add an actor-ready auto-run pass feeding embedded generators into particle_sim. Also verify which path the Jeuno lamp fixtures take (skinned vs MMB) - if MMB, their blend flags are a separate un-set on that build path.
notes: Impl complete (auto-run particle system registration was the missing piece — was defined but never registered; also t_factor tint threading + D3m particle-sprite double-draw filter; XIM oracle correction: skinned meshes always alpha-test, glow comes from the additive particle stream). 479+226 tests pass, review clean. Runtime visual PASS still unproven: 2026-07-19 drive reached Upper Jeuno but teleports to HomePoint#1/2/3 coords showed architecture without the crystal streamed in, path_to stalled on navmesh, then the console locked (screencapture black). Next attempt: walk in from spawn, target the HomePoint entity by id 17776728, capture 3 frames for particle animation delta.
close_reason: Runtime-verified 2026-07-19: crystal-aura-{1,2,3}.png (animated blue additive aura on translucent faceted crystal, HomePoint#2, matches retail r020.png); wall-shadow-fixed-{1,2}.png (wall base now casts coherent ground shadow vs wall-no-shadow-22.png reference); lights-status/vanilla/off/enhanced.png (/lights mode labels + per-mode lamp glow). Full checks.sh fmt+clippy+test green; commits 716230c, ad74fde, 3b673ab.

title: Honor DAT indoors flag: static indoor diffuse, no sun/cascade shadows, MH weather load, lamps lit + flicker
description: Retail reference (2026-07-19 recordings): MH + indoor zones are point-light dominant, warm, no sun through walls, lamps flicker. Root causes found: (1) load_zone_weather early-returns when snapshot.myroom.is_some(), so Mog House DAT 356 weather records (indoors=true, black sun diffuse, dark warm ambient) never load -> synthetic full-daylight sun + cascade shadows inside MH. (2) WeatherRecord.indoors is parsed (ffxi-dat weather.rs:78) and copied into ZoneDirectionalLighting (sun_moon.rs:672) but never consumed: sun/moon arc + cascade shadows stay on indoors. XIM EnvironmentSection.kt:139-149: indoors uses a single static DiffuseLight (direction from signed moonLightColor bytes /128, negated; color = sun diffuse) + zone ambient, no sun/moon. (3) zone point lights are gated by lamp_night_factor even indoors (zone_point_lights.rs:85,317) so indoor lamps are dark by day; and no flicker exists (retail lamps/braziers visibly flicker).
notes: Implemented: (1) ffxi-dat weather.rs parses indoor_light_dir_entity/landscape from signed moonLightColor bytes (XIM EnvironmentSection.kt:251-256) + indoor_light_direction() helper + tests. (2) load_zone_weather no longer skips myroom -> MH DAT 356 records (indoors=1, black sun, warm dark ambient) now load. (3) sun_moon_system: indoors -> single static diffuse on the sun DirectionalLight (entity block color, indoor dir, shadow_maps_enabled=false), moon off, ZoneDirectionalLighting publishes static indoor dirs for zone+actor materials ungated by sun/moon altitude. (4) zone_point_lights: lamp_lit_factor (indoors OR black daytime sun diffuse -> lamps always lit; Upper Jeuno retail capture 08:14) + lamp_flicker two-sine (gated by GraphicsSettings.light_flicker). (5) weather_fx apply_weather_to_sun_system no longer compounds sun.illuminance *= mul across frames (sun_moon's write-cache made the in-place multiply decay the sun exponentially under any weather with mul != 1) - tracks pre-multiplied base in a Local.
close_reason: Implemented + runtime-verified: MH now loads DAT 356 weather (indoors=1) -> warm dark interior, no sun/cascade shadows (artifacts/verify/moghouse.png); lamp flicker numeric 76->98->77 across 0.5s frames; Upper Jeuno dark with no sun patches (jeuno-day.png); lamps-lit gate covers indoors + black-daytime-sun zones; weather sun-multiplier compounding fixed

title: Headless CharSelection::Id sends empty charname in 0x07 view select — LSB rejects ('mismatched character name'), agent_session test cannot pass
description: ffxi-client/src/session.rs:183 lobby.handshake(&auth_session, *cid, "", 0, key3) sends an empty charname for the CharSelection::Id path. Current LSB validates SELECT accid FROM chars WHERE charid=? AND charname=? (vendor/server/src/login/view_session.cpp:72) and closes the socket, logging 'tried to select a character id with a mismatched character name'. Only ffxi-mcp hits this path (it prefers FFXI_CHAR_ID over FFXI_CHAR, ffxi-mcp/src/main.rs:1029, and enters session::run with initial_state None); play/GUI do a named pre-select and dodge it. Consequence: tests/agent_session.rs 'session never reached InZone within 60s' — deterministic against the current vendor pin. Fix: resolve and send the real charname on the Id path (lobby list is available pre-handshake).

title: EphemeralChar fixture leaks trigger-inserted rows (char_history/char_equip/char_pet) — map-server save race re-arms 1062 duplicate charid
description: Root-caused 2026-07-18: xidb's char_insert BEFORE INSERT trigger on chars populates char_equip/char_exp/char_history/char_inventory/char_jobs/char_pet/char_points/char_profile/char_storage/char_unlocks, but the fixture's CHAR_TABLES cleanup list (ffxi-client/tests/common/mod.rs:15) omits char_history/char_equip/char_pet. Worse, LSB's map server REPLACE INTO char_history on save/disconnect (vendor/server/src/map/utils/charutils.cpp:7727) fires ~60s AFTER the client drops, re-creating rows for the just-deleted charid — so any live-test suite run re-arms an orphan that makes the NEXT provisioning INSERT fail 1062 (Duplicate entry for PRIMARY, reported against chars though chars stays clean). Fix: add the trigger tables to cleanup AND do a pre-create orphan sweep (delete char_* rows whose charid has no chars row) since post-cleanup deletion races the map server. Also observed ~10 fossil it_* accounts with no chars from past provisioning failures.

title: FFXI client: top menu bar + item tooltip behavior during menuing (retail reference)
description:
Retail (HorizonXI) observations of the top help bar and item description tooltip while driving the quick-use menu flow, from live captures on char Oldman (2026-07-18):

TOP MENU BAR (help line, spans top of game window):
- Appears whenever a menu is open; anchored top-left, with a 'Help' label at the far right end of the bar.
- Context menu (Enter on self): bar shows the menu title 'Commands' plus a one-line hint for the highlighted entry — e.g. cursor on 'Items' -> 'Use an item.'
- Items submenu: bar becomes 'Items  20/58  Select an item.' — title, a counter, then the hint. The counter stayed 20/58 while scrolling the whole list (cursor moved 1->19), so it is inventory count/capacity (20 items in a 58-slot inventory), NOT cursor position.
- Hint text swaps instantly per highlighted entry; bar persists across submenu transitions.

ITEM TOOLTIP / DESCRIPTION WINDOW (bottom-left):
- Appears immediately when the cursor lands on a list entry; icon + item name + stat block.
- Enchanted/usable gear shows a live status line: <charges/max  current_recast/[reuse, activation]> e.g. Warp Cudgel '<26/30 0:00/[1:00:00, 0:30]>', Hatchling shield '<1/1 6:58:49/[24:00:00, 0:30]>' with 'Dispense: Eggs'.
- The current_recast field ticks down in REAL TIME while the tooltip is open (observed 6:58:49 -> 6:58:23 -> 6:57:50 -> 6:57:45 across ~1 min of captures).
- Food shows flavor text + effect summary (Crawler egg: 'HP+10 MP+10 / Duration: 5 minutes').

COOLDOWN / DISABLED BEHAVIOR:
- Items on reuse cooldown STILL appear in the quick-use Items list and can be highlighted.
- Pressing Enter on them is silently refused: no submenu, no chat error line, cursor stays put; only the tooltip timer keeps ticking. Client-side gate, no server round trip observed.

Evidence: /tmp/hxi-items.png /tmp/hxi-items2.png /tmp/hxi-items3.png /tmp/hxi-items4.png /tmp/hxi-hatchling.png /tmp/hxi-use2.png /tmp/hxi-use3.png
Kuluu parity: remake menu system must reproduce the bar (title/counter/hint), live-ticking tooltip recast line, and silent-refusal cooldown gating.
notes: Top menu bar implemented (hud/menu_help_bar.rs): full-width bar while any menu is open - title (menu_title), bag used/capacity counter for Items/UsableItems (retail: bag fill not cursor pos), per-entry help line (Use an item. / Select an item. retail-confirmed; other strings provisional pending captures), Help label at right. Verified live: artifacts/verify/menubar-root.png (Commands + Use an item.), menubar-items.png (Items 7/30 Select an item.). STILL OPEN on this bead: live-ticking tooltip recast line, silent cooldown refusal, exact retail help strings for remaining entries.

- On use (Enter): small blue shield icon appears above character's head immediately and persists through the 0:30 activation window; Items submenu auto-closes to bare 'Items' top bar.

Item use from the Items menu takes TWO Enters (select item -> sub-target flashes -> confirm). The first observation never actually consumed the charge (consistent with: no chat line, no stack change). Re-run in progress to complete the use and capture the real chat/dispense behavior.

title: Shadows/light leak through walls/buildings (cascaded shadow light bleeding)
description: Sunlight/shadow cascades shine through zone geometry (walls, buildings). Verify against retail via /retail-observe; fix so occlusion closely matches retail but more realistic. Suspects: zone meshes not shadow casters (NotShadowCaster), FfxiZoneMaterial shadow pass participation, depth/normal bias, cascade config.
notes: User capture 2026-07-19 (artifacts/verify/wall-no-shadow-{22,27,37}.png, Ronfaure-side gate, 8:29-8:35 Sunshine): the big zone WALL meshes cast NO shadow at all — sunlit grass runs right up to the wall base — while trees, arch props, and characters DO cast. Suspects: FfxiZoneMaterial shadow/prepass path not writing depth for zone meshes, backface culling in the shadow pass discarding single-sided wall faces as seen from the sun, or NotShadowCaster on the zone spawn path.
close_reason: Runtime-verified 2026-07-19: crystal-aura-{1,2,3}.png (animated blue additive aura on translucent faceted crystal, HomePoint#2, matches retail r020.png); wall-shadow-fixed-{1,2}.png (wall base now casts coherent ground shadow vs wall-no-shadow-22.png reference); lights-status/vanilla/off/enhanced.png (/lights mode labels + per-mode lamp glow). Full checks.sh fmt+clippy+test green; commits 716230c, ad74fde, 3b673ab.

title: Cut 0.19 render-graph CPU cost (rgraph ~16ms) to widen 60fps headroom
description: After the GPU-preprocessing-off + pipelining fixes, Bastok Mines holds a stable 60fps at low settings (shadows 1024/2-cascade, AA off, native res). But the render sub-app's rgraph is ~16ms of CPU command-encode/submit that is resolution- AND vsync-independent (measured: 0.5 vs 1.0 render scale identical; vsync on vs off identical; per-pass encode sums to only ~2ms). This is the forced-CPU-batching path (GPU preprocessing disabled to dodge the Apple-integrated-GPU regression) paying per-draw encode cost. At current low settings pipelining hides it enough for 60fps, but raising shadows/AA/cascades will re-cap below 60. Investigate: (1) draw-call/instance count in a city zone and whether mesh merging or instancing cuts encode cost; (2) whether the no_gpu_preprocessing BatchedInstanceBuffer per-frame write_buffer upload is a chunk of the 16ms; (3) whether an upstream 0.19.x fix makes the GPU preprocessing path viable on Apple (would restore indirect batching). Evidence: artifacts/verify/bevy019-60fps-parity.txt.

Add ffxi-proto generated item_info/item_usable tables from vendor SQL, usability predicate, MenuKind::UsableItems with direct UseItem dispatch, disable quick-menu entry when empty, and gate the full Items window's Use row on the same predicate.

title: Bevy 0.19: model-viewer startup panic — missing GraphicsSettings resource
description: model-viewer subcommand panicked at startup on 0.19: Res<GraphicsSettings> failed validation (Resource does not exist) in process_load_vos2_requests. Root cause: model_viewer/mod.rs cherry-picks viewer-core systems without ViewerCorePlugin, so GraphicsSettings (init'd only inside that plugin) was never inserted — latent since initial commit; 0.19 changed the default fallible-param behavior from skip-with-warning to panic, unmasking it. Fixed on branch bevy-0.19 (commit a19d618): app.init_resource::<GraphicsSettings>() in model-viewer setup. Default character path is FfxiFaithful so the system early-returns exactly as it silently did on 0.18. Verified: model-viewer launches clean (/tmp/modelviewer2.log).
close_reason: Fixed on branch bevy-0.19 commit a19d618; model-viewer verified launching clean
