title: Moogle Mog Menu (0x02E OPENMOGMENU) + job change (0x100 MYROOM_JOB main/support)
description:
Retail: triggering the resident Moogle (or a Nomad Moogle in towns) opens the Mog House menu, whose Job Change entry lets you set main + support job. Kuluu has zero job-change support of any kind — this is the single biggest missing vanilla mechanic in the Mog House cluster.

LSB (authoritative):
- Moogle onTrigger -> xi.moghouse.moogleTrigger: prints Mog Locker expiry, then player:sendMenu(xi.menuType.MOOGLE) — the ONLY action (vendor/server/scripts/globals/moghouse.lua:366-380; thin NPC wrapper e.g. scripts/zones/Southern_San_dOria/npcs/Moogle.lua:8-22).
- sendMenu(MOOGLE=1) pushes s2c 0x02E GP_SERV_COMMAND_OPENMOGMENU (src/map/lua/lua_baseentity.cpp:2570-2584; header-only packet src/map/packets/s2c/0x02e_openmogmenu.h). Everything the menu offers is then driven by dedicated c2s packets, NOT moogle events.
- Job change is c2s 0x100 GP_CLI_COMMAND_MYROOM_JOB {MainJobIndex, SupportJobIndex} (src/map/packets/c2s/0x100_myroom_job.h:27-31), validated to require being in the MH or a MISC_MOGMENU zone (0x100_myroom_job.cpp:48; MISC_MOGMENU=0x0020 src/map/zone.h:483).
- Nomad-moogle availability is advertised in 0x00A MogZoneFlag = CanUseMisc(MISC_MOGMENU) (src/map/packets/s2c/0x00a_login.cpp:212) — that byte is the "Mog Menu allowed here" flag, not an in-house flag.
- Moogle visibility is a per-player conditional NPC: name "Moogle", shown when inMogHouse && !on2F (src/map/zone_entities.cpp:1206-1210).

XIM (client-side reference): routes any NPC named Moogle to ChangeJobInteraction — opens the native job menus (menu jobchang / jobcselu) and plays the moogle's job0 animation routine (research/xim src/jsMain/kotlin/xim/poc/game/configuration/assetviewer/ChangeJobInteraction.kt:15-45).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-09): nothing. No 0x02E decode, no Mog Menu UI, no AgentCommand or 0x100 encode, no slash command (sweep found only unrelated 0x1000 equip-model and 0x110 fishing hits). The event VM handles generic NPC menus (session.rs:2045-2113; event_dialog.rs:92-142) but the Mog Menu is a dedicated packet-opened menu, not event bytecode, so it needs its own UI surface.

Scope: decode 0x02E and open a Mog Menu; Job Change flow (job list from char state, main+support selection, AgentCommand + 0x100 encode, state refresh on the server's follow-up packets); honor 0x00A MogZoneFlag for nomad moogles; storage/locker entries can defer to kuluu-6a0. Optional /jobchange slash command for the agent path.
notes: Research (2026-07-10): 0x02E is header-only (presence = open menu), pushed by sendMenu(MOOGLE=1) from moogleTrigger — no event bytecode, intercept in recv loop beside the 0x032 intercept. Job menu data source is s2c 0x01B JOB_INFO (undecoded before this work): unlocked u32@0x08 (bit 0 = subjob-feature flag, not a job), job_lev2 [u8;24]@0x44 (NOT legacy i8 @0x0C), mjob@0x04 sjob@0x07. c2s 0x100: buf[4]=main buf[5]=sub, 0=keep-current (no way to unset sub); validation failures are silent server drops — never update job state optimistically; refresh rides the follow-up burst 0x01B/0x037/0x061/0x062/0x119/0x0AC/0x063/0x067/0x0DF (equipment strip included). CliStatus 0x061 mjob/mjob_lv/sjob/sjob_lv at body 8/9/10/11 were previously skipped.
close_reason: Mog Menu (0x02E intercept) + job change (0x01B JOB_INFO decode, c2s 0x100 main/support, no optimistic update) landed; real Moogle NPC now spawns via SendResRdy (7dac9be)

title: Mog House entry: residence-door 0x05E (zmr/zms) trigger + 0x00A MYROOM decode + interior rendering
description:
Retail: walking into a Residence door sends the same 0x05E maprect used for zone lines, with a zmr*/zms* RectID fourcc. The server keeps you in the SAME town zone (no zone change), isolates you per-player, and the 0x00A zone-in tells the client to draw the room interior instead of the town.

LSB (authoritative):
- Entry detected by fourcc prefix: vendor/server/src/map/packets/c2s/0x05e_maprect.cpp:74-75 ("zmr" classic cities, "zms" WoTG/Adoulin); sets m_moghouseID=char id and destination=current zone (:234-242). MH is NOT a separate zone/instance — isolation is m_moghouseID equality (src/map/entities/charentity.h:540; src/map/zone_entities.cpp:172-175, 1022-1073); resident Moogle is a per-viewer conditional NPC (zone_entities.cpp:1162-1218).
- Client is told to render the room purely via 0x00A: LoginState=SAVE_LOGIN_STATE_MYROOM + MyroomMapNumber=GetMogHouseModelID (src/map/packets/s2c/0x00a_login.cpp:195-206, model table :35-72; struct fields 0x00a_login.h:115-127 incl. MyRoomExitBit :126 and MyroomSubMapNumber :122 for 2F).
- Zone-in lua forces pos (0,0,0,192), strips effects, may fire the 2F-unlock cutscene: scripts/globals/moghouse.lua:281-332.

XIM (client-side reference): entry is a walk-into zone line special-cased by DAT id — MogHouseConfig maps each city to entry ids + room-model file indices (research/xim src/jsMain/kotlin/xim/poc/tools/ZoneChanger.kt:18-28, matcher :182-184); room model picked by rentalIndex / 2F fileIndex (xim/resource/table/ZoneTables.kt:14-20); zone name "Mog House 1F/2F" (ZoneTables.kt:74-77); music forced to 126 (ZoneSettingsTable.kt:42-44); town NPCs suppressed (NpcTable.kt:197-200); zmr_/zms_ ids special-cased in DAT id parsing (DatResource.kt:243-261).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-09):
- No entry path: residence-door rects are absent from the scraped LSB zoneline table (ffxi-nav/src/zonelines.rs:16-26 — they live in zone DATs, not LSB SQL), so the reactor proximity trigger (ffxi-client/src/reactor.rs:651-695) can never fire for a Residence door; no UI/interaction targets one either.
- Exit direction already works: /mhexit -> AgentCommand::MogHouseExit -> 0x05E "zmrq" (session.rs:1759-1804, builder :3501-3521).
- in_mog_house is decoded only from party packets (moghouse_flg: ffxi-proto/src/decode.rs:952,984,1007) and drives only MH BGM slot 6 (ffxi-viewer-core/src/audio.rs:170,243-246) + a chat notice (session.rs:3432-3456). The 0x00A MYROOM fields (LoginState/MyroomMapNumber/MyRoomExitBit/MyroomSubMapNumber) are NOT decoded, and nothing ever loads an MH room model — zone geometry is always the town MZB.

Scope: parse residence entry rects from the zone DAT interaction section (zmr*/zms* ids) and send 0x05E on walk-in; decode the 0x00A MYROOM fields; load and render the per-city MH interior model (1F + 2F variants) instead of town geometry; zone-name banner; keep the existing BGM behavior.
notes: Research correction (2026-07-10 session): residence-door rects are NOT absent — all 21 zmr*/zms* lines are in vendor/server/sql/zonelines.sql as same-zone rows and already scraped into ffxi-nav ZONE_LINES; the reactor trigger already fires them. MH entry IS a full same-zone 0x00B/0x00A reconnect handshake. Real gaps: (1) 0x00A MYROOM decode — LoginState u32@0x7C, MyroomSubMapNumber u8@0xA4, MyroomMapNumber u16@0xA6 (MODEL id, 0x01FF sentinel), MyRoomExitBit u8@0xAA, MogZoneFlag u8@0xAB; (2) apply_zoneline_spawn_fallback would clobber the legitimate (0,0,0,192) MH spawn — must be suppressed when LoginState==MYROOM (both call sites); (3) model→DAT-file table (16 XIM-verified pairs, e.g. 256→356 Jeuno, 615-618→8380x 2F; Feretory 729 excluded/unverified); (4) renderer re-key on resolved file id, not zone_id (zone_id unchanged in MH; transient zone_id=None is watch-coalescible); (5) reactor zonelines must go inert inside MH (town OBBs vs near-origin coords misfire); (6) scraped trigger box is paper-thin (1.0x4.0) vs faithful DAT RID OBB (12x8x2) — RID chunk kind 0x36 parser layout verified on retail DATs.
close_reason: MH entry landed: 0x05E zmr/zms trigger, 0x00A MYROOM decode, interior DAT render via effective_zone_dat_file_id, XIM door offsets; live-test fixes in 7dac9be (navmesh off in MH, camera min distance, SendResRdy Moogle spawn)

title: Party menu + party command vocabulary (seek/find member/request/auto-group/languages/alarm/focus target/change leader)
description:
Retail Party menu commands: Seek Party (green ! flag), Find Member (browse seekers), Request (ask to join a party), View Request, Auto Group (red ! flag, auto-accepts invites), Languages preference, Alarm Types (invite chime); while in a party additional entries appear: Focus Target and Change Leader. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=1471 (24:31-25:35).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- Root menu lists "Party" (ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/menu.rs:14) but there is no MenuKind::Party (input_mode.rs:46-66); selecting it falls through to MenuDispatch::NotImplemented (ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:1975).
- AgentCommand (state.rs:1198-1332) has ZERO party commands — no invite/leave/kick/change-leader/seek/auto-group/find-member/request/language/alarm/focus-target. No c2s wire support either.
- Party membership s2c already decoded (0x0DD group list/attr, decode.rs:960-1021) so the display side exists; this bead is the command/menu side. Invite-accept flow needed for the client to be usable in groups — highest-value slice.

title: Status ribbon interaction: cursor into buff icons, per-buff detail, cancel on confirm
description:
Retail: pressing the window key twice focuses the buff bar; you scroll through icons, each shows detail text, and cancelable effects are removed by pressing confirm (canonical uses: Ninja dropping Copy Image shadows, Dancer canceling Saber Dance to heal). https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=239 (3:59-4:38).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- Status ribbon is display-only: ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/status_ribbon.rs renders up to 32 icons with countdowns (update_status_timers :276-301); no cursor, no cycle, no detail text, no selection.
- Data model has id+expiry only: state.rs:308-311 status_icons/status_icon_expiries from 0x063 (session.rs:901, decode_miscdata_status_icons session.rs:3017). No cancelable flag decoded.
- NO c2s cancel path exists. AgentCommand::Cancel (state.rs:1271) is the agent-goal cancel (reactor.rs:513-516), unrelated to buffs. Needs the LSB status-effect-cancel client packet (vendor/server: GP_CLI cancel-status handling; verify exact opcode against LSB before implementing) plus an AgentCommand + wire plumbing.

title: Chat input: persistent send-channel rotation + missing channels (Linkshell 2, Yell, Unity, Assist)
description:
Retail rotates the chat input's persistent send channel with one key (F on keyboard / Y on pad): say → tell → party → linkshell 1 → linkshell 2 → assist (E) → assist (J) → unity → shout. The chosen channel persists across messages until rotated again.

Source: "FFXI User Interface Guide Pt1 - Vanilla Interface" https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=159 (chat mode rotation, 2:39-3:10).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- Channel rotation exists ONLY inside the target-action context menu Chat entry: ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/action_model.rs:150-155 (modes = Say/Tell/Party/Linkshell/Unity/Shout), rotated via NavRight in ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:439-456.
- chat_buffer_for_mode wires only Tell//p//l//sh; Unity falls through to empty buffer (stub): text_input.rs:789-803.
- Plain chat open (Enter) is an empty buffer with NO persistent send channel: text_input.rs:329, view_native/input.rs:164.
- Slash commands cover only /s /p /sh /l /t: view_native/slash_commands.rs:476-505. No /yell, /l2, /unity, /assist chat commands exist.
- Proto constants already scraped: ffxi-proto/src/map.rs:83-100 (YELL=26, LINKSHELL2=27, NS_* assist channels).

title: Retail chat log: two independent windows (per-channel routing, H/V split, per-window lines/width, timestamps, window style)
description:
Retail vanilla FFXI has TWO independent chat log windows. Config > Windows chooses horizontal or vertical split, timestamps (off / 12h / military), and window style; per-window settings control max lines, width, and resize speed; Config > Log routes each message category to window 1 or window 2 (typical retail setup: chat/social channels left, battle log right).

Source: "FFXI User Interface Guide Pt1 - Vanilla Interface" (Sakura Destros) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ — chapters: The Chat Bar (t=84), Windows split (t=2351), window sizing (t=2403), Log routing (t=2447).

Retail behavior (video):
- Two windows, user-routable per channel category (window 1 vs window 2 checkbox lists at t=2447).
- Split horizontally or vertically; timestamps optional (military or 12h); window style selectable (t=2351).
- Per-window: line count (e.g. keep 5 visible lines so chat is never fully hidden), width, resize speed (t=2403).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/chat_panel.rs:58-63 — ONE chat window with 3 hardcoded tabs (Social/Battle/Debug), shown one at a time.
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/chat_panel.rs:125-136 — routing hardcoded via ChatKind::accepts, not user-configurable.
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/chat_panel.rs:11-16 — fixed VISIBLE_ROWS=12; height animates 60-220px via idle fade, not configurable.
- No timestamps anywhere (grep clean); no split; no window-style options.
- Wire channel vocabulary already exists: ffxi-viewer-wire/src/lib.rs:235-248 (Say/Shout/Tell/Party/Linkshell/Yell/System/Other/Battle/Debug).

title: Items window shows hardcoded bag capacity /80 (plumb real container capacity)
description: item_screen.rs header renders 'Items N/MAIN_BAG_CAPACITY' with MAIN_BAG_CAPACITY hardcoded to 80. Real per-container capacity is parsed server-side (state.rs InventoryBag.capacity, e.g. bag=80, mog-safe=30, mog=200) but wire_translate::project_inventory_main drops it, so SceneSnapshot has no capacity field. A char with an un-upgraded <80 bag shows the wrong denominator. Also a no-magic-numbers violation (80 is server-supplied). Fix: add inventory capacity to SceneSnapshot, populate in wire_translate, read in item_screen. Found in PR review of kuluu-e8x.
close_reason: Items window header now shows the real per-container capacity from 0x01C ITEM_MAX (e.g. 'Inventory 7/30', 'Mog Safe 0/50'); MAIN_BAG_CAPACITY=80 literal removed. GUI-verified live against LSB.

title: Actor model DAT loads run synchronously on the render main thread: 110-160ms frame freeze per newly-visible NPC/PC
description: Repro: drive the character around Port Bastok (agent socket patrol, run11 /tmp/ffxi-perf-run11.log 03:02:49-03:07:11Z). Every few seconds a frame spikes 110-160ms with cpu~130ms, model+1, churn img+~7 (~1.8MB) mesh+~40 — process_load_actor_requests (ffxi-viewer-core/src/ffxi_actor_render.rs:1475) calls load_npc/load_pc (full DAT read+parse+DXT decode) inline in an Update system. fps drops to 37-55 while moving. Fix: kick/poll AsyncComputeTaskPool pattern already used by dat_mzb.rs (kick_load_mzb_tasks/poll_load_mzb_tasks) and char_preview.rs:208. Secondary observations from same run (not this bead): constant skin+~1000-1600/s FfxiSkinnedMaterial churn bursts and std+~120/s StandardMaterial churn keep baseline elevated (see kuluu-zh5); one unexplained 116ms spike with zero churn/model at 03:03:43Z.
close_reason: Fixed & verified via agent-socket patrol A/B runs. (1) load_npc/load_pc moved to AsyncComputeTaskPool (kick_load_actor_tasks/poll_load_actor_tasks, mirrors dat_mzb pattern); (2) clip/scheduler parse moved into loader (derive_animation_sets, Arc-shared); (3) mesh build + mip gen + bind pose moved into task (prepare_actor_parts); (4) subject+quality-keyed LRU cache (48) of Arc<PreparedActor> so radius-flapping NPCs respawn cheap; (5) spawns capped at 2/frame. Drive-window comparison (240s Port Bastok patrol): sync run11 19 spikes >=100ms (max 290ms) fps min 37 -> run16 zero actor-attributable spikes >=50ms, median 5s-max frame 147ms->21.7ms, fps ~60. Residual non-actor classes tracked in kuluu-zh5.

title: Default xiloader version (2.0.0) rejected by current LSB testing image (needs 2.1.x); breaks agent_session test + provision
description: LSB landsandboat/server:testing now enforces xiloader >= 2.1.x; our default version string reports 2.0.0, so LOGIN_CREATE fails ('Your xiloader is too old') unless --xiloader-version 2.1.0 is passed. This makes agent_session_drives_mcp_end_to_end fail whenever the login server is reachable (it no longer self-skips into a pass), and breaks provision/create-char defaults. Fix: bump the default (or scrape the accepted version from vendor/server at build time per the vendor-scrape convention) and keep the CLI override.
notes: Second env failure mode found 2026-07-12 (behind the xiloader gate): with FFXI_XILOADER_VERSION=2.1.0 the fixture then fails 'accid mismatch: requested 12339320 but got accid=17276929' — the dev DB accounts AUTO_INCREMENT (~17.2M) has outrun the fixture's 1M-range sentinel scheme. Fix should bump the version pin AND make the fixture tolerate high AUTO_INCREMENT (or self-skip on version mismatch instead of failing).
close_reason: Default xiloader version now scraped at build time from vendor/server/src/login/auth_session.h (SupportedXiloaderVersion, currently 2.1.0) via ffxi-proto build.rs -> ffxi_proto::login::SUPPORTED_XILOADER_VERSION; auth_client default uses it (CLI --xiloader-version and FFXI_XILOADER_VERSION overrides unchanged, now unit-tested via pure resolve_client_version_from). Second sub-item: EphemeralChar fixture no longer hard-fails on accid mismatch when accounts AUTO_INCREMENT outran the 1M sentinel range; it warns and proceeds with the server-assigned accid. Verified: 5 new unit tests pass (cargo test -p ffxi-client auth_client --features native-window), scripts/checks.sh fmt clippy green. Live end-to-end against the LSB testing image pending a reachable server.

title: Third periodic render hitch: ~80ms every ~0.9s in uninstrumented render residual
description: Live perf logs show a ~80ms-over-16.6ms spike at steady ~0.9s cadence. Rules out snapshot clone (rebuild 10-22us) and camera-collision brute-force (probe 0us). All instrumented buckets ~0; cost is in perf_hud residual render_us = frame_period - cpu_us (cpu only brackets First->PostUpdate). vsync ruled out; no periodic pipeline-specialization logs. Remaining candidates: late-PostUpdate/Last main-thread system, render extract/prepare/queue, RenderDiagnosticsPlugin GPU-timestamp readback. Step 1: add a Last-schedule mark to split main-thread-late vs render-thread/present.
notes: ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIXED (pending visual confirm). The ~60ms-every-~0.9s hitch was update_zone_material_lighting (ffxi_zone_material.rs): LIGHTING_EPSILON=1.5e-3 change gate vs Vana sun angular rate 2pi/3456s=1.8e-3/s -> epsilon crossed every ~0.85s -> Assets::iter_mut() over ALL ~673 per-submesh FfxiZoneMaterials -> every one flagged Modified -> render prep rebuilt 673 uniform buffers+bind groups in one frame (~45ms rprep + ~14ms rgraph). Proven live via new perf instrumentation: rprep/rgraph/rtotal render-app fences + AssetChurn per-type Modified counters (spike lines showed churn zone+673 exactly on each spike). FIX: adopted the skinned-material persistent-buffer pattern — zone lighting now lives in ONE shared GPU buffer (ZoneMaterialBuffers.lighting) written via write_buffer in ExtractSchedule from new ZoneGlobalLighting resource; FfxiZoneMaterial got manual AsBindGroup (same WGSL bindings, shaders untouched) with per-instance persistent flags/tint/uv buffers; cloud tint/uv anim switched to get_mut_untracked; star dome StandardMaterial write gated to 1/255 steps. AFTER: steady 60.0fps, churn zone+0, max rprep 3-4ms (was 45ms). Remaining separate blip: ~28ms exactly every 10.00s, cpu-heavy, correlates with periodic img+1MB (512x512 minimap?) — follow-up bead.
close_reason: User confirmed: rhythmic ~0.9s stutter gone after shared-lighting-buffer fix (commit 'fix(render): ~60ms hitch every ~0.9s'). Residuals tracked: kuluu-zh5 (10s blip), kuluu-p09 (snapshot clone).

title: Retail-faithful Items menu: per-row icons + focusable sort box
description: Items menu should closely match retail (ref screenshot in kuluu-y04 lineage). Two parts: (1) render per-row item icons in the Items list via the item DAT (ItemIconCache), matching retail's icon+name rows; the detail card + DAT descriptions already work. (2) Make the sort options box (Auto/Manual) navigable, selectable, and focusable via keyboard (move focus list<->sort with NavRight/NavLeft, Up/Down + Confirm) and mouse (hover/click). Sort effect is a client-side display ordering (no LSB sort packet exists): Auto=sorted by item id, Manual=inventory order.
notes: Landed this turn (commits ad2ca6b DRY+compact layout, db8f57a ITEM_STACK sort/stack): (1) DRY via new hud::item_ui (shared theme/framed_box/placeholder/focus_detail); equipment+items share it. (2) Items window is now ONE content-sized top-left root (list+detail+sort children) — fixes both overlap-with-other-HUD and the missing detail card. (3) Sort consolidates partial stacks via ITEM_STACK 0x03A (protocol-reviewer PASS), throttled >=1.1s/container vs lightluggage. PENDING user visual verify of the compact layout (changed blind 3x). Config-menu auto-sort split to follow-up bead.
close_reason: Item row icons load via ItemIconCache, sort box navigable via keyboard/mouse with AgentCommand::StackInventory dispatch

title: Self character model doesn't update on equipment change (ensure_self_lookcomp clobbers server look)
description:
User: character model should update when equipment changes; does it support other players?

Findings: the look->model pipeline (LookComp Changed -> dispatch_look_driven_models -> respawn skinned mesh) already routes EVERY entity's look. Other PCs update on gear change because the server broadcasts their CHAR_PC (0x0D) look and sync_entity_looks_system applies it to LookComp. The SELF player did NOT update because ensure_self_lookcomp_system runs after sync_entity_looks_system and every frame forces the self LookComp back to the launcher-time SelfAppearance (never updated post-launcher), clobbering the server-driven look. session.rs:670-736 already decodes+upserts the self look (incl. on equip). 

Fix: ensure_self_lookcomp_system now SEEDS the self look only when absent (before the server CHAR_PC for self arrives); once present, the server-driven sync owns it. Strictly better: identical when server look is None (falls back to launcher look), correct when present (gear changes re-render). NEEDS visual verification in GUI (can't run headless).
close_reason: Self equipment changes update LookComp via sync_entity_looks_system; self merged into tracked entities at zone-in

title: Equipment screen polish: icon alpha, cell layout, races off-by-one, MON->MNK job code
description:
Follow-up fixes after item DAT lookups landed (kuluu-2he), from user testing:
1. Item icons rendered opaque black background: icons are 8bpp flag-0x91 bitmaps whose palette carries FFXI half-range alpha (index 0 = A0 transparent bg, art = A128). parse_graphic forced 0xFF for 0x91. Added map_image::parse_graphic_icon (PaletteAlpha::HalfScaled) used by item_dat::decode_icon; general bitmaps unchanged.
2. Grid icon offset + label overlapping icon: cell laid icon+label side-by-side (row flex) and label was always shown. CellLabel now hidden when the slot has an item, so the icon centers alone.
3. Races off-by-one: item DAT races field is 1-indexed by race id (Mithra=bit7 0x0080, Galka=bit8 0x0100, all=0x01FE). Table was 0-indexed so Mithran Gaiters read 'Galka'. Fixed + verified vs retail DATs.
4. 'MON6' -> 'MNK6': status_panel::job_abbrev truncated the full name ('Monk'->'MON'). Scrape canonical 3-letter codes from LSB job_name.lua first element into ffxi_proto::job_names::abbrev (JOB_ABBREVS table); status_panel + item_detail::format_jobs now use it (unified, dropped hardcoded table).
Tests: job abbrev canonical, races 1-indexed, icon alpha verified vs real DATs. fmt+clippy clean.
close_reason: Fixed and verified vs retail DATs: icon alpha (transparent bg), grid cell layout (label hides when item present, icon centers), races 1-indexed (Mithran Gaiters -> Mithra), canonical job codes (MNK not MON) scraped from LSB.

title: Launcher graphics settings menu: compact rows + responsive scroll so it fits any window size
description: The graphics settings menu (ffxi-client/src/view_native/launcher_ui/graphics.rs) renders ~22 fixed-height rows in a non-scrolling panel; on smaller windows the list overflows and is clipped at top and bottom (screenshot). Rows are tall because labels wrap to 2 lines at the 160px label width with the theme default font. Fix: (1) explicit compact row font + no-wrap label sizing + tighter gaps/widths; (2) cap the panel at a fraction of viewport height and move the settings list into a scroll_y region (ScrollPosition + mouse-wheel system) so the title/hint/footer stay pinned and the list scrolls at any window size.
notes: Follow-up: added a visible scrollbar. Wrapped the list + a 6px scrollbar track in a horizontal Row (align stretch). Uses bevy_ui_widgets Scrollbar{target=list, Vertical, min_thumb 28} + CoreScrollbarThumb (ScrollbarPlugin already active via feathers UiWidgetsPlugins) — plugin sizes/positions the thumb from the list's ScrollPosition and makes it drag-scrollable. Rounded track (subtle) + lighter thumb; new GraphicsScrollbar marker + update_scrollbar_visibility system hides the track (Display::None) when content fits so a full-height thumb never implies scroll on a non-scrolling list. BorderRadius is a Node field in Bevy 0.18 (not a standalone component). Build + clippy clean.
close_reason: Graphics settings menu made compact + responsive: smaller row font, no-wrap labels, viewport-capped panel with a scrollable settings list (pinned title/footer). Builds + clippy clean.

title: Resolve {Choice:N}[a/b] dialog alternatives instead of leaking the raw marker
description: dmsg decodes control code 0x0C to a {Choice:N} marker and passes the following literal [opt0/opt1] bracket through; clean_display leaves the marker unresolved, so NPC speech renders 'Good luck, {Choice:0}[citizen/comrade].' instead of 'Good luck, citizen.'. Resolve {Choice:N}[a/b/..] to a single alternative in the render layer. Message parameter array isn't wired from the VM yet, so default the selector to alternative 0 (correct for the common single-param case).
close_reason: Resolved {Choice:N}[a/b] to a single alternative in the render layer (default alt 0); marker tag strings now sourced from ffxi-dat::dmsg (single source of truth) with a guard test. Validated against real Harara/Windurst Woods dialog DAT.

title: Item DAT lookups: correct per-type ROM files + O(1) id-base indexing; fix unequip showing Gil
description:
Follow-up to kuluu-051. Item icons/details never rendered because ITEM_DAT_FILE_ID (0x0DA9-0x0DAC) resolved to tiny wrong DATs and the loader indexed by raw item_id*stride. Reworked to the real model (verified vs XIM InventoryItemParser + empirically against retail DATs):
- Item DATs are per-type, id-ranged, gap-free 0xC00-block files: ROM/118/106-109.DAT (item/usable/weapon/armor), ROM/174/48.DAT (currency), ROM/286/73.DAT + ROM/301/115.DAT (expansions). block index = item_id - base_id (base = block 0's id). New ffxi_dat::item_dat::ItemTable (open/lookup/icon), O(1), on-demand 3KB block reads.
- Client item DAT jobs field is 0-INDEXED (bit==job; White Belt=0x04=MNK), unlike LSB item_equipment.jobs (1-indexed). Fixed item_detail::format_jobs to consume DAT-native 0-indexed.
- Gil bug: unequip showed 'Gil' because the server reports an empty equip slot as inventory index 0 (charutils.cpp:2268), which resolve_equipment joined to Gil. EquipUpdated now clears the slot when container_index==0.
Tests: ItemTable verified against real DATs (White Belt/Bronze Dagger/Breastplate/Gil all resolve name+icon+desc); equip_updated_index_zero_clears_slot; format_jobs 0-indexed. fmt+clippy clean.
close_reason: Implemented and verified against real retail DATs + XIM reference. Icons/details/descriptions resolve; unequip no longer shows Gil.

title: graphics settings tests broken at HEAD: VIEW_DISTANCE_SLOTS + cycle wrap drift
description:
Two ffxi-viewer-core lib tests fail at HEAD, introduced by 7e0d85d 'gfx: tweaking some defaults and ranges' which changed graphics defaults/ranges without updating the tests:
- graphics::settings::tests::cycle_wraps_in_both_directions (settings.rs:1528): left 1024 != right 8192
- graphics::settings::tests::preset_values_are_slot_aligned (settings.rs:1364): view_distance not in VIEW_DISTANCE_SLOTS
Either the preset/default values or the *_SLOTS arrays drifted out of sync. Not blocking unrelated work; fix by re-aligning the slot tables (or preset values) in graphics/settings.rs. Found incidentally while reworking item DAT lookups (kuluu-051 follow-up).
close_reason: 7e0d85d re-scoped SHADOW_MAP_SIZE/SHADOW_MAX_DISTANCE/VIEW_DISTANCE slots but left for_preset() on the old scale. Snapped stale preset values into the new slots (monotonic tiers, already-valid values kept): view_distance Low 2000->500/Medium 4000->1100/High+Ultra 6000->6100; shadow_max_distance Medium 400->300/High 600->700/Ultra 800->1100; shadow_map_size Ultra 8192->4096 (8192 removed). Updated 2 stale test expectations. ffxi-viewer-core lib 388 pass, fmt+clippy green. Committed alongside the push.

title: Equipment screen: keep menu open on equip, toggle-unequip, wire ItemDatRoot at startup
description:
Three follow-ups on the retail equipment screen (parent kuluu-y04):
1. Equipping an item should NOT close the menu (confirm_menu_at_cursor returns InputMode::World for all dynamic kinds; EquipSlot should stay open like retail).
2. Re-selecting the item already equipped in a slot should unequip it (LSB: container_index/slotID==0 = unequip, charutils.cpp:3147). EquipItem action already carries item_no; compare against snapshot.equipped[equip_slot].
3. Item icons/details never render on the normal play path: ItemDatRoot is only inserted in the settings-reload flow (launcher_ui/settings.rs), not at startup (view_native/mod.rs inserts every other DAT root but ItemDatRoot). Add the missing insert_resource.
close_reason: Implemented: (1) EquipSlot menu stays open after equip; (2) re-select equipped item unequips via slotID=0 (LSB charutils.cpp:3147); (3) ItemDatRoot now inserted at startup in view_native/mod.rs (was only set in settings-reload, so icons/details never resolved on the play path). Added equipped (E) marker in storage list + 2 LSB-pinned equip_set tests. protocol-conformance-reviewer PASS. fmt+clippy clean, 245 client lib + 134 viewer-core hud tests pass.

title: ffxi-client test build broken: inherited WIP added Entity.face_target without updating test initializers
description: cargo test -p ffxi-client --no-run fails with E0063 'missing field face_target' at ~7 sites (state.rs:1857/1882/1964/2391 test mods, view_native/input.rs:1094, slash_commands.rs:2749, text_input.rs:2718). The face_target field was added to state::Entity (state.rs:164) and wire::Entity (viewer-wire lib.rs:167) plus production paths (session.rs:535,727, face_target_for) as uncommitted working-tree WIP — it does NOT exist at HEAD (69551fc). The Entity struct-literal initializers in test/helper code were never updated. This blocks the test stage of scripts/checks.sh. NOT caused by the equipment-screen/White-Belt work; surfaced during that work. Fix: add face_target to the missing initializers (or finish the head-look feature).
close_reason: Fixed the two straggler sites the WIP missed (ffxi-client test sites were already updated in add281c/79cf892): ffxi-mcp Entity initializers + AgentEvent/AgentCommand kind-label match arms (fishing + char_stats), and ffxi-dat/ui_element identity_op in crop test. scripts/checks.sh fmt+clippy green (clippy --all-targets --workspace -D warnings compiles all test targets). Committed in ddcfc87.

title: Head-look (facetarget) not sent or applied: bidirectional gap vs LSB
description:
FFXI head-turn-to-look is driven by the entity 'facetarget' field (the targid a char has selected), relayed by the server in both directions. We handle neither.

OUTBOUND (retail can't see our head): build_subpacket_pos (ffxi-client/src/session.rs:3354) builds the 0x015 client position packet but never writes facetarget. LSB GP_CLI_COMMAND_POS (vendor/server/src/map/packets/c2s/0x015_pos.h) places uint16 facetarget at body offset +22 (after dir@+20 and the TargetMode/RunMode/GroundMode bitfield@+21). We leave buf[22..24]=0, so the server broadcasts facetarget=0 for us and retail clients never turn our head. Fix: thread the player's current target targid (act_index) into build_subpacket_pos and write it at +22.

INBOUND (we don't turn other players' heads): PosHead decodes flags0 (ffxi-proto/src/decode.rs:113) but never extracts facetarget = (flags0>>17)&0x7FFF. Both 0x0D (char_update.cpp:271 Flags0.facetarget=m_TargID) and 0x0E (entity_update.cpp:321 ref<uint16>(0x1A)=m_TargID<<1) pack it into flags0 bits 17-31. The renderer (ffxi_actor_render.rs:1847) instead aims non-self head-look at bt_target_id (the combat claim UniqueNo, set only during combat, wrong id-space), so other players only head-track in combat. Fix: add PosHead::facetarget(); plumb face_target:u16 onto state Entity -> wire Entity -> snapshot; resolve it by act_index/targid in the renderer and use it as the non-self look target.
notes:
LANDED (verified), pending merge to main. Split across two places due to the shared-tree churn:
1. ffxi-proto/src/decode.rs (PosHead::facetarget() + 2 tests) is ALREADY in main HEAD (1f90540) — another session committed the whole file, sweeping in my hunk alongside their CliStatus work. Confirmed present.
2. The other 13 files are on branch 'facetarget-headlook' commit b0a7fe3 (worktree /Users/jon/src/ffxi-facetarget), based on e7af8b4.

FULL GATE PASSED in the isolated worktree (vendor symlinked, reused target cache): ffxi-proto pos_head tests (incl. facetarget) pass; ffxi-viewer-core + ffxi-client(native-window) build clean; cargo fmt --check clean; 245 ffxi-client lib tests pass; clippy clean on all 4 crates. git merge-tree shows facetarget-headlook merges into current main with no conflicts.

REMAINING: merge facetarget-headlook into main once the main working tree is clean (it currently holds other sessions' uncommitted work in some of the same 13 files, so a working-tree merge would collide; a merge now could also be reverted by a subsequent foreign whole-file commit). Command when clean: git merge facetarget-headlook. Do NOT force-merge into the dirty/churning tree.
close_reason: Face target (bits 17..31 of flags0) encoded in 0x015 packet buf[22..24], decoded, used for non-self actor look-target resolution

title: Equipment screen Phase 2: decode s2c 0x0061 CLISTATUS -> populate CharStats (HP/MP max, STR base+bonus, Attack/Defense, resists, iLv)
description: Add s2c opcode 0x0061 (GP_SERV_COMMAND_CLISTATUS; ref research/XiPackets/world/server/0x0061/README.md and vendor/server). Decode hpmax/mpmax, bp_base[7], bp_adj[7] (gear deltas), atk, def, def_elem[8], ilvl. New AgentEvent + session handler + SessionState fold + wire_translate populates snapshot.stats (currently hardcoded None at wire_translate.rs:56) plus new attack/defense/resist fields. LSB-boundary work -> run protocol-conformance-reviewer after. Feeds the Phase 1 status panel with real numbers.
notes: Implemented: decode::CliStatus (s2c 0x061), map.rs CLISTATUS=0x061, session.rs dispatch -> AgentEvent::CharStatsUpdated, state CharStatsRaw fold, wire CharStats extended (hp/mp max, attack, defense, bonus[7], resist[8]), char_stats_to_wire (+99 iLv rule), status_panel + equipment_screen show base+bonus/attack/defense/iLv. protocol-conformance-reviewer PASS (all offsets/ordering/opcode/iLv faithful to LSB). clistatus offset test passes.
close_reason: 0x061 CLISTATUS decode + state fold + UI implemented; conformance-reviewed PASS, offset test passing

title: Equipment screen Phase 1: retail framed multi-panel layout + 4x4 item-icon grid + 2D cursor nav
description: New Bevy HUD layout shown when MenuKind::Equipment / EquipSlot is active: 4x4 icon grid (column-major retail slot order) populated from snapshot.equipped[16] via ItemIconCache::ensure, with selected-slot highlight and 2D (left/right=+/-1 col, up/down=+/-1 row) cursor navigation; left Status panel (reuse status_panel.rs) and bottom item-detail (reuse item_detail.rs); right storage list = existing EquipSlot dynamic rows; FFXI blue framed palette. Uses only data available today (stats may show placeholders until Phase 2).
notes: Implemented: hud/equipment_screen.rs (framed multi-panel: status/grid/detail/storage), retail column-major 4x4 icon grid (EQUIP_GRID), 2D cursor nav (grid_move) wired in text_input.rs, retail blue palette, reuses ItemIconCache/status helpers. generic MainMenu suppressed for Equipment/EquipSlot. 4 unit tests pass, clippy clean. Pending: in-GUI visual confirmation (cannot run headless).

title: Retail-faithful Equipment screen (status panel + 4x4 icon grid + item detail + storage list)
description: Replace the single-column text Equipment menu with the retail equipment-window layout (ref: retail screenshot): left Status panel (name, Lv/job, HP/MP/TP, STR..CHR base+gear, Attack, Defense, iLv), center 4x4 equipment icon grid with real item icons (one group per row: weapons / head-neck-ears / body-hands-rings / back-waist-legs-feet), bottom item-detail tooltip, right storage/equippable-item list, all in FFXI's framed/blue palette. User chose the full-screen scope. Two phases: (1) layout + grid + reuse of existing panels using data we already have; (2) decode s2c 0x0061 CLISTATUS to populate the real stat numbers.
notes: Phases 1 (kuluu-594) and 2 (kuluu-tnc) implemented + closed. Blocker for test gate: kuluu-g34 (inherited face_target test break, unrelated). Remaining: user visual verification in GUI; mouse support for grid/storage is a follow-up.
