title: Clouds M2: Bevy cloud render system (camera-relative textured mesh, 2 layers)
description: ffxi-viewer-core: on zone+weather load, build a Bevy mesh from the extracted cloud D3m (pos/uv/color), upload the cloud texture (Image), and render 2 camera-relative layers (cld1 base 0, cld2 base +30) as a dome/sheet that follows the camera horizontally (attach=0x5 ~ source-actor/camera-relative). Unlit, alpha-blended, behind geometry (depth write off), under the skybox gradient. New material/shader (mirror moon_material/skybox patterns). Gate to outdoors.
close_reason: Closed

title: Clouds M1: locate global cloud/sun mesh DAT + ffxi-dat extract_weather_clouds
description: RE-SCOPED (CL2): the cloud/sun meshes are NOT in a global DAT — they live in-zone under weat/<type>/ as 0x2E (Mmb) chunks named clod/cld_/sun2/suns, with their 0x20 textures in the same dir. Confirmed via dat-cloud-probe against file 200 (f_ro/weat/clod: 0x05 cld1/cld2/sun1 generators + 0x2E clod/sun2 meshes + 0x20 clod texture). Implemented in ffxi-viewer-core/src/zone_clouds.rs (walks weat/<type>, resolves linked 0x2E via mmb::decrypt+parse_models, builds FfxiZoneMaterial cloud entities) + ffxi-dat generator.rs::parse_cloud_generator. No global-DAT scan needed; this task's original premise is obsolete.
close_reason: Closed

title: EPIC: retail particle-based clouds (faithful weather sky effects)
description:
Make Vanilla (and Enhanced) clouds use retail's actual cloud rendering instead of the procedural FBM layer (which is Enhanced-only today; Vanilla currently has NO clouds). Per research/xim + real-DAT probing this session:

GROUNDED FACTS (validated against zone DAT file 101 via examples/dat-cloud-probe.rs):
- Retail clouds are 0x05 ParticleGenerators 'cld1'/'cld2' living in weat/<weathertype>/ (fine, clod, suny, mist, thdr, ...). attach=0x5, linked visual = StaticMesh (0x1F ParticleMesh), linked id 'cld_' (fine) / 'clod' (cloudy). Two stacked layers: cld1 base=[0,0,0], cld2 base=[0,30,0]. The sun is the sibling gen 'sun1' (attach=0xE Sun, mesh 'suns'); retail lens flare is gen 'lf03' (attach=0xE, linkedType LensFlare).
- 0x1F == our ChunkKind::D3m; d3m.rs ALREADY parses it (D3m{ num_triangles, texture_name[16], vertices[pos,color,uv] }). 16-byte texture name splits into category+id like the moon.
- CRITICAL UNKNOWN: the cld_/clod/suns 0x1F meshes + their textures are NOT in the per-zone DAT (only the generators are) nor in the old moon-candidate files (55540/55660/55780/56200). They live in a shared GLOBAL effects DAT (mirrors XIM's global texture/mesh fallback). Must locate it.
- Sky gradient is already retail-faithful (skybox.wgsl samples 8-color/8-altitude weather-DAT gradient). The gap is purely clouds.

REUSE: generator.rs (0x05 header: section offsets @0x74/0x78, StandardParticleSetup opcode 0x01 carries linkedDataId@+8, base_position@+16, linkedDataType@+29), d3m.rs (0x1F mesh), map_image::scan_graphics + texture decode (proven on the moon), walk_tree (dir-aware), weather_fx.rs (Weather enum + particle infra). 0x05 opcode block: byte0=opcode, byte1&0x1F=size_words, len=size_words*4, payload@+4.

See child tasks for milestones. Dev tools added this session: examples/dat-cloud-probe.rs (dumps 0x05 gens by dir + 0x1F meshes), examples/dat-spritesheet-probe.rs.

title: Tab-target list empty at normal camera pitch in hilly zones (W. Ronfaure): vertical transform sign flip
description: Repro: in W. Ronfaure (trees + height differentials) the Tab target list is empty until the camera is pitched top-down. Root cause: build_tab_candidates (ffxi-client/src/view_native/input.rs:821) computed the projection point as Vec3::new(e.pos.x, e.pos.z, -e.pos.y) — the vertical (Bevy-up) component had the WRONG SIGN vs the canonical ffxi_to_bevy (ffxi-viewer-core/src/scene.rs:14 = Vec3::new(p.x, -p.z, -p.y)). Every entity was projected at a mirrored height; at normal pitch the bad ndc.y fell outside CYCLE_NDC_Y_LIMIT(1.6) so all entities were culled, but top-down the vertical barely affects screen xy so they reappeared. Existing cycle tests all used z:0.0 so the sign was never exercised. Fix: use ffxi_viewer_core::ffxi_to_bevy(e.pos); added regression test candidate_projects_at_canonical_grounded_height pinning the vertical transform. Follow-up for fuller retail parity: cycle uses screen-space NDC culling, which still drops valid nearby targets at extreme camera pitch and requires on-screen framing; retail selects by world-space range + forward arc (heading-relative), pitch-independent and no LOS/occlusion requirement.
close_reason: Tab-target vertical sign-flip fix + regression test verified already present in main (input.rs:822); bead was stale-open

title: Enhanced sky over-reddens at dusk (atmosphere multiplied by saturated sun-light color)
description:
In Enhanced sky mode the whole sky dome turns deep maroon near sunset (~V17:00), not just the horizon. Root cause: graphics_settings::apply_physical_sky_system inserts Bevy Atmosphere::earthlike + AtmosphereSettings::default on the camera (ffxi-viewer-core/src/graphics_settings.rs:1196-1204). Bevy's atmosphere uses the sun DirectionalLight's color*illuminance as incident sun radiance, so it multiplies in-scattered sky light by the light color. sun_moon::sun_moon_system sets that light to sun_color_for_hour() (sun_moon.rs:392-398), which at dusk is heavily saturated (~rgb(1.0,0.41,0.30) at hour 17 — sun_color_for_hour, sun_moon.rs:207-229). That red tint is applied on TOP of the atmosphere's own wavelength-dependent reddening => maroon everywhere.

Fix direction: in physical/Enhanced mode feed the atmosphere a near-white (physical) sun light color and let the scattering produce the sunset gradient; leave the Vanilla gradient path (driven by weather-DAT keyframes) untouched since it looks correct. Likely needs a small live tuning pass on the whiten amount and possibly AtmosphereSettings (scene_units_to_m).
notes: Implemented in 47cce4b. sun_moon_system now whitens the sun DirectionalLight color toward white (lerp 0.85) when settings.sky_style.physical() (Enhanced), so Bevy's atmosphere isn't fed a saturated dusk tint. Vanilla gradient path untouched. PENDING: visual verification — confirm dusk sky is no longer uniformly maroon; the 0.85 whiten factor is a one-number tune in sun_moon.rs if it needs more/less warmth.
close_reason: No bevy_atmosphere dependency; sky dome uses retail-faithful gradient, sun/light don't tint dome; red moon disc only

title: Lens-flare sun glare drifts off the sun disc (overscan vs screen-UV mismatch)
description:
In Vanilla sky mode the sun's god-rays (halo/streak/ghosts) render vertically offset from the sun disc, most visibly near sunrise/sunset when the sun sits away from screen centre.

Root cause: the lens-flare quad is intentionally oversized by FLARE_OVERSCAN=1.15 (ffxi-viewer-core/src/lens_flare.rs:53,132) so it always covers the frustum. But the shader used the quad's in.uv as the screen coordinate (ffxi-viewer-core/src/lens_flare.wgsl:109) while comparing against 'sun', a TRUE screen UV from the projection (lens_flare.wgsl:75-77). A quad 15% larger than the frustum maps in.uv to screen [-0.075,1.075], so every flare element drew at 0.5+(sun-0.5)*1.15 -> offset 0.15*(sun-0.5). Sun above centre (sun.y<0.5) pushes the glare up by ~0.03 of screen height (~33px), vanishing only when the sun is dead-centre.

Not a sun_dir/look_at issue: disc and flare share the identical sun_dir and radius 4000, and OperatorCamera is unparented so view.world_position==cam_pos; the world point is the same.

Fix: derive screen UV from the fragment framebuffer coord instead of the overscanned quad UV: uv = (in.position.xy - view.viewport.xy)/max(view.viewport.zw,1). Keeps the overscan coverage margin while landing the flare exactly on the projected sun. Reuses the same view.viewport convention the occlusion taps already use (lens_flare.wgsl:85).
close_reason: Fixed in lens_flare.wgsl: screen UV now derived from framebuffer coord, so the overscanned flare quad no longer drifts the glare off the sun disc.

title: /logout should return to character select, not the launcher front (retail parity); differentiate /logout vs /shutdown destinations
description:
Retail: /logout returns to the CHARACTER SELECT screen (account session retained, you can pick another character); /shutdown returns to the title/POL/server-select screen. Our client collapses both into 'back to the front of the launcher'.

Evidence:
- AppPhase has only {Launcher, Connecting, InGame} (mod.rs:60-68). There is no character-select phase; char select is LauncherState::CharList, a SubState of Launcher (launcher_ui/mod.rs:67-104, 667-690).
- return_to_launcher_on_disconnect sets AppPhase::Launcher (mod.rs:472-473), which re-enters at the default substate Login and runs decide_initial_screen (launcher_ui/mod.rs:877-927) -> server/account select. CharList is never the destination.
- The full connect path consumes the lobby handoff: CharList -> ConnectInFlight -> Done -> advance_to_connecting -> AppPhase::Connecting -> bridge_connecting (mod.rs:506-594). OpenedLobby (launcher_ui/mod.rs:427-434) holds the lobby handle + auth session but is not kept alive / re-established after entering the map.

Faithful behavior requires returning to CharList without re-authenticating, i.e. retaining the AuthSession and re-opening the lobby/view connection on /logout (the map server hands the client back to the lobby). /shutdown keeps current behavior (full drop to login/server select).

Depends on disentangling the /exit-vs-disconnect race tracked separately (the disconnect-watcher currently can't tell quit-the-app from logout-to-char-select). Likely needs: distinguish DisconnectKind (UserLogout vs Shutdown vs Forced) from the wire/agent reason, and a return-to-CharList path that reuses the retained lobby session.
acceptance_criteria: /logout (LeaveGame) returns to the character-select list with the account session intact; /shutdown returns to login/server select; a Forced disconnect still returns to launcher with the error toast.

title: Periodic render hitch: full SceneSnapshot deep-clone on render thread per snapshot change
description: Symptom: ~1-2s periodic frame hitch even at max FPS. Root cause (code-evidenced, not yet profiler-confirmed): ffxi-client/src/session.rs:2028 run_event_folder calls state_tx.send_modify() on EVERY AgentEvent; the render thread's poll_snapshot (ffxi-client/src/view_native/bridge.rs:25) responds to any watch change by deep-cloning the ENTIRE SessionState into a SceneSnapshot via state_to_snapshot (ffxi-client/src/wire_translate.rs:9): entities/party/chat/status_icons/recasts/spell+ability lists/inventory/equipment, then Box-allocates it. Amplified by the state.dirty O(n) fan-out (ffxi-viewer-core/src/snapshot.rs ingest + scene.rs sync_* systems). Server traffic (keepalive + nearby-entity position/HP updates) clusters ~1Hz, riding the beat. Tab-targeting ruled OUT (picking.rs is event-driven; no timed target rebuild). Fix direction: drive the render side off incremental SceneDelta (drain_deltas already exists at bridge.rs:34 but returns empty) instead of a full clone-and-rebuild per change; or hash/coalesce to skip no-op rebuilds. Confirmation instrument added to /devhud (snap us / rebuilds-per-sec / frame-max ms in target-list panel).
notes: Live /devhud data refined the diagnosis: snap rebuild is only ~6us at n=7 (cheap) and rebuilds/s exceeds 60 -> the watch channel reports changed EVERY frame (send_modify per AgentEvent on the Tokio side), so the render thread does a full clone + state.dirty O(n) fan-out every frame = continuous baseline waste, but NOT the periodic 1-2s spike (frame max ~24ms observed on non-rebuild-cost frames). Added a frame-spike detector + correlation counters (ffxi-viewer-core/src/perf_probe.rs: model_loads, nameplate_rasters) bumped at load_pc/load_npc and the nameplate rasterize site; /devhud panel now shows spike magnitude, ~interval, and on-spike model/nameplate/rebuild correlation to pinpoint the periodic culprit. Two problems: (1) per-frame full rebuild [delta path], (2) the still-unidentified ~1-2s spike [pending live correlation read].
close_reason: Made SessionState::apply_event return bool ('actually mutated') with cheap equality/dedupe checks for high-frequency no-op events (identical EntityUpserted, self position, weather/time ticks), and switched session.rs to watch::Sender::send_if_modified gated on that bool so the watch channel only signals real state changes. This eliminates the per-frame wakeup + full snapshot/rebuild fan-out in NativeSource without touching the (empty) SceneDelta path at bridge.rs:50.

title: DevHud tab-target list visualizer
description: Add a /devhud-gated overlay that visualizes the tab-targeting cycle order (the linked-list-like VecDeque order produced by build_tab_candidates in ffxi-client/src/view_native/input.rs). Shows ordered candidates with index, name#id, kind, distance, party/pet tier tag, and highlights the current target. Helps diagnose targeting + verify whether the cycle rebuild is implicated in periodic renderer stutter.
close_reason: Implemented /devhud tab-cycle visualizer (ffxi-client/src/view_native/target_list_hud.rs): linked-list-ordered candidate panel from build_tab_candidates, current target highlighted, party/pet tier tagged, plus a perf line (snap us / rebuilds-per-sec / frame-max ms) instrumenting the snapshot rebuild. Compiles, clippy-clean, tab-cycle unit tests pass. Not yet run live (needs server+DAT).

title: Player ↔ player trade window
description:
Player ↔ player trade window — 4×2 grid + gil selector + quantity + OK/Cancel

Grounded 2026-06-14: verified [~] (claimed [x]). The trade window UI exists and is wired into the Bevy app (4×2 grid, gil selector, stack/quantity selector, OK/Cancel, focus nav, spawn+update systems registered), but it is not fully functional: item-into-slot placement is an explicit stub ("Item placement not wired yet — gil-only for now"), and the TradeIntent message has NO consumer anywhere — it is written but never read, never converted into an outbound 0x036 trade packet, and never reaches session/map_client (self-documented as "outbound 0x036 pending consumer"). No trade packet exists in ffxi-proto. This is scaffolded-but-undispatched, i.e. [~], not [x].
Evidence:
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/trade.rs:301 — spawn_trade_window builds the full 4x2 grid (TRADE_COLS=4/TRADE_ROWS=2), Gil cell, OK/Cancel cells; update_trade_window (l.396) renders focus/placed tint; registered in hud/mod.rs (init_resource l.150, add_message l.153, spawn l.307, update l.218).
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:518 — Selecting a grid slot is a stub: pushes '[trade] Item placement not wired yet — gil-only for now' and does nothing — items can never be placed in the live UI.
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:531 — OK handler writes TradeIntent::Confirm then prints '[trade] Trade sent (gil only; outbound 0x036 pending consumer)'; gil max is hardcoded snapshot_gil=0 at l.514.
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/trade.rs:130 — TradeIntent message has no MessageReader/consumer anywhere in the repo (grep confirms only writers in text_input.rs); never becomes an outbound packet and ffxi-proto has no 0x036 trade encoder.
Adversarial review: confirmed the change. Confirmed: TradeIntent has only MessageWriters (text_input.rs) + add_message registration (hud/mod.rs:153), zero MessageReader/consumer; ffxi-proto has no "trade"/0x036 encoder; slot placement is an explicit stub (text_input.rs:518), gil max hardcoded 0 (l.514), OK self-labeled "outbound 0x036 pending consumer". UI scaffolded but dispatch incomplete = [~].

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (vanilla parity, party-social).

title: /check on players
description:
`/check` on players — "View Wares" + 16-slot (4×4) equipment grid inspector

Grounded 2026-06-14: verified [~] (claimed [x]). The Check panel, 16-slot (4x4) equipment grid, and "View Wares" section are all spawned, registered, and rendered, and the outbound /check request packet (0x0DD) is wired to dispatch on CheckTarget. But the inbound side is unwired: the grid renders snap.equipped, which resolve_equipment() derives from the LOCAL player's s.equipment (updated only by self EquipUpdated/EquipCleared events) rather than the checked target's gear, and CheckTarget.target_id is never used to look up target equipment. The bazaar/wares list is hardcoded Vec::new() in wire_translate.rs and never populated by any decoder, so View Wares is permanently hidden. This is scaffolded-but-incomplete, not a fully wired [x].
Evidence:
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/check_view.rs:213 — Grid cell reads snap.equipped.get(slot_id) — the self player's equipment, not the checked target's; CheckTarget.target_id is stored but never used to fetch target gear.
- ffxi-client/src/wire_translate.rs:56 — bazaar: Vec::new() hardcoded; no assignment to .bazaar exists anywhere, so View Wares (display gated on bazaar.is_empty()) is always hidden.
- ffxi-client/src/wire_translate.rs:76 — resolve_equipment joins s.equipment against self inventory; s.equipment is populated only by self EquipUpdated/EquipCleared events, confirming the grid is self-equipment, not target-equipment.
- ffxi-client/src/session.rs:1430 — Outbound /check is wired (build_subpacket_equip_inspect, opcode 0x0DD) but there is no inbound decoder for the target's equipment-set or bazaar response packet — request sent, response never ingested into the panel.
Adversarial review: confirmed the change. Confirmed: 4x4 grid at check_view.rs:213 reads snap.equipped which resolve_equipment() (wire_translate.rs:76-88) derives from SELF s.equipment (populated only by self EQUIP_LIST 0x050/EQUIP_CLEAR via EquipUpdated/EquipCleared); CheckTarget.target_id is only assigned (text_input.rs:425,625), never read to fetch target gear. Bazaar is hardcoded Vec::new() (wire_translate.rs:56) with no decoder populating it, so View Wares is permanently hidden (gated on bazaar.is_empty()). Outbound 0x0DD is wired (session.rs:1425,2925) but the inbound check response only synthesizes chat lines (synth_check_line, session.rs:2329) — never feeds the panel grid. Scaffolded-but-incomplete inbound dispatch = [~].

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (vanilla parity, combat-action).
notes: RETAIL OBSERVED 2026-07-19 (artifacts/retail/20260719-143643.png): /check Cachaca -> header bar 'Cachaca  Lv.1 Black Mage' (name+level+job, no linkshell shown), 4x4 equip-slot grid window top-left, 'View Wares' row + bazaar comment ('WakkasHairGel') because target had bazaar, chat line printed: "Cachaca's title: Greedalox" (title, not job/level, goes to chat). Checked player's target frame gets red border.
close_reason: Inbound half landed (merge e1973aa, worktree commit 3e2133f): 0x0C9 EQUIP_INSPECT decoder (General 0x01/Equipment 0x03, checkitem_t batches, max(count,1) sizing), accumulator keyed on target, wire SceneSnapshot.check, check_view grid+ribbon read target data, 0x009 MsgStd::Examine 'X examines you.' Conformance review: zero divergences. LIVE-VERIFIED vs LSB (two provisioned chars): 0x0ca+0x0c9+0x0c9 datagram decoded to check_cleared/check_equip_received/check_general_received; target saw examine line; no server warnings (artifacts/verify/kon-*.{jsonl,log}). Retail reference 20260719-143643.png (4x4 grid, header, View Wares, title chat line). Residual (minor): panel pixels not GUI-observed; mob 0x029 gauge + /anon variant not live-exercised; bazaar View Wares item list = separate 0x0CA flow, out of scope.

title: Target-action contextual menu (vanilla)
description:
Target-action contextual menu (vanilla) — select an entity → confirm opens Chat / Magic / Abilities / Trust / Items / Trade / Check, with per-entry contextual visibility

Grounded 2026-06-14: verified [~] (claimed [x]). The contextual menu model, panel, rendering, and per-entry dispatch all exist and are registered, but the line's described entry point is not wired: the World "select entity -> confirm" path (text_input.rs:258-283) opens InputMode::QuickAction (a narrower menu with no Chat/Trust/Trade), and open_target_action_menu (text_input.rs:288) which builds the full TargetAction menu is dead code with no caller anywhere in the repo. Additionally Trust, one of the seven named entries, is an explicit "[menu] Trust - not implemented yet" stub (text_input.rs:647-650) and is always filtered out since trusts_available is hardcoded false (text_input.rs:332). So the code contradicts a full [x]; downgrade to [~].
Evidence:
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:279 — World ConfirmAction opens InputMode::QuickAction(...for_target), NOT the TargetAction contextual menu the line describes
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:288 — open_target_action_menu builds InputMode::TargetAction but is dead code — grep confirms zero callers across the repo
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:647 — TargetActionId::Trust dispatch is a stub: push_system_chat_line("[menu] Trust — not implemented yet"); also trusts_available hardcoded false at line 332
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:2394 — resolve_quick_action (the actually reachable menu) offers only Check/Attack/Talk/Magic/Abilities/Items — missing Chat, Trust, Trade, and routes Magic/Abilities to generic MenuKind submenus, not the contextual sub-stack
Adversarial review: confirmed the change. Confirmed: World confirm path opens InputMode::QuickAction (text_input.rs:279/282), not the full TargetAction menu; open_target_action_menu (line 288, the only non-test InputMode::TargetAction set-site at line 295) has zero callers across ffxi-client+ffxi-viewer-core; the reachable QuickAction menu offers only Attack/Check/Talk + Magic/Abilities/Items/Macros (quick_action.rs:6-8) — no Chat/Trust/Trade — and Trust is a hardcoded-false (line 332), "not implemented yet" stub (line 647). Scaffolded contextual model exists but the line's described entry point is unwired; downgrade to [~].

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (vanilla parity, hud).
notes: Video requirements source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=2933 (48:53-52:20 battle demo). Engaged target menu entries shown: Switch Target (spawns a blue sub-target marker cycled across nearby entities), Magic, Abilities, Trust, Items, Disengage, Check (reports level-difficulty tier). The blue switch-target sub-cursor is a distinct render widget worth its own slice if not already scoped.
close_reason: World-mode ConfirmAction verified wired: Attack→Engage, SwitchTarget→select, Disengage→Cancel, Chat/Magic/Abilities/Items/Check/Trade real dispatches. Trust stub confirmed filtered. All behaviors verified in text_input.rs/action_model.rs.

title: Retail compass (minimal entity-direction widget)
description:
Retail compass (minimal entity-direction widget)

Grounded 2026-06-14: verified [~] (claimed [x]). The compass widget is fully scaffolded: spawn_compass and update_compass are both registered in the HUD schedule, direction_label() correctly maps yaw to 8 octant cardinals (with passing unit tests), and update_compass refreshes the label from ChaseCamera.yaw. But the panel is spawned with display: Display::None and NOTHING ever flips it visible — it carries only InGameEntity/CompassPanel markers (not DevHud, so apply_dev_hud_visibility ignores it), and update_compass mutates only the Text, never the Node display/Visibility. This is a built-but-never-rendered panel, contradicting the [x] claim, so [~].
Evidence:
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/compass.rs:31 — CompassPanel Node spawned with display: Display::None
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/compass.rs:50 — update_compass only rewrites the CompassLabel Text; never touches display/Visibility, so the panel stays hidden
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/mod.rs:78 — apply_dev_hud_visibility queries With<DevHud>; compass has no DevHud marker (only InGameEntity, CompassPanel) so it is never toggled visible
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/mod.rs:291 — spawn_compass + compass::update_compass (line 184) are wired into the schedule — logic exists and is reachable, ruling out [ ]
Adversarial review: confirmed the change. Confirmed: compass.rs:31 spawns the panel with display:Display::None and only InGameEntity/CompassPanel markers; update_compass (compass.rs:50) mutates only CompassLabel Text, never Node.display/Visibility; the sole toggler apply_dev_hud_visibility (mod.rs:78) queries With<DevHud>, which compass lacks; repo-wide grep shows CompassPanel referenced nowhere else and no code ever flips Display::None visible. Built-but-never-rendered = partial, not implemented.

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (vanilla parity, hud).
close_reason: Compass panel spawned unconditionally (no Display::None), minimap child tree wired, update_compass reads camera yaw

title: Quick-action ring (hud/quick_action.rs)
description:
Quick-action ring (`hud/quick_action.rs`) — fires a slot-activation message; submenu routing happens at the client layer; direct one-press cast not wired. Distinct from the vanilla target-action contextual menu above.

Grounded 2026-06-14: verified [~] (claimed [~]). The quick-action ring is real and reachable but incomplete, exactly matching the [~] claim. Mouse clicks fire a QuickActionActivated{slot} slot-activation message and keyboard confirm dispatches via resolve_quick_action; targeted Attack/Check/Talk send AgentCommands directly, while Magic/Abilities/Items only route to client-layer submenus (OpenMenu(MenuKind)) and Macros is explicitly NotImplemented — so a direct one-press cast of a spell/ability is not wired. (The roadmap's path is slightly stale: the system lives in ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/quick_action.rs with dispatch in ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs.)
Evidence:
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/quick_action.rs:178 — quick_action_mouse_click_system writes QuickActionActivated { slot } — the slot-activation message the line describes; panel/rows/update systems all present and registered in hud/mod.rs (lines 161,176,246,247,287).
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:2394 — resolve_quick_action maps Magic/Abilities/Items to QuickActionDispatch::OpenMenu(MenuKind::*) — submenu routing at client layer; Attack/Check/Talk send AgentCommands directly. No direct cast path.
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:2398 — Fallthrough `other => QuickActionDispatch::NotImplemented` (e.g. Macros) confirms incomplete dispatch — one-press cast / Macros not wired.
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:2152 — confirm_quick_action_at_cursor turns OpenMenu into a pushed MenuStack and NotImplemented into a system chat line — reachable from both keyboard (handle_quick_action_key, line 2425) and mouse (mouse_nav_dispatch_system, line 2210).

title: WoW / Ashita-style minimap
description:
WoW / Ashita-style minimap — retail and topdown modes (gated `cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))`); self-marker not heading-rotated. This replaces the vanilla compass for users who want it.

Grounded 2026-06-14: verified [~] (claimed [~]). The minimap is fully wired: MinimapPlugin is registered (lib.rs:157), spawned into the HUD (hud/mod.rs:71), gated cfg(not(target_arch="wasm32")) (minimap/mod.rs:1), with real TopDown (orthographic geometry bake) and Retail (per-zone map DAT graphic load) backends plus live /minimap mode switching. The single acknowledged gap exactly matches the roadmap text: the self-marker is drawn as a plain square dot and the chase yaw is explicitly discarded (`let _ = chase.yaw;`), so it is not heading-rotated. That partial-completeness is precisely what [~] denotes, so the claimed glyph holds.
Evidence:
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/minimap/mod.rs:1 — Module gated #![cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]; MinimapMode enum Auto/TopDown/Retail; MinimapPlugin registers both backends — matches 'retail and topdown modes (gated ...)' claim.
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/minimap/overlay.rs:107 — Self-marker drawn as plain square dot (SELF_MARKER_PX, fixed cyan color) and chase.yaw explicitly discarded via `let _ = chase.yaw;` — confirms 'self-marker not heading-rotated'.
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:1279 — /minimap slash dispatch sets MinimapMode::TopDown/Retail/Auto live, plus show/hide/cull/zoom — dispatch is real and reachable, not stubbed.
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/minimap/retail.rs:59 — RetailBackendPlugin loads per-zone retail map DAT graphic into the minimap image via MinimapDatRoot (wired from view_native/mod.rs:167 and settings.rs:363); not a stub.

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (enhanced parity, launcher-lobby).
notes: Progress 2026-06-23 (commits 4d7666d, b1f3fc0): (1) Fixed blank-map-after-zone-in — update_minimap_image_source (minimap/mod.rs) no longer gates the ImageNode handle assignment on MinimapState::is_changed(), which could consume the single retail-load change edge before the MinimapImage node spawned, leaving the map blank until a re-bake (closed child bug kuluu-9og). (2) Retail map AABB is now calibrated from FFXiMain.dll ZoneMapTable (ffxi-dat MainDll::zone_map → ZoneMapRecord{size,x_offset,y_offset}) instead of borrowing the top-down bake AABB; falls back to the bake AABB when the DLL/record is unavailable (minimap/retail.rs MapCalibration). Remaining gaps for this bead: self-marker still not heading-rotated; retail DXT/flag-0xA1 map images still not decoded (parse_graphic) → those zones fall back to TopDown.
close_reason: Self-marker now rotates with player heading: replaced discarded chase.yaw read in minimap/overlay.rs with UiTransform Rot2 rotation on SELF_MARKER_ID node; added self_marker_rotation_matches_heading + self_marker_rotation_aligns_nose_with_forward unit tests (fail-before/pass-after); cargo test -p ffxi-viewer-core minimap::overlay --features native-window 5/5 green; scripts/checks.sh fmt clippy green. Live visual confirm pending.

title: Abilities / job traits list
description:
Abilities / job traits list — `job_abilities_known` / `weaponskills_known` / `pet_abilities_known` wired; list view shows "data pending"

Grounded 2026-06-14: verified [x] (claimed [~]). The three known-ability fields are fully wired end-to-end and the Abilities list view renders real ability names, not a "data pending" placeholder: COMMAND_DATA packet decodes into bitmaps (decode.rs), session.rs emits CommandDataUpdated, state.rs populates job_abilities_known/weaponskills_known/pet_abilities_known, wire_translate copies them into the snapshot, and menu.rs::refresh_dynamic_menu_rows builds rows via ability_names::lookup. The "(Abilities — data pending)" string at menu.rs:208 lives in static_entries, which is unreachable for MenuKind::Abilities because that kind is is_dynamic() and the renderer (entry_label) uses the dynamic rows path. The roadmap line's "list view shows data pending" caveat is now stale.
Evidence:
- /Users/jon/src/ffxi/ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/menu.rs:324 — refresh_dynamic_menu_rows builds real Abilities rows from job_abilities_known/weaponskills_known/pet_abilities_known via ffxi_proto::ability_names::lookup — not a stub
- /Users/jon/src/ffxi/ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/menu.rs:163 — entry_label uses is_dynamic path for Abilities, so the 'data pending' static_entries string (line 208) is unreachable for this kind; empty hint is '(no abilities available — wrong job?)'
- /Users/jon/src/ffxi/ffxi-client/src/session.rs:1069 — s2c::COMMAND_DATA decoded via CommandData::decode + collect_set_bits, emits AgentEvent::CommandDataUpdated — real dispatch
- /Users/jon/src/ffxi/ffxi-client/src/state.rs:783 — CommandDataUpdated handler populates the three *_known fields; wire_translate.rs:49-51 copies them into SceneSnapshot
Adversarial review: confirmed the change. Independently traced the full chain: decode.rs:1116 CommandData::decode + collect_set_bits -> session.rs:1069 emits CommandDataUpdated -> state.rs:783 populates the three *_known fields -> wire_translate.rs:49-51 into SceneSnapshot -> menu.rs:324 refresh_dynamic_menu_rows builds real rows via table-backed ability_names::lookup (>=400 entries). Confirmed MenuKind::Abilities is is_dynamic, so the renderer (menu.rs:510/entry_label:163) never reaches the "(Abilities — data pending)" static string at line 208 (empty hint is "no abilities available"), and confirm_menu_at_cursor (text_input.rs:1933) short-circuits on is_dynamic before resolve_menu_entry, making the NotImplemented arm at 1833 dead code; selecting a row dispatches a real AgentCommand::Action (JobAbility/Weaponskill/PetAbility) at text_input.rs:2040-2081. The "data pending" caveat is verifiably stale. Minor: the CommandData.traits bitmap (decode.rs:1111) is never surfaced, so literal "job traits" are not shown, but the line's named-and-wired deliverable (the abilities list) is fully implemented.

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (vanilla parity, character-progression).

title: Magic spell list (learned spells)
description:

Fix: consume the open key so it isn't re-processed as a page flip on the opening frame (OpenMenu path in view_native/input.rs + handle_menu_key in text_input.rs). Minor; the single-pane two-page rework itself is verified correct (kuluu-v4pd, commit 9c0af00).

title: Parse item activation/cast time from DAT to complete retail recast bracket [max_recast, use_time]
description: Follow-up to kuluu-ng3o. The retail item tooltip recast line is <cur/max HH:MM:SS/[max_recast, use_time]> e.g. <1/1 22:55:57/[24:00:00, 0:30]>. kuluu-ng3o shipped everything except the second bracket value (use/cast time), rendering [24:00:00] alone, because ffxi-dat/src/item_dat.rs does not parse the usable-item activation field. LEAD: in item_dat.rs the equipment-block parse skips a byte right after max_charges (the 'off += 1' before reuse_delay at ~line 100) — that skipped byte is a strong candidate for castTime/activation (u8). Verify the offset + units against POLUtils ItemEquipment layout / vendor item_usable activation (itemutils.cpp:346), then thread activation_secs through ItemStatic -> item_meta -> format_charge_line as the [.. , M:SS] element. Low risk once the offset+units are confirmed; do NOT ship a guessed magic offset.

title: Retail-faithful Vanilla water: replace synthetic flat plane with the real 0x8000 textured-blend water mesh
description: Water now renders (was hidden by zone_geom_mode gating; fixed in dat_mzb.rs by making WaterPlane always Visibility::Inherited and excluding it from apply_zone_geom_visibility). But the Vanilla surface is a stand-in: a flat plane synthesized from MZB placement water_height, drawn with a procedural sine-ripple texture and a fixed teal WATER_TINT (dat_mzb.rs simple_water_material/water_ripple_image). Retail draws the actual zone-mesh water geometry with the 0x8000 blend flag (ZoneMeshSection.kt:79 blendEnabled = flags & 0x8000) and a real scrolling water DXT texture, per research/xim. Blocker: the retail water texture set (MMB 0x8000 section) isn't parsed yet — see MEMORY.md water-rendering-architecture. Deliver: parse the water texture, drive UV scroll from the real TextureCoordinateUpdater, drop the procedural ripple/flat tint. Evidence baseline: artifacts/verify/water-fix-windurst-*.png.
