title: Retail key-layout parity: arrows move the character (not camera), numpad4/6/8/2 camera orbit/tilt, numpad5 first-person toggle
description: Retail observation 2026-07-19 (HorizonXI): left/right ARROWS rotate the character exactly like A/D (tank turn) — arrows are movement keys; camera orbit is numpad4/6 (camera-only, character does not turn); numpad5 toggles first person (model hidden; A turns view in FP). numpad8/2 produced no visible pitch in a Mog House interior — inconclusive, retest outdoors. Kuluu binds arrows to camera yaw/pitch (presets.rs:108-111), V for first-person (presets.rs:62), no numpad handling at all. Also: strafe actions exist but are unbound in every preset (presets.rs:177-223) — retail has no unlocked strafe either, so consider dropping or lock-on-gating them rather than binding. Decide preset shape: a retail-faithful preset (arrows=move, numpad=camera, numpad5=FP) alongside the modern WASD presets; per-action rebind UI is a prerequisite gap (only /keybinds preset|reset exists, text_input.rs:1847-1875).

title: Mog House lamp luminance ordering anomaly: Off measured brighter than Vanilla in lamp crop
description: During kuluu-042y verification (2026-07-19) the lamp-region luminance ordering measured Enhanced(124.7) > Off(83.0) > Vanilla(47.3) on wider crops — Off showing more glow than Vanilla is unexpected (faithful Generator lamp should light in Vanilla, nothing in Off). Caveat: lights-off/vanilla PNGs came from an earlier session (char Kfxveri) and lights-enhanced from a later one (Verilamp), so exposure/time-of-day/comparability is suspect; that earlier session's visual read was the opposite (Vanilla glow present, Off flat). Re-verify all three modes in ONE session with fixed camera, then check zone_point_lights Off-gating and any ambient fallback that might brighten when ActiveSceneLights is empty.

title: Entity-stream NPC name labels shifted (name_extraction_miss): wrong names attached to nearby NPC ids
description: Observed live 2026-07-19 (kuluu-76z verify, zone 230): 37 name_extraction_miss events; id 17719636 labelled 'Rolandienne' is actually Voidwatch_Purveyor per vendor LSB npc_list.sql; the real Rolandienne 17719638 was labelled 'A.M.A.N. Reclaimer'. Names appear shifted across adjacent ids — likely an off-by-one/misalignment in NPC-name resolution (DAT name table vs act_index/id mapping). Misleads targeting for agents and players. Evidence: artifacts/verify/kuluu-76z/events-phase1.jsonl.

title: Event watchdog force-ends open VM dialogs in user-driven mode (drains pending_event_end but not dialog_session)
description: Observed live during kuluu-76z verification (artifacts/verify/kuluu-76z/events-phase3.jsonl): with user_driven_events=true, the 10s PENDING_EVENT_END_GRACE watchdog auto-flushed a pending EVENT_END while the player still had the dialog open, and it drains pending_event_end but NOT dialog_session — a later Esc then sends a stale 0x05B that LSB rejects ('Not in an event', ValidatedPacketHandler:220). Harmless to session health but a GUI dialog left open >10s gets force-ended under the player. Fix: suspend the grace timer while dialog_session is user-driven-open, or clear dialog_session together with the flush.

title: Dev-stack era skew: LSB text ids vs HorizonXI-era client DATs (TALKNUMWORK ids ≥~6425 off by varying amounts)
description: Live verify of the 0x02A TALKNUMWORK chat path showed zone 230 !addkeyitem rendering 'Lost key item: X.' instead of 'Obtained key item: X.': the vendored/live LSB pin (c39004ce, Apr 2026) uses text ids synced to a NEWER retail client than the installed HorizonXI-era DATs (LSB b3af49c62ae2 2023-05-25 matches this install byte-for-byte: zone 230 KEYITEM_OBTAINED=6437 physical, CONQUEST_BASE=7062, TRICK_OR_TREAT=7385; modern LSB says 6438/7071/7394 — SE inserted entries, offset varies by range so no constant base reconciles). Client indexing is identity-correct and must NOT be patched with an offset. Remediation is environmental: either era-match the dev LSB pin to the install's client era or update vendor/game-files DATs to LSB's target client. Until then every TALKNUMWORK id ≥ ~6425 mis-texts by a range-dependent amount on the dev stack (retail-era clients would show the same). Evidence: artifacts/verify/parity3-events.jsonl; test anchors: ffxi-dat dmsg real_zone230_keyitem_obtained_decodes_marker (entry 6437), ffxi-client session talknumwork_composes_real_keyitem_line_from_zone_dat.

title: Build-script scrape counts: add zero/low-count sanity signal (canary) now that counts are quiet
description: Follow-up to kuluu-d33k: the 'scraped N entries' lines were demoted from cargo:warning to plain build-script stdout, which removed their incidental canary value (a fresh build no longer surfaces suspiciously low or zero scrape counts). Add explicit validation in the ffxi-dat/ffxi-proto/ffxi-audio/ffxi-nav build scripts: assert!(n > 0) — or emit a real cargo:warning — when a scrape yields zero or suspiciously few entries (e.g. vendor submodule checked out at wrong rev, format drift in vendored SQL/XML sources). Keep normal success output on plain stdout.

title: weather.rs: global ClearColor written by weather fog with no reset path for weatherless zones
description: Code review nit from the kuluu-ehye/weather session: weather.rs now writes ResMut<ClearColor> as a horizon backdrop when fog is active, but zones/weather states without fog never restore the previous ClearColor, so a stale horizon color can leak across zone lines or weather transitions. Fix: capture/restore a default, or write ClearColor every frame from current weather state including the 'no weather' branch. Verify with a zone-line crossing from a foggy zone to a clear zone.
close_reason: apply_zone_weather previously wrote ClearColor only inside the volumetric-fog-with-record branch and early-returned when zone_weather.current was None, so a painted fog horizon leaked across zone lines into weatherless zones and survived the volumetric-fog toggle. Fix: added a DefaultClearColor resource that captures the app's startup backdrop in PreStartup, extracted a pure zone_clear_color(rec, volumetric_fog, daylight_smooth, default) selection function, and restructured apply_zone_weather to compute and write the desired ClearColor every frame BEFORE the no-record early return (change-detection-guarded write). Volumetric+record paints the daylight-scaled zone fog color as before; every other state (no record, wasm, DistanceFog mode) restores the captured startup default.

title: Build-output noise: demote 'scraped N entries' info from cargo:warning; fix clang -ffp-model/-ffp-contract override in vendored recastnavigation-rs
description: (1) ffxi-dat/ffxi-proto/ffxi-audio/ffxi-nav build.rs emit success telemetry ('scraped N entries', 'generated X with N zones') via cargo:warning=, rendering as warnings on every fresh build. Move to plain build-script stdout (visible with -vv); keep genuine skip/failure messages as cargo:warning. (2) vendor/recastnavigation-rs/build.rs passes -ffp-model=precise then -ffp-contract=off; clang emits -Woverriding-option once per TU because precise implies contract=on. clang's default model is already precise, so drop the redundant model flag — identical FP semantics, no warning.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 1824dd6 on worktree-build-warnings-cleanup: informational scrape counts demoted from cargo:warning to plain build-script stdout; redundant -ffp-model=precise dropped in vendored recastnavigation-rs, eliminating -Woverriding-option spam. Follow-up count-sanity check tracked in kuluu-m4yk.

title: Drop [patch.crates-io] git pins when bevy_water/bevy_framepace publish 0.19 releases
description: Pinned revs: bevy_water gameartel@0da726338576cca487e1642d2403442573e2c276 (PR Neopallium/bevy_water#45), bevy_framepace noprobelm@a9b8eb256dd3bbe746fda06fad77790bb3143ba7 (PR aevyrie/bevy_framepace#78). When upstream releases land on crates.io, remove patches and bump version reqs.

title: MH interior: giant glowing green 'T' + ring artifact fills the room
description: In the Mog House interior (S. San d'Oria model 289, DAT 389), a huge green bloom-heavy 'T' shape hovers mid-room floor-to-ceiling and a giant green ring arc clips the bottom-right of the screen (screenshots 2026-07-14, /tmp/mog-01-login.png). Suspect the client-synthesized exit-door zone-line marker or its 'Door: To Town' nameplate rendering at a broken scale in the interior. Only observed inside the MH.

title: Delivery Box: 0x04D PBX protocol (send/receive items via Moogle)
description: The Mog Menu Delivery Box row is a stub. Retail delivery box speaks c2s/s2c 0x04D (vendor/server/src/map/packets/c2s/0x04d_pbx.cpp) — a multi-action sub-protocol (open box, list slots, take, send, confirm). Needs its own decode/encode + UI. Split out of kuluu-6a0 so storage-container work can close independently.
notes: RETAIL PIXEL REFERENCE for grid HUD captured 2026-07-19: artifacts/retail/20260719-142746.png — Delivery Box panel: 2x4 slot grid (slot 1 highlighted), item list incl. Pet Fd. Gamma, Take/Drop/Return buttons, 'Current Gil 80,146 G' bar, help bar 'Select an item from the delivery box.' Esc from grid returns to Receive/Send submenu (142835).

title: Mog House exit door menu: retail Where-to? UI (district choice, floor change, Mog Garden) over the existing 0x05E zmrq path
description:
Retail: interacting with the Exit door inside the Mog House opens a menu — stay in your room / area you entered from / change floors (when 2F unlocked) / pick a city district — and the choice maps to the (MyRoomExitBit, MyRoomExitMode) pair in 0x05E. Kuluu can already send every one of those exits, but only via the /mhexit slash command with a hand-typed destination; the retail menu UI does not exist.

LSB (authoritative):
- Exit fourcc "zmrq" in the 0x05E maprect handler: vendor/server/src/map/packets/c2s/0x05e_maprect.cpp:72, branch :83.
- MyRoomExitMode enum: AreaEnteredFrom=0, Option1..Option4=1..4, Mog2F=125, Mog1F=126, MogGarden=127 (src/map/packets/c2s/0x05e_maprect.h:40-50); MyRoomExitBit region enum SandOria=1..Adoulin=9 (:26-38).
- Destination = mode + region base zone (0x05e_maprect.cpp:96-123, e.g. San d'Oria Option1/2/3 -> Southern/Northern/Port :99); exit legitimacy validated against profile.mhflag quest bits (:134-193); floor toggle requires 2F unlocked (mhflag & 0x20, :171-186).
- Which districts the client menu may offer is advertised server-side in 0x00A MyRoomExitBit via GetMogHouseLeavingFlag (src/map/packets/s2c/0x00a_login.cpp:74-115, assigned :160); being on 2F forces the full menu via MyroomSubMapNumber=0x02 (:199-203).
- Post-exit door placement: moghouseZoneLines + exits coordinate tables (scripts/globals/moghouse.lua:74-135; Bastok Markets has two exit doors :98-106).

XIM (client-side reference): hand-coded query menu "Where to?" with exactly the retail options Stay / Area you entered from / Change floors / Select an area (research/xim src/jsMain/kotlin/xim/poc/game/configuration/assetviewer/MogHouseDoorInteraction.kt:53-91), floor selection :93-122, exit-area map :25-49, door-open animation _720/open from the zone DAT :148-153; dispatched off an NPC named "Door: To Town" (AssetViewer.kt:120-122).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-09): slash-only. /mhexit parsing ffxi-client/src/view_native/slash_commands.rs:1672-1712; AgentCommand::MogHouseExit (state.rs:1212-1214); the MogHouseExit enum + wire_pair() already match LSB's bit/mode values exactly (state.rs:1406-1443); 0x05E zmrq builder session.rs:3501-3521. No door interaction, no menu, and no district gating (MyRoomExitBit is not decoded — see kuluu-pnfc).

Scope: targetable Exit door inside the MH; retail menu with district options filtered by the decoded MyRoomExitBit; floor-change entries when 2F is unlocked (+ Mog Garden entry); drive the existing MogHouseExit command; door-open animation nicety optional.
notes: Research (2026-07-10): LSB spawns NO door NPC in the MH (only the conditional Moogle, zone_entities.cpp:1162-1218) — the exit door must be a client-synthesized targetable entity (XIM does the same: 'Door: To Town' at FFXI-native (0,-1,-8), AssetViewer.kt:654-671). Menu built on the existing DialogState pipeline via a session-side LocalMenuSession (headless parity for free). District gating: MyRoomExitBit from 0x00A is a single city code 0/1-5/9 (GetMogHouseLeavingFlag). 2F gating: mhflag&0x20 crosses ONLY as byte 0x27 of 0x067 char-sync (char_sync.cpp:61); on-2F itself forces the full menu (MyroomSubMapNumber==2). Server rejections of 0x05E are silent log-only drops — surface via pending_maprect watchdog. Terminal rows must map through the existing MogHouseExit::wire_pair (verified matching LSB enums; Mog2F=(0,125), Mog1F=(0,126), MogGarden=(0,127), Home=(1,0)).
close_reason: Exit door 'Where to?' menu landed: district rows per MyRoomExitBit, floor change, Home echoes exit_bit; door named 'Door: To Town' per XIM; wire pairs pinned against LSB 0x05e_maprect

title: Zone-teardown despawn flood: guard recursive double-despawns with try_despawn
description: During zone teardown, renderer teardown systems call bare commands.entity(e).despawn() on entities Bevy already freed as descendants of a recursively-despawned parent (index reused -> 'generation 2'). Produces thousands of 'Entity ... is invalid; its index now has generation 2' WARN lines per zone-in from bevy_ecs::error::handler. Harmless (Bevy logs and continues) but noisy + adds error-handler overhead to the teardown frame. Fix: swap the bare .despawn() at renderer teardown sites for the fallible try_despawn() (audio.rs:938 already does this). Adjacent to the zone-change-not-clean-lifecycle behavior.
close_reason: despawn_ingame_entities now uses commands.entity(entity).try_despawn() so recursively-despawned children freed by a parent earlier in the query no longer flood bevy_ecs error-handler WARNs during zone teardown. Verified by new unit test zone_teardown_tests::teardown_tolerates_recursively_freed_children_without_warns (parent+child both InGameEntity; counts error-handler warns via BevyEcsWarnCounter): fails with bare despawn(), passes with try_despawn. cargo test -p ffxi-client zone_teardown --features native-window green; scripts/checks.sh fmt clippy green. Live visual confirm pending.

title: Event/status packet paths swallow diagnostics (pre-existing, surfaced by PR #111 review)
description:
Three pre-existing silent-failure gaps found while reviewing PR #111 (not introduced there, not fixed by the stacked follow-up PR alexcstark/kuluu-ffxi#1):

1. event_dialog.rs load_event_dat/load_strings collapse missing-file, IO error, and parse error into None (.ok()?). With auto-release in place, a bad FFXI_DAT_PATH now auto-skips EVERY cutscene in a zone with 'not yet supported' chat lines, indistinguishable from an unimplemented opcode. Log once per zone on load failure.

2. ffxi-event vm.rs:154-157: exec_pointer running past the end of bytecode reports a clean Done, indistinguishable from OP_END — exactly the failure mode that hides a wrong opcode skip size. A distinct StepResult (or debug log) for 'ran past end without END' would make size bugs discoverable. Same for eventgetcode's unwrap_or(0) OOB reads.

3. session.rs handle_sub_packet: every 'if let Ok(x) = decode::X::decode(...)' silently drops decode errors; CHAR_STATUS now also loses the speed sync when it fires. A dedup'd tracing::warn on the Err arm would cost nothing.
close_reason: (1) event_dialog.rs: DAT load failures now log once per zone with the resolved FFXI_DAT_PATH file path and the underlying error (dedup'd per zone id). (2) vm.rs: added a distinct StepResult::RanPastEnd path when the exec pointer runs past the end of bytecode without an explicit OP_END, logged separately from a real END; eventgetcode out-of-bounds reads (the unwrap_or(0) sites around lines 283-299) now emit a debug log with pc/index before falling back. (3) session.rs: all 23 'if let Ok(...)' decode arms in handle_sub_packet (22 decode::* plus the from_utf8 arm) now route the Err through .inspect_err into a new warn_decode_err(opcode, err) helper that warns once per opcode (process-global OnceLock<Mutex<HashSet<u16>>> gate) and logs repeats at DEBUG, so dropped packets are no longer silent.

title: Config: Misc display toggles (damage display, PC model cap, icon type, armor static, clipping plane, footsteps, animation rate, aspect ratio, macro palette size/position, weapon effects, style lock, target expressions, party icons, timer display, furniture camera collision)
description:
Retail Config > Misc 1-3 display/behavior toggles. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=2644 (44:04-48:20):
- Damage display: log / on-screen / both (t=2644)
- Character models displayed: cap on other-PC models drawn, players "blink out" beyond it (t=2674)
- Icon type old/new; PC armor display normal/static (t=2696)
- Clipping plane (draw distance; low values darken world without max gamma) (t=2718)
- Footstep effects; animation rate (low rate degrades other players' anim FPS) (t=2729)
- Background aspect ratio (t=2777); movement key assignment per-key rebinding GUI (t=2789)
- Macro palette size + position dynamic/static (t=2804)
- Weapon effects (glow) toggle; style lock (t=2833)
- Target expressions, party icon display, timer display (t=2874)
- Furniture camera collision (t=2880)

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- Draw distance partial: /drawdistance //dd setworld/setmob (slash_commands.rs:745-750, DrawDistanceOp :988-993; DrawDistance resource in dat_mzb) + GraphicsField::ViewDistance (settings.rs:944) feeding camera far plane (camera.rs:228-232). No PC-count model cap.
- Keybinding: presets only (presets.rs, /keybinds slash_commands.rs:704-708); no per-key GUI.
- Everything else in the list: MISSING as config. Note camera_collision.rs is chase-cam-vs-MZB-geometry, not furniture.
- Area Display aura is split out: kuluu-aeoa. Macro palette itself: kuluu-mco.

title: Config: Effects tab — screen shake + animation visibility filters (non-party PC/party/monster-vs-monster)
description:
Retail Config > Effects: screen-shake toggle and per-source animation visibility filters — hide non-party-PC-vs-monster, party-vs-monster, and monster-vs-monster action animations to keep large fights stable/readable. Retail gotcha to preserve (video 43:32): turning monster-vs-monster animations OFF hides proc indicators (blue/white !! procs), so the guide insists that one stays ON — surface that warning in the UI or default. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=2567 (42:47-44:04).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05): MISSING — no screen-shake toggle, no animation visibility filters anywhere in viewer-core/client settings.

title: Config: Mouse & Camera tab (mouse control mode, screen-edge panning, chase cam mode, invert 3rd-person camera)
description:
Retail Config > Mouse & Camera: mouse movement-control mode (follow character vs cursor), screen-edge panning on/off, camera Normal vs Chase mode, invert third-person camera axes. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=2524 (42:04-42:47).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- Mouse control mode + edge panning: MISSING (mouse.rs is drag-based only).
- Chase mode: CameraAutoRecenter auto-tails in Chase (input.rs:943-1004) but there is no user toggle between normal/chase.
- Invert: MISSING — pitch/yaw signs hardcoded (input.rs:418-442, mouse.rs; no invert flag in camera.rs).
notes: Retail Mouse/Cam page transcribed verbatim 2026-07-19 (HorizonXI, artifacts/retail/20260719-195735.png / 195846 / mousecam_crop2.png): 7 rows, cyclic — 1) Mouse Control: Type 1/Type 2 ('Use cursor as point of reference for movement.') 2) Screen Edge Panning: ON/OFF ('The camera will rotate when the mouse pointer is moved to the edge of the screen.') 3) Camera View: Normal/Chase Cam ('Allows unrestricted movement of the third-person camera.') 4) Third-Person Camera Y Axis: Normal/Inverted 5) Third-Person Camera X Axis: Normal/Inverted 6) First-Person Camera Y Axis: Normal/Inverted 7) First-Person Camera X Axis: Normal/Inverted. So FOUR inversion toggles (3P/1P x X/Y), not just 3rd-person. Kuluu currently has zero inversion/sensitivity config: mouse.rs:9-13 hardcodes MOUSE_YAW_SENS/MOUSE_PITCH_SENS 0.005, pitch fixed -delta.y (mouse.rs:121-123); no settings surface (launcher settings.rs edits only DAT path/navmesh/MAC).

title: Config: Gameplay tab (auto-target on engage, BGM/SFX volume levels, gamma, inventory sort) + System/Global toggles
description:
Retail Config > Gameplay: auto-target lock when drawing weapon, sound-effect and music volume sliders, gamma, inventory sort on/off + sort type (1 = alphabetical-ish, 2 = acquisition order). Also scope the small System tab (system-message toggles, calls for help) and Global tab (chat profanity filter, auto-disconnect idle timer). https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=2247 (37:27-38:05), t=2479 (41:19 System), t=2495 (41:35 Global).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- Auto-target config: MISSING (Tab-cycle target acquire exists, input.rs:178-208, but no engage-lock setting).
- Volume: /sound bgm|sfx is mute-toggle only (slash_commands.rs:593-598, SoundOp :1024-1033); no levels. Gamma: MISSING.
- Inventory auto-sort: kuluu-z94 (related) — this bead provides the tab it lives in.
- Auto-disconnect / profanity filter / system-message toggles: MISSING.

title: EPIC: Retail Config menu — tabbed taxonomy (Gameplay / Chat Filters / Font Colors / Windows / Log / System / Global / Mouse & Camera / Effects / Misc 1-4)
description:
Retail's Config menu is a tabbed settings tree; ours currently contains only keybind presets (CONFIG_ENTRIES = Standard/Compact 1/Compact 2/Reset/Show bindings — ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/menu.rs:91-97), with graphics in a separate Graphics submenu (settings.rs:934-961). This epic tracks rebuilding the retail taxonomy and its settings surface, persisted across sessions.

Source: "FFXI User Interface Guide Pt1 - Vanilla Interface" https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=2247 (37:19-48:20 walks every tab).

Retail tabs and where each is tracked:
- Gameplay (auto-target, volumes, gamma, inventory sort) — child bead
- Chat Filters + Font Colors — kuluu-80u
- Windows (split/timestamps/style) + per-window sizing + Log routing — kuluu-r8p
- Window Effect (menu open/close animation on/off; retail's is slow, most players disable) — small line item, fold into whichever child touches menu chrome
- System (system-message toggles, calls for help) + Global (chat language profanity filter, auto-disconnect timer) — line items, scope with Gameplay child
- Mouse & Camera — child bead
- Effects (screen shake + animation visibility filters) — child bead
- Misc 1-4 (display toggles) — child bead; Area Display aura split out as kuluu-aeoa
- Movement key assignment: per-key rebinding GUI (presets + /keybinds exist: slash_commands.rs:704-708, presets.rs) — line item under Misc child
- Explicitly out of scope: software keyboard (Misc 4), keyboard-size layouts beyond the existing presets.
Related: kuluu-z94 (inventory Auto-Sort setting) becomes part of the Gameplay tab.

title: Area display: blue action-target aura (Config Misc 3, off by default in retail)
description:
Retail "Area Display" (Config > Misc 3, off by default) draws a blue aura ring around the entity your pending action will actually hit — enemy or friend — so misdirected actions are visible before they land. The guide strongly recommends it, so treat it as a default-worthy vanilla feature behind the config toggle. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=2856 (47:36-47:54).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05): no target-aura rendering exists; target selection/picking exists in the viewer but no action-target ring. Config surface tracked by the Retail Config menu epic.

title: Logout menu: separate Log Out / Shut Down entries; instant logout in Mog House and safe areas
description:
Retail main menu has distinct Log Out and Shut Down entries; logging out takes 30 seconds except in your Mog House (and equivalent safe areas) where it is instant. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=466 (7:46-8:05) and t=2102 (35:02 menu entries).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- 30s countdown exists and is server-anchored: ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/logout_countdown.rs:36 (OPTIMISTIC_TOTAL_SECS=30), banner :75-282; 0x053 SYSTEMMES leavegame ticks session.rs:963-968.
- /logout and /shutdown are separate slash commands (slash_commands.rs:647-659; ReqLogoutKind mapping text_input.rs:1817-1841) but the in-game menu has ONE root entry "Logout" (menu.rs:20) → ReqLogoutKind::LogoutToggle (text_input.rs:1922-1929).
- Instant-in-Mog-House isn't reflected client-side: the toast claims it (text_input.rs:1926) but the optimistic timer always shows 30s; mog-house state is tracked (session.rs:985-995 note_mog_transition) yet not wired to countdown length.
Related: kuluu-uu1 (/logout should return to character select).
close_reason: Split root menu Logout into 'Log Out' (LogoutToggle) and 'Shut Down' (ShutdownToggle) entries via pub consts in hud/menu.rs; optimistic countdown now carries total_secs and uses MOG_HOUSE_TOTAL_SECS=0 when SceneSnapshot::self_in_mog_house() (mirrors server leavegame.lua onEffectGain instant disconnect), so Mog House logout shows LoggingOut instantly. Verified: 7 logout_countdown unit tests (incl. new mog_house_total_shows_logging_out_immediately), 3 menu_dispatch tests (incl. new shut_down_dispatches_shutdown_reqlogout_with_toast), new ffxi-viewer-wire self_in_mog_house_mirrors_producer_logic test; scripts/checks.sh fmt clippy green. Live visual confirm pending.

title: Auto-run retail semantics: toggle from standstill
description:
Retail auto-run (L1) engages from a standstill — press it and you start running until you hit a wall, steerable while active. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=342 (5:42-6:03).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- AutoRun exists (view_native/input.rs:49-53) and is steerable (turn_in_chase input.rs:559,647-685), with sensible cancels (backpedal :502-504, strafe/rotate>=300ms :540-553, zone change :74, death :460-461, rest :488-489).
- GAP: Action::ToggleAutorun only engages if MoveForward is held at the moment of toggle (input.rs:235-239 `&& bindings.pressed(Action::MoveForward…)`); retail toggles from standstill.
close_reason: Dropped the MoveForward-held gate on Action::ToggleAutorun in view_native/input.rs: toggle now flips phantom_forward from a standstill, matching retail L1 auto-run. Extracted pure helper autorun_after_toggle() and added 3 unit tests (engage-from-standstill, disengage-when-active, unchanged-without-press); existing steer/cancel paths (backpedal, strafe/rotate>=300ms, chat/dialog, death, rest) untouched. Verified: cargo test -p ffxi-client --features native-window view_native::input (32 passed) and scripts/checks.sh fmt clippy green. Live visual confirm pending.

title: Alliance-aware party list (3x6 columns) + leader dots (party list + nameplates)
description:
Retail party list scales from 6-member HP/MP rows to full 18-member alliance display (3 columns), and marks the party leader with a yellow dot next to the name (two dots = alliance leader). https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=2900 (48:20-48:44 party/alliance HP-MP display) and t=3003 (50:03 leader dot).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- Roster widget renders one flat row list: ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/roster.rs:98-122; no alliance partitioning — party is a single Vec<PartyMember> (state.rs:279; wire lib.rs:337).
- Leader flags ARE decoded (is_party_leader/is_alliance_leader, ffxi-proto decode.rs:1006-1007; wire PartyMember lib.rs:277) but never rendered — format_header (roster.rs:245-255) ignores them; no leader logic in nameplate.rs/nameplate_billboard.rs.
- Cosmetic divergence while here: roster adds a TP bar per member (roster.rs:179,211); retail shows HP/MP only.

title: HUD-hide toggle + screenshot keybinding (retail: Select hides UI, Select+R1 screenshots)
description:
Retail: a button hides the entire UI (look around / clean view), and pressing the screenshot combo while UI is hidden captures a clean shot. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=363 (6:03-6:45).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- UI-hide: MISSING entirely — no Action, resource, or system (grep for hud-hide/toggle-ui/ui_hidden clean).
- Screenshot: exists as /screenshot //ss slash only (view_native/slash_commands.rs:739-744 → ScreenshotRequest → Bevy Screenshot::primary_window, view_native/screenshot.rs:13-28). No keybind Action in ffxi-viewer-core/src/keybinds/mod.rs:14-79.
Implement: Action::ToggleHud (hide all HUD root nodes), Action::Screenshot, and compose them for the clean-shot flow.
notes:
2026-07-19: IMPLEMENTED + MERGED (commit 95d8886, merged to main): Action::ToggleHud (ScrollLock) + Action::Screenshot (PrintScreen) in all presets; hud_hide.rs apply_hud_hidden hides all root UI Nodes (stash/restore exact Visibility, HudHideExempt carve-outs for nameplate root + loading overlay), gated off Chat input mode; screenshot keybind reuses ScreenshotRequest via screenshot.rs::next_default_path. 431 viewer-core tests pass. Retail oracle recorded: Scroll Lock full-UI hide, nameplates persist (artifacts/retail/20260719-1444*.png).
REMAINS before close: GUI pixel verification — blocked 2026-07-19 by locked macOS console (screencapture black, keystrokes undeliverable). Ready-to-run recipe: throwaway char Konalpha/account konchka validated headless from merged main (artifacts/verify/yo5-headless-*); once console unlocked: launch native play, capture, key code 107 (ScrollLock) hide/restore, key code 105 (PrintScreen) -> screenshot-N.png, W-move while hidden. Also open: retail PC screenshot key unverified; /screenshot counter unification bead filed.

title: Config: chat message filters (+ hold for priority channels) and per-channel font colors
description:
Two retail Config sections for chat presentation:
1) Chat Filters — suppress message categories (e.g. "special action started by others", others' effect lines) to cut spam in zerg fights; a per-channel "hold" option pins tell/party/linkshell/assist messages at the top until seen. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=2285 (38:05-38:52).
2) Font Colors — per-channel color pickers with live preview at the top of the screen and reset-to-default. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=2332 (38:52-39:38).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- No filter-config UI, no suppression toggles, no hold pinning. In-game Config submenu is keybind presets only: ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/menu.rs:91-97 (CONFIG_ENTRIES).
- Per-channel colors EXIST but are hardcoded in channel_color(): ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/chat_panel.rs:487-502; autotranslate brace segments colored separately chat_panel.rs:156,456-485. No config resource, preview, or reset.

title: Full-screen chat log view with per-channel filter (backscroll)
description:
Retail: pressing confirm on the chat bar raises the log to full screen for backscrolling, and the view can be filtered to a single channel (e.g. shouts only, to find an old shout): https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=104 (1:44) and t=182 (3:02-3:18).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- Per-tab mouse-wheel backscroll only: ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/chat_panel.rs:528-582 (chat_wheel_scroll_system), clamped in apply_wheel_delta chat_panel.rs:504-526.
- No full-screen log mode; filtering is only the 3 fixed tab buckets (ChatKind::accepts chat_panel.rs:125-136) — cannot filter to a single channel.
- Buffer capped at 256 lines: ffxi-client/src/state.rs:280,692-694 — may want a deeper scrollback for this view.

title: Items window type-design + test follow-ups (PR review of kuluu-e8x)
description: From the multi-agent review: (1) model retail's two sort TYPES (alphabetical vs by-type) — SortOptions.auto:bool is under-modeled and 'Manual => !sort.auto' becomes a latent bug when a 3rd mode arrives; prefer enum SortMode (relates to kuluu-3z6). (2) ItemMenuFocus{sort_focused:bool,sort_cursor:usize} keeps its invariant in call sites, not the type; consider enum ItemFocus{List,Sort(SortOptionId)} or private fields + enter/move/exit methods (removes the duplicated wrap arithmetic in text_input.rs + item_screen.rs and the {0}else{1} coupling). (3) an ffxi-proto CONTAINER_ID enum/newtype scraped from LSB (LOC_INVENTORY const added as a start). (4) Highest-value untested logic: the sort-focus keyboard state machine in handle_menu_key (extract the ~40-line block to a pure helper first) and the refresh_dynamic_menu_rows Items-vs-alphabetical branch selection incl. Manual (no-sort) mode.
close_reason: Made the ItemMenuFocus invariant type-level: replaced the (sort_focused: bool, sort_cursor: usize) pair with enum ItemMenuFocus{List, Sort(SortOptionId)} plus sort_focused()/sort_selection()/enter_sort()/exit_sort(), so an out-of-range sort cursor or focus/cursor desync is unrepresentable. Extracted the ~40-line sort-focus keyboard block from item_screen.rs handle_menu_key into a pure helper (SortPaneKey + sort_pane_key in item_detail.rs) that steps/wraps SORT_OPTIONS, applies on Confirm via apply_sort_option, exits on Exit/NavLeft, and swallows unbound keys. Also extracted the refresh_dynamic_menu_rows ordering branch into a pure order_dynamic_rows(kind, auto_sort, rows) helper in menu.rs. select_active_window now takes &SortOptions so keyboard entry lands the cursor on the active sort mode. text_input.rs turned out not to need changes; the wrap arithmetic there was untouched by this refactor.

title: Small ~28ms frame blip exactly every 10.0s (cpu-heavy + periodic 1MB image re-upload)
description: Post kuluu-996 fix, perf logs show a residual single-frame ~28ms spike at an exact 10.00s cadence: cpu 11-17ms + rprep up to 17ms, churn img+1 (1024KB) in nearby windows (512x512 RGBA = 1MB — likely the retail minimap image being re-baked/re-uploaded), std+4 on spike frames. Repro: run instrumented client (perf summary/spike lines in view_native/perf_hud.rs, FFXI_GPU_TIMING=1), stand idle in Port_Bastok. Find the exact 10s timer (client or LSB-driven), then either make the image update incremental/GPU-side or skip re-upload when content unchanged. Also worth chasing the constant std+~600/5s StandardMaterial Modified churn (~2/frame, sun/moon disc or water suspects) — constant small bind-group rebuild load, not a hitch.

title: Add inventory Auto-Sort setting to Config menu (Game Settings)
description: Retail exposes auto-sort in Config > Game Settings with two types: Type 1 = alphabetical, Type 2 = by item-type + auto-stack. We currently expose the Auto/Manual toggle only in the Items-window Sort box (SortOptions.auto in ffxi-viewer-core::hud::item_detail). Surface it in the Config menu too, and consider modelling the two retail sort types (alpha vs by-type) rather than a bool. Ref: https://nocturnalsouls.net/__arch_i_ve_d/getting-setup/tweaking-the-ffxi-ui/

title: Resolve non-param dialog markers: {PlayerName}/{SpeakerName} substitution + strip {Element}(Set Color) formatting
description: clean_display leaks {PlayerName} (0x08) and {SpeakerName} (0x09) into rendered NPC speech; both are resolvable now without the message param array — player char name is in session state, speaker name is frame.speaker_index -> entity name. Also 0x1e is Set Color per POLUtils (a formatting/color code), not visible text, so it should be stripped like {Auto} rather than shown as {Element:N}. Thread the two names into the render layer and strip the color code. Param-driven markers are kuluu-6zy.
close_reason: {PlayerName}/{SpeakerName} substituted in event_dialog::frame_to_dialog (player name threaded through DialogSession; speaker from active.npc_name). {SetColor}(0x1E) stripped as formatting in clean_display. POLUtils naming corrected in dmsg: 0x1a=KeyItem, 0x1c=ChocoboName, 0x1e=SetColor. Single source of truth via dmsg::plain_marker + prefix consts, guarded by render_markers_match_emitted_output.

title: Thread event-message parameter array through the VM for {Num:N}/{Item:N}/{KeyItem:N}/{ChocoboName:N} substitution
description: EventMessage only carries message_id; the numeric/item/choice parameters the dialog control codes reference (0x0A Num, 0x0C Choice, 0x19 Item, 0x1A KeyItem) are never extracted, so {Num:N}/{Item:N}/{KeyItem:N} markers leak and {Choice:N} defaults to alternative 0 (see kuluu-e2v). Extract the message parameter list from the message opcode in ffxi-event vm.rs, carry it on EventMessage/EventChoice, and resolve all param-driven markers in the render layer with real values.
notes: Params threaded vm.rs->runner.rs->event_dialog.rs; {Num:N}/{Choice:N} resolved from params; DialogState.nums populated; begin() passes empty params (0x32 carries none; session.rs left to kuluu-p09 lane). ffxi-event tests 27/27, ffxi-client --lib event_dialog 5/5, clippy clean on both lib targets. ffxi-client bin currently broken by unrelated in-flight ffxi-viewer-core hud changes (text_input.rs vs item_screen/item_detail API), pre-existing.
close_reason: Extracted the event numeric parameter array in ffxi-event/src/vm.rs (EventVm carries params; EventMessage/EventChoice gained a params field populated on OP_MESSAGE and OP_QUERY yields), threaded it through DialogRunner in runner.rs (DialogFrame.params; clean_display/resolve_choice_brackets now select {Choice:N}[a/b/…] alternatives via params[N]), and consumed it in ffxi-client/src/event_dialog.rs where frame_to_dialog now resolves {Num:N} markers to params[N] via a new substitute_nums helper and populates DialogState.nums. begin() passes an empty params array since its sole trigger path is 0x32 (which carries no num[8]); session.rs was deliberately left untouched as it is in kuluu-p09's lane, and the render path is fully driven by DialogFrame.params for future 0x33/0x34 callers. Out-of-range {Num:N} markers are left verbatim so misses stay visible in fixtures.

title: Char-create preview: render naked default body + lay model beside panel (not occluded)
description: Character creation preview showed only the head and was occluded behind the centered create panel. (1) spawn_preview_pc passed raw 0 for every equipment slot to spawn_equipped; resolve_equipment_slot(0,race) returns None (slot-prefix nibble 0), so only the face loaded. Fix: pass slot-prefixed model id 0 (body=0x2000, hands=0x3000, legs=0x4000, feet=0x5000) so the naked default body loads; head/weapons stay 0 so hair shows. (2) Preview model was centered behind the full-width panel. Fix: frame the model into the right of the preview render (camera+look-at slid left by MODEL_SCREEN_SHIFT) and reserve the right MODEL_AREA_PCT of the screen for it (create-screen root right-padding), centering the form panel in the left space. Both percent/perspective based -> responsive.
close_reason: Fixed: naked default body now loads (slot-prefixed model id 0) + model framed to the right of the preview with the form panel reserved to the left. Committed 06fe0cc on main; builds clean (ffxi-client --features native-window), fmt clean.

title: Character creation: split broken Face slider into Face 1-8 + Hair A-B button rows
description: The create-character dialog packed face and hair into one slider (rendered as '10.0' with no real control). Retail picks Face (1-8) and Hair (A-B) as two separate menus. The wire 'face' byte (0-15) encodes both as face_index*2+hair (A=0,B=1), capped at 15='Face 8B' per LSB vendor/server/src/login/login_helpers.cpp:240. Replaced the slider with two spawn_enum_row button rows matching the Race/Job/Nation/Build style, centralizing the byte pack/unpack in CharCreateForm::set_field/field_selection.
close_reason: Implemented: Face 1-8 and Hair A-B button rows replace the slider; verified with cargo check/clippy/fmt

title: Fountains & waterfalls: zone-static particle generators (gravity spray + UV-scroll sheet)
description:
We render no fountains/waterfalls yet. Per research (research/xim) these are zone-static Generator-chunk effects, registered once at zone load (XIM Scene.kt:126 registerEffect(ZoneAssociation)), anchored at the generator base_position — same DAT family that drives braziers/lamp point-lights (ffxi-dat/src/generator.rs). Three render parts:

1. Fountain jet + waterfall splash = particle generator: emits billboard particles (frames_per_emission/particles_per_emission) with initial upward/radial velocity, then a section-3 VelocityAccelerator (gravity) opcode arcs the spray back down. The gravity/velocity-accel sec3 updater is NOT applied yet — tracked in kuluu-8i3. XIM evidence: ParticleDrawer.kt:227 ('needed for Ro'Maeve's fountain effects'); research/xim/src/Notes.txt (Metalworks + N.San d'Oria fountains).

2. Waterfall sheet = textured mesh with scrolling UVs (TextureCoordinateUpdater sec3 0x27/0x28 -> V scroll). Parsing landed: ffxi-dat/src/generator.rs:99-250 (uv_scroll); application at render time (particle_sim.rs) is the remaining gap (kuluu-8i3).

3. Same scrolling-UV mechanism overlaps the river/flat-water surface work (kuluu-980).

Concrete gaps: sec3 VelocityAccelerator (gravity) not applied; UV-scroll parsed but not applied in render; zone-static fountain/waterfall generators not placed/spawned. Blocked on the particle VM sec3/sec4 work (kuluu-8i3). Area: zone-render. Vanilla parity.
notes: 2026-07-21: zone water sprays now resolve MMB meshes by DatId + filtered to water. Bastok abuk/awgd/oti1 + Port Windurst rivs render via particle path (life>0); static sheets via model-spawn path (life==0). Committed. Needs in-client verification (headless has no particle sim).

title: Fishing mini-game native HUD + key bindings
description:
Phase 2 follow-up to kuluu-pyg. The fishing protocol + state machine + self pose are done and the data is exposed in wire::SceneSnapshot.self_fishing { phase, fish_max, fish_hp, arrow }. Remaining GUI work (needs a live LSB server + GUI to validate):

- Draw the fish-HP bar + 'Small/Large Fish' label when self_fishing.arrow-active (research/xim FishHppUi.kt: menu framesus idx 98/103, position ~480,120).
- Draw the on-screen arrow prompt (left/right, golden) during the active reeling state (research/xim FishingArrowAnimation mai0-maib via resource 0xE8F).
- Bind Enter->FishingInput::Hook, Left/Right arrows->FishingInput::Left/Right, movement/Esc->Cancel while self_fishing is active (currently exposed via /hook, /rl, /rr, /reelstop slash commands).
- Optional faithful extra: spawn the fishing-rod model as a child actor in the hand (research/xim FishingStartEvent.kt: MainDll.getBaseFishingRodIndex(race)+rangedModelId, plays the same fsh* clips).

Follow the hud/logout_countdown.rs pattern for a snapshot-driven HUD element.
close_reason: Fishing mini-game native HUD (ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/self_fishing.rs: stamina bar, arrow prompt, phase/status text, key-hint line rendered from live bindings) wired into hud/mod.rs spawn/update sets; modal key bindings (FishingHook/ReelLeft/ReelRight/Cancel, defaults Enter/Left/Right/Escape) added to keybinds + handled in view_native/input.rs, emitting AgentCommand::FishingInput while self_fishing is active. Verified: 7 new self_fishing unit tests + full ffxi-client suite (289+165) green; scripts/checks.sh fmt clippy exit 0. Live visual confirm pending.

title: Simplify config-dir resolution: drop ffxi-mcp legacy migration, adopt dirs::config_dir()
description: Follow-up to kuluu-20v. Nobody is on the new layout yet, so remove the legacy ffxi-mcp->kuluu migration fallbacks entirely (config_dir.rs migrate_* + launcher_store load_from legacy branch). Replace the hand-rolled XDG env parsing (which violated the XDG basedir spec: empty XDG_CONFIG_HOME yielded a relative path instead of $HOME/.config, and relative values weren't ignored) with dirs::config_dir() (already a workspace dep via ffxi-nav-recast). That gives native macOS (~/Library/Application Support) + Windows (%APPDATA%) paths and spec-correct XDG on Linux, with no hand-written platform code.
close_reason: dirs::config_dir() adopted (native macOS/Windows + spec-correct XDG); legacy ffxi-mcp migration removed; clippy+tests green for ffxi-client/ffxi-mcp

title: Docked window-nav button (close/back) on launcher login, server-select, char-select
description: Add a reusable docked window-nav button to the launcher screens: Close (quits app via AppExit) on Login and ServerSelect, Back (to Login) on CharList. Single widget in launcher_ui/common.rs, absolutely positioned top-right with high GlobalZIndex, wired into the three states' OnEnter/OnExit.
close_reason: Added reusable docked window-nav button in launcher_ui/common.rs; Close (AppExit) on Login+ServerSelect, Back→Login on CharList; wired OnEnter/OnExit. Compiles + clippy clean.

title: Rename launcher_store config dir from ffxi-mcp to a player-facing name
description: launcher_store writes settings/credentials under $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ffxi-mcp/launcher.json — a legacy agent name confusing for a player launcher. Migration reads the old path if present and writes the new one, touching both the GUI and the agent harness. Flagged, not silent.
close_reason: Config dir ffxi-mcp->kuluu for launcher + graphics/keybinds/goal stores + agent harness, with flagged migration; merged to main (266b915)

title: Publish SHA256SUMS and verify in the updater
description: Add a SHA256SUMS asset to the dist release and have the updater verify the downloaded artifact before replacing the running binary. Integrity seam usable before code-signing lands; pins the asset-name contract dist must preserve.
close_reason: SHA256SUMS release asset + updater sha256 verify; merged to main (78b5c14)

title: macOS: go universal (lipo x86_64 + arm64)
description: macOS leg is aarch64-only (release.yml:88), excluding Intel users. Add x86_64-apple-darwin to the dist target matrix and lipo into one universal binary before Developer ID signing so a single notarized download covers both arches. Verify x86_64 links under empty RUSTFLAGS.

title: Wire actor texture filtering (mips/anisotropy) to the GUI Texture Filtering setting
description: ffxi_actor_render::decoded_texture_to_image currently hardcodes TextureQuality{mipmaps:true, anisotropy:8}; the GUI Texture Filtering knob (Vanilla/Aniso2-16x) does not apply to characters. Thread GraphicsSettings-derived TextureQuality through the actor spawn path (process_load_actor_requests -> spawn_live_actor -> build_actor_children -> decoded_texture_to_image), as zone textures already do.
close_reason: Texture-filtering setting threaded into actor texture path; merged to main (aa43d00)

title: [bug] Zone lamp point lights pop-in + over-bright base
description: Zone point lights cut from ~29% of peak to 0 when leaving the 4-slot nearest set (player-relative, camera further), popping; and the 1/0.5 const blasted the lamp base. Windowed falloff (1-(d/range)^2)^2 -> exactly 0 at edge (no pop), const 0.5->1.0 softens base. zone_ffxi.wgsl + zone_point_lights.rs.
close_reason: Fixed this session: 35efe13 (mipmap actor textures), 7635184 (ground_nearest), 1576079 (windowed lamp falloff + softened base).

title: Particle VM: shared/global billboard meshes + sec3/sec4 updaters (Phases 4-5)
description: Particle emission VM (kuluu-uvm) renders local-mesh effects. Remaining: (1) some effect DATs reference shared/global billboard meshes (e.g. Boost g004 'hi14', JA impact 'hi22'/'hi16') not stored in the per-action DAT — need a shared-mesh loader; ActionAssets only has local D3m. (2) sec3 updaters beyond scale/alpha (rotation 0x05/0x56-0x58, oscillation 0x29-0x2B, velocity accel) and sec4 child generators (0x44/0x53 ChildGeneratorSetup) not applied. (3) non-flat meshes (maz2=144 verts) instanced+billboarded as if flat quads. (4) velocity units + base_position x/z need live tuning. Evidence: ffxi-viewer-core/src/particle_sim.rs, ffxi-dat/src/particle_gen.rs.
notes: Implemented 2026-07-21: ParticleGeneratorDef sec3 walk (0x27/0x28 uv_scroll, 0x03 accel) + particle_sim tex_translate/accel apply + D3M Repeat sampler. Committed. Tests green. Visual unverified (headless has no particle sim).

title: Render scale: FSR1 (EASU+RCAS) WGSL upscale pass to replace bilinear
description: Follow-up to kuluu-aoc. The render-scale composite currently upscales the off-screen 3D image with a plain bilinear sampler (ImageNode + ImageSampler::linear in ffxi-viewer-core/src/graphics/render_scale.rs). Replace with an FSR1 spatial upscaler (EASU edge-adaptive upsample + RCAS sharpen) as a custom WGSL material/post-process on the composite camera, for far better quality at <100% scale. FSR1 is MIT/public-domain shader code with no temporal/motion-vector dependency, so it runs cross-platform (Metal/Vulkan/DX/web) — ideal for the macOS + Steam Deck (AMD) targets where Bevy's DLSS can't run. Register the material like FfxiZoneMaterial (embedded:// wgsl, MaterialPlugin).

title: Render scale (low-res 3D target + upscale pass), exposed in graphics GUI + /renderscale
description:
Add a user-controllable render scale: render the 3D world to a lower-resolution RenderTarget::Image, then upscale-composite to the window while keeping HUD + minimap at native resolution. Bilinear first, FSR1 (EASU+RCAS) WGSL pass as a follow-up. Cross-platform (Metal/Vulkan/DX/web); the only AI upscaler in Bevy 0.18 (DLSS) is RTX+Vulkan-only so it does not help the macOS dev box or the AMD Steam Deck target.

Evidence / wiring points:
- Graphics GUI is the launcher pre-login screen: ffxi-client/src/view_native/launcher_ui/graphics.rs (spawn_field_row), driven by GraphicsSettings Resource + GraphicsField enum + GRAPHICS_FIELDS in ffxi-viewer-core/src/graphics_settings.rs (TAA = AaMode::Taa is the template option, applied by apply_anti_aliasing_system ~L934 which respawns build_operator_camera).
- Persistence: ffxi-client/src/graphics_store.rs (JSON at ~/.config/ffxi-mcp/graphics.json; auto via Serialize on GraphicsSettings).
- Camera: ffxi-viewer-core/src/camera.rs build_operator_camera (RenderLayers 0 + gizmo). HUD is Bevy UI on layer 0 of this same camera (ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/mod.rs) => need a separate native-res composite/UI camera; 3D camera renders to the low-res image.
- Existing render-to-texture precedent: minimap topdown bake (ffxi-viewer-core/src/minimap/topdown.rs:170, RenderTarget::Image on layer 3) — reuse the image-creation pattern.
- Slash command: add /renderscale to COMMANDS + SlashOutcome in ffxi-client/src/view_native/slash_commands.rs (mirror parse_minimap/parse_devhud) and dispatch in view_native/mod.rs.
- Upscale shader: new embedded WGSL material alongside ffxi-viewer-core/src/zone_ffxi.wgsl etc., registered in lib.rs.

Gotchas: picking (ffxi-viewer-core/src/picking.rs) and nameplate billboards (ffxi-viewer-core/src/nameplate_billboard.rs) project via the 3D camera; with a low-res target their viewport coords are in low-res space and must be scaled by render_scale to stay aligned with the native-res HUD. Minimap is already render-layer-isolated (no change). Also establish/enforce the convention that NEW graphics options must be surfaced in the launcher graphics GUI.
notes: Committed 73b0990 (feat(viewer): user-controllable render scale — low-res 3D target + bilinear upscale). Staged surgically: only render-scale hunks, inherited WIP in lib.rs/picking.rs/text_input.rs left unstaged. compile + 363 viewer-core tests + fmt/clippy green. STILL pending: user runtime verification (visual upscale + click-to-target alignment at 75%/50%). FSR1 EASU+RCAS WGSL upscale pass tracked as a separate follow-up bead.
close_reason: Base render scale shipped (#66 closed). FSR1 upscale remains in kuluu-dk5.

title: Clouds M4: enable in Vanilla, retire procedural FBM, Enhanced polish
description: CL2 done: procedural FBM dome retired — SkyCloudLayer removed from skybox.rs, vnoise/fbm stripped from skybox.wgsl; mesh clouds (zone_clouds.rs) render under Vanilla (sky_style==Vanilla) and are hidden under Enhanced. Remaining cloud polish (weather-switch fade, wind drift, dusk tint) tracked separately in kuluu-1vo (M3).
close_reason: Closed

title: Clouds M3: weather-driven swap + drift + time-of-day tint
description: PARTIAL (CL2): weather-driven SWAP is implemented — zone_clouds.rs::rebuild_zone_clouds keys spawned cloud entities on (zone_id, weather_type_id(current_weather)) and rebuilds the weat/<type>/ mesh set on weather change. STILL TODO: cross-fade on weather switch (~3.33s), UV/position wind drift (0x05 sec3 updaters 0x27/0x28/0x02), and time-of-day cloud tint from the sky gradient (ClockValueUpdater 0x3C-0x3F).
close_reason: All 3 gaps implemented: cross-fade (WEATHER_FADE_SECS, CloudFade alpha), UV drift (0x27/0x28 uv_scroll), time-of-day tint (ClockValueUpdater sampling). Systems registered in ZoneCloudsPlugin. Unit tests present.

title: Vanilla moon should use retail 12-frame sprite sheet, not procedural shader
description:
Vanilla sky draws the moon with a procedural shader (ffxi-viewer-core/src/moon.wgsl: hash noise + 3 gaussian maria + an in-shader ellipse terminator for phase; driven by MoonMaterial params in moon_material.rs). Retail FFXI instead draws the moon as a 12-frame sprite sheet, one baked image per phase, indexed by phase (confirmed via research/xim: EnvironmentManager.MoonPhase 0..11; MoonPhaseSpriteSheetUpdater sets particle.spriteSheetIndex; SpriteSheetMeshProvider returns spriteSheet.meshes[index]). Sprite sheet lives in environment DAT Section 0x21 (SpriteSheetMesh) referenced from Section 0x2F (EnvironmentSection), textures in Section 0x20.

Current infra (per audit): ffxi-dat texture.rs decodes DXT1/DXT3/palettized/0xA1/raw; zone_texture::decoded_texture_to_image bridges to bevy Image. ffxi-dat/src/weather.rs parses ONLY the 176-byte lighting/color header of 0x2F (skybox_colors/altitudes/fog/light), NOT the sprite-sheet refs. Section 0x21 is NOT in kind.rs and not parsed. moon_material.rs currently loads a single static texture by hardcoded file ids [55660,55780,56200,55540] via map_image::scan_graphics — a stopgap, not the phase sprite sheet.

Work: add ChunkKind 0x21 + sprite_sheet.rs parser (frame count, per-frame texture/UV), walk 0x2F->0x21->0x20 to locate the moon sprite sheet, expose 12 frames to the renderer, drive frame by VanaSky.moon phase, and have Vanilla moon sample the chosen frame instead of the procedural path. ~500-800 LOC + reverse-engineering the 0x21 binary layout. Re-express XIM logic in our own code; do not copy.
notes: Implemented in 47cce4b. ffxi-dat: ChunkKind::SpriteSheet(0x21) + sprite_sheet.rs::extract_moon_sprite_sheet (12-frame moonshap sheet -> UV rects + matched 256x256 texture via scan_graphics). Format validated against real DATs (dat-spritesheet-probe example) and research/xim. ffxi-viewer-core: MoonSpriteFrames resource + zone-driven loader in moon_material.rs (FFXI half-scale alpha expanded), moon.wgsl samples the phase frame's sub-rect, sun_moon_system selects frame via moon_phase_frame() (unit-tested 0=new/6=full). Procedural disc remains indoor fallback. PENDING: visual verification on running client — confirm (a) correct phase frame shows, (b) crescent waxing/waning direction (u-axis) not mirrored, (c) opacity looks right. Quick tweaks if off: flip u or v in moon.wgsl sprite branch; adjust alpha_remap.
close_reason: 0x21 chunk SpriteSheet parsed with 12 frames; moon_phase_frame() maps phase to retail index; shader branches on frame vs procedural

title: Zone-line marker display modes (Off/Pillar/Gate) + /zoneline + config GUI
description: Zone-line transition markers currently render as an always-on glowing orange cylinder (ffxi-viewer-core/src/zone_lines.rs), which is unfaithful (retail zone lines are invisible) and doesn't match the real trigger shape (oriented scale_x*scale_z rectangle from LSB zonelines.sql, detected in reactor.rs:797). Add a ZoneLineDisplay mode resource defaulting to Off (retail/vanilla-faithful). Modes: Off (invisible), Pillar (current cylinder, debug), Gate (oriented translucent box matching scale_x*scale_z + rotation). Expose via /zoneline slash command (mirror /navmesh, /zonegeom, /minimap mode patterns) and a config GUI row in GraphicsSettings. Viewer-only change; does not touch reactor.rs LSB-boundary detection.
close_reason: Implemented ZoneLineDisplay (Off default/Pillar/Gate) in GraphicsSettings; /zoneline slash command; auto-rendered Graphics-menu row; mode-aware sync_zone_lines_system with Gate drawing the real oriented scale_x*scale_z+rotation footprint; lifecycle reset on InGame re-entry. Tests + clippy + fmt green.

title: Zone point lights: extend reach + day/night gating (on at dusk, off at dawn)
description: Lanterns/braziers currently light only very close and stay lit 24h. Widen reach (ZONE_LIGHT_REACH_SCALE) and gate all lamp feeds on Vana'diel sun altitude so they ramp on through dusk and off through dawn — client-side, not the Events VM.
close_reason: Implemented: dusk/dawn gate (lamp_night_factor on sun altitude) across all three lamp feeds + reach scale 1.6→2.4. Committed in e6e390f (swept into sibling commit by a cross-session git race; content intact).

title: Plugin / extension API (the umbrella that lets the rest of this section exist...
description:
Plugin / extension API (the umbrella that lets the rest of this section exist as community contributions) --- The vanilla menu / target-interaction gaps that remain (main-menu "Commands" ordering, Magic/Abilities list views, region map, recast/buff timers, macro editor, door zone-transition flow) are specced and staged in [`vanilla-menu-parity-plan.md`](vanilla-menu-parity-plan.md).

Grounded 2026-06-14: verified [ ] (claimed [ ]). No community extension/addon/plugin API exists. There is no scripting host (mlua/rhai/wasmtime), no dynamic library loader (libloading/dlopen), no extension/hook registry, and no plugin-loading infrastructure for third-party code anywhere in the workspace; the only "plugin" hits are Bevy's internal Plugin trait, which is the engine framework concept, not a community-extension umbrella. The claimed [ ] (missing) is accurate.
Evidence:
- docs/ROADMAP.md:198 — Roadmap line itself: '[ ] Plugin / extension API (the umbrella that lets the rest of this section exist as community contributions)'
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/lib.rs — grep for 'plugin' across crates returns only Bevy framework Plugin impls (e.g. viewer-core lib), unrelated to a community extension API
- Cargo.toml — Workspace/crate Cargo.tomls have no scripting/loader deps (libloading, mlua, rlua, rhai, wasmtime, wasmer) — grep returned empty
- docs/ROADMAP.md:194 — Sibling enhanced/addon lines (DPS meter, skillchain visualizer, gear-swap macros) are also [ ], confirming the umbrella API has no implementing code

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (enhanced parity, launcher-lobby).

title: Merits, job points, capacity points (not in wire/session state)
description:
Merits, job points, capacity points (not in wire/session state)

Grounded 2026-06-14: verified [ ] (claimed [ ]). No merit, job-point, or capacity-point data is decoded in ffxi-proto or held in session/reactor/map_client state — the only matches are unrelated (zlib `capacity`, `Vec::with_capacity`, a test name). The sole code signal is placeholder Status-menu labels: "Merit Points"/"Master Levels" are `enabled: false` with a "not available" handler, and "Job Points" routes to a "not yet decoded" branch — confirming the roadmap's "not in wire/session state" claim. The HUD placeholder labels are covered by the separate `[~]` "Status menu" line, not this one.
Evidence:
- ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/status_panel.rs:82 — MeritPoints and MasterLevels StatusEntry are enabled:false placeholders; JobPoints enabled:true but with no backing data
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:1894 — Handler: MasterLevels|MeritPoints emit '— not available'; JobPoints lumped into '— not yet decoded' branch (line 1908)
- ffxi-proto/src/decode.rs:299 — char_update / GP_SERV_CHAR_PC decode exists but no merit/jobpoint/capacity fields; grep across ffxi-proto finds zero merit/jobpoint/capacity-point decode
- ffxi-client/src/session.rs:2684 — No merit/job-point/capacity-point field in SessionState; only unrelated 'capacity' hits (Vec::with_capacity)

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (vanilla parity, character-progression).
notes: Video requirements source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=837 (13:57-20:10). Merit menu detail: Mode Switch (EXP vs Limit Points accumulation) + categories; HP/MP merits (10 each, 15 tiers); Maximum Merit Points upgrade (30 base → 75 cap, costs merits); attributes to 15; combat/magic skills 8 tiers of +2 (explains 18s on off-skills); Others (enmity +/-, crit rate, spell interruption down) capped at 10 total; per-job 2 categories x 10 points; Weapon Skill merits (Aeonic WS, unlocked by quest at 99, 5 tiers each). Job Points at t=1151 (19:11): 20-tier categories, increasing cost, capped = master stars. Job Gifts at t=1187 (19:47): right-tab from Job Points, passive bonuses at point thresholds (100/1200 notable).

title: Skillchain visualizer
description:
Skillchain visualizer

Grounded 2026-06-14: verified [ ] (claimed [ ]). No implementing code exists for a Skillchain visualizer. The only "Skillchain" string in the main tree is an auto-generated status-effect name in a build artifact (status_names_table.rs), and there is no HUD module, panel, system, feature flag, or session/reactor state referencing skillchains, resonance, or chain tracking. The claimed [ ] (missing) glyph is correct.
Evidence:
- docs/ROADMAP.md:195 — The line itself, '- [ ] Skillchain visualizer', sits in the enhanced/addon section gated behind a feature flag; no other roadmap entry claims it as built.
- ffxi-client/src/hud/mod.rs — HUD module list (action_model, quick_action, target_action_menu, status_panel, etc.) contains no skillchain/visualizer panel; grep for Visualizer/Skillchain across ffxi-client/src returned nothing.

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (vanilla parity, party-social).
notes: 2026-07-19 ANIMATION FIX (user report: emotes not animating). Root causes: (1) commit 0b70928 accidentally deleted the whole dispatch_entity_emoted block from scheduler_runtime.rs inside an unrelated launcher-UI commit (restored); (2) scheduler stage timing was inclusive of the stage's own delay — XIM runEffects semantics are exclusive (delay gates LATER stages), so every routine stage fired late by its own delay (emote bow: 5s late; also shifts spell/WS/sound stages one step earlier = more faithful); (3) the id/8+em0(id%8) mapping HYPOTHESIS was falsified by the DAT dump (examples/zz-emote-probe.rs): Point/Bow swapped in file 0, Salute em02-04 by nation Param, ids 6..37 at (id+2)/8, HELM files pinned by Japanese tool particle names (ono=axe, turu=pickaxe, kama=sickle — agrees with XIM 5/6/7), Toss=(11), Dance1-4=(12,em00-03). emote_routine(id,param) now encodes the observed table with DAT-gated tests (real bow chain: Motion@frame0, bow? clip present). REMAINING: hurray xe0-6 weapon-variant selection, bell rx/rs note-variant, aim ye0-6 ranged-variant (default em00); Shocked(38) has no body routine found; visual GUI confirmation still pending (screen/VM contention).

Evidence:
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:2623 — Test `unwired_root_entries_stay_not_implemented` asserts the Root-menu "Search" entry resolves to MenuDispatch::NotImplemented — explicitly a stub.
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/text_input.rs:1858 — resolve_menu_entry catch-all `(_, other) => NotImplemented(other.to_string())` is what "Search" falls through to; no real handler.
- ffxi-client/src/view_native/slash_commands.rs:461 — Slash command registry (chat /s,/p,/sh,/l etc.) contains no `/search` entry; grep for "search" in this file returns nothing functional.
- ffxi-proto/data/autotranslate.tsv:450 — Only `/search` reference is an autotranslate token-name row, not a packet decoder or command dispatch.

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (vanilla parity, party-social).

Retail behavior: two palettes on hold-L2/hold-R2 (Ctrl/Alt rows); d-pad navigates while held; page switch only while trigger held (hold R2 + up/down) so pages can't be bumped accidentally; 10 macros per bar; 40 books x 10 pages x 20 macros (10 per side); 6 lines per macro; book/page names editable. Palette size + position (dynamic vs static) are Config settings (t=2804, tracked under kuluu-uos3.4).

title: Unify screenshot filename counter: /screenshot slash path should use screenshot.rs::next_default_path
description: kuluu-yo5 added Action::Screenshot using screenshot.rs::next_default_path() as the single owner of the screenshot-N.png counter; text_input.rs (locked by a concurrent session during that work) still holds its own local counter for the /screenshot slash path — a slash-shot and a keybind-shot can collide on filename. 3-line fold-in once text_input.rs is free.

title: EVENTUCOFF modes Standard(0)/CancelInput(3) unhandled; scrape eventucoff+msg_std enums; Examine name from packet payload
description: Follow-ups from kuluu-76z/kon conformance review: (1) s2c 0x052 modes Standard=0 (release() with no active event) and CancelInput=3 (matters once the event VM grows numeric/string input frames) are decoded but inert. (2) eventucoff_mode consts and MSG_STD_EXAMINE=89 are hand-cited copies; scrape GP_SERV_COMMAND_EVENTUCOFF_MODE (0x052_eventucoff.h) and msg_std.h at build time per the vendor-scrape skill. (3) Examine chat line resolves checker name from local entity cache; LSB puts the authoritative name in the 0x009 Data payload (0x009_message.cpp:83-88) — prefer the payload to cover uncached checkers.

title: Mog House gardening: flowerpot interaction + plant packets (0x0FC sow/feed, 0x0FD examine, 0x0FE harvest, 0x0FF stop)
description:
Retail: flowerpot furniture placed in the Mog House supports the full gardening loop — sow a seed, feed crystals, examine, harvest/uproot, or dry out the plant. Each step is its own c2s packet; results echo back via the s2c 0x0FA MYROOM_OPERATION state update.

LSB (authoritative):
- 0x0FC GP_CLI_COMMAND_MYROOM_PLANT_ADD — sow seed / feed crystal {MyroomPlantItemNo, MyroomAddItemNo, two indexes, two categories} (vendor/server/src/map/packets/c2s/0x0fc_myroom_plant_add.cpp:52; seed-vs-crystal branch :79-109).
- 0x0FD GP_CLI_COMMAND_MYROOM_PLANT_CHECK — examine plant; replies with status battle-messages (0x0fd_myroom_plant_check.cpp:45, :56-81).
- 0x0FE GP_CLI_COMMAND_MYROOM_PLANT_CROP — harvest/uproot, CancellFlg=0 means harvest (0x0fe_myroom_plant_crop.cpp:49; harvest math :70-95).
- 0x0FF GP_CLI_COMMAND_MYROOM_PLANT_STOP — dry out plant (0x0ff_myroom_plant_stop.cpp:48).
- Each handler pushes s2c 0x0FA GP_SERV_COMMAND_MYROOM_OPERATION back to the client (e.g. 0x0fc_...:119, 0x0fe_...:97).
- Packet-ID registry: src/map/enums/packet_c2s.h:131-137.

Current state (grounded 2026-07-09): none of the plant packets exist in ffxi-proto/ffxi-client and there is no flowerpot interaction anywhere. Prerequisites: MH interior rendering (kuluu-pnfc) and placed-furniture support (kuluu-6a0, which covers the 0x0FA/0x0FB furniture layout packets).

title: Communication menu: friends list + emote list browser

Retail Communication menu: Emote List (browse/trigger all emotes) and Friends List (online status, messages, set your own online status). https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=2117 (35:17-35:42).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05): no Communication menu (not in ROOT_ENTRIES, menu.rs:7-21); no emote browser (grep emote clean in viewer-core/client/wire — emote *animations* are kuluu-d4u); no friends UI or data (grep friend clean). Verify LSB friend-list support before scoping the friends half; the emote-list half is client-local and cheap once emote commands exist.
notes: 2026-07-18 emote-list HALF DONE: Communication -> Emote List menu (scraped 'Name (/command)' rows, HELM ids excluded, Job row gated on 0x11A bit for current main job, 0x119 refreshed on submenu open, row select dispatches AgentCommand::Emote). Friends-list half untouched (verify LSB friend-list support before scoping). Retail unknown: Communication menu placement/row wording.

Retail Status > Unity: Unity Info (your unity, rank, partial rank results, personal evaluation, unity-chat on/off), Ranking Info (leader standings + point totals), Unity Lists (browse players by unity). https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=732 (12:12-13:30).

Check LSB unity support level before scoping; the Status-menu Unity entry stub is tracked under kuluu-uvl.

title: Current Time menu entry: toggleable clock widget + Earth time readout
description:
Retail main menu "Current Time" toggles the lower-left clock widget and reports both Vana'diel and Earth time in chat. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rL2lL_dnIBQ&t=2102 (35:02-35:26).

Current state (grounded 2026-07-05):
- Clock widget exists but is always-on and Vana'diel-only: ffxi-viewer-core/src/hud/vana_clock.rs (update_vana_clock :120-158; label "{vana_time} {cell}" :138 — no Earth-time readout, no toggle, no menu entry).
notes: 2026-07-18 IMPLEMENTED + verified (protocol/unit level; GUI leg needs human eyes — host screen was locked). Current Time root entry between Config and Debug toggles VanaClockVisible (Display flip on VanaClockPanel, session-only, survives zone change; Added<VanaClockPanel> covers respawn) + prints Vana'diel line (weekday, H:MM, d/m/y C.E.) and Earth line (local via chrono). /clock show|hide|toggle aliases the same resource. Epoch consolidated: ffxi_proto::vana_time::VANA_EPOCH_UNIX (cites earth_time.h:40) — vana_clock.rs re-exports, session.rs imports. 12 new tests incl. LSB calendar boundary pins (3456s day, 8-day week, day-30/day-360 rollovers). RETAIL UNKNOWNS (blocked: VM owned by another session): exact chat wording/format + timezone, menu label/position, close-on-activate, persistence — all provisional behind exported consts (ROOT_CURRENT_TIME, VANA_TIME_LINE_PREFIX, EARTH_TIME_LINE_PREFIX, EARTH_TIME_FORMAT); one retail capture corrects them in one place.
close_reason: Implemented + unit/protocol verified; provisional wording pinned behind consts pending a retail capture (tracked in the bead notes).
